Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Order of generated code is partially swapped for chain of three or more inlined atomic subsystems with reusable child subsystems
Description: Order of generated code is partially swapped when all of these conditions are true: The model configuration parameter?Simulink block comments ?is selected. At least three atomic subsystems are connected in a chain with or without intermediate blocks. The first two atomic subsystems have the Function packaging  block parameter set to Auto  or Inline . All three atomic subsystems have a child atomic subsystem with reusable? the Function packaging  block parameter set to Reusable function . Data input to the reusable child atomic subsystem in the second and third parent atomic subsystems: Comes from a signal line with a non-auto storage class, and The non-auto storage class is specified outside the reusable child atomic subsystem, for example in the parent atomic subsystem.  In the final generated code, the function calls corresponding to the three child atomic subsystems do not contain code statements between them.  This swap in the order of generated code can lead to incorrect results, such as the second reusable function call occurring before the first reusable function call.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized variables in the generated code with reusable functions and Bus Selector blocks
Description: ?Generated code for a model might have uninitialized variables when all of the following conditions apply: The model contains an atomic subsystem that has the Function packaging ?parameter?set to?Reusable function . A Bus Selector block with a nonvirtual bus input connects directly, or through virtual blocks, to the input or output port of this subsystem.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block at the input port and all output ports of the Bus Selector block.

Summary: Assertion error when updating model containing coding pattern that indexes into a matrix using unsigned integers
Description: Simulink might display an assertion error when updating a model containing a MATLAB Function Block, MATLAB System Block, or MATLAB function in Stateflow that?indexes into a matrix using unsigned integers. The assertion error contains the following text? failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\expr\indexexpranalysis\indexinfo.cpp ?at line 147:?  ConstTools::fitsInTypePrecisely(fxpDV, typeForConst)  For an example see issue_model_1927585  in the attached issue_example_1927585.zip . Note in this model that idxVec  is a vector of uint32.
Workaround: To workaround the issue instead index into the matrix using signed integers. See workaround_model_1927585 that avoids the error. In this model idxVec is a vector of int32.

Summary: Cloning repository from GitHub fails with "cannot open git-upload-pack" error
Description: When you attempt to clone or pull a GitHub repository over HTTPS, the operation fails with the error message: Cannot open git-upload-pack .
Workaround: You can use the same workaround as this bug report:?https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/1741173

Summary: Saturate on overflow diagnostic is ignored in Stateflow
Description: The configuration parameter Diagnostics > Data Validity > Saturate on overflow? is ignored by Stateflow. The error or warning is not thrown to the Diagnostic Viewer when the condition is triggered.

Summary: Serial I/O blocks can cause the target computer to crash sporadically.
Description: Real-time applications that contain any of the serial I/O blocks can cause the target computer to crash sporadically. To determine whether this issue occurred, inspect the crash info to check whether the crash occurred in fiforead.c . If so, apply the workaround.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change Log:? ?? attachment_1845756_17a_2018_08_28.zip: initial version

Summary: PDF image mapping fails to map small areas and implement area styles
Description: The DOM API incorrectly positions ImageArea  objects whose height is less than 2 points.The DOM API also ignores the Style  property of ImageArea  objects. This make it impossible to draw borders around image hot spots.
Workaround: To enable approximately correct positioning of image areas, wrap the image to be mapped in a paragraph with a font size less than 2pt, for example,import mlreportgen.dom.*import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('imgmap','pdf');img = Image(which('b747.jpg'));map = ImageMap;append(map,ImageArea('https://google.com','',0,0,10,2));img.Map = map;p = Paragraph;p.FontSize = '1.5pt';append(p,img);append(d,p);close(d);rptview(d);

Summary: Unexpected license error can occur if local workers in a Parallel Computing Toolbox pool are idle for longer than FlexLM license timeout period
Description: When using a parallel pool, a worker can be idle, that is, notexecuting any MATLAB code. For example, a worker can be idle when the client is not executing anyparallel language constructs such as parfor, parfeval, or spmd, or when not allworkers are busy executing parallel constructs, such as the last few iterationsof a parfor loop.  If a worker is idle for longer than the FlexLM license timeoutperiod, then the license is returned. When the worker resumes executingMATLAB code, it does not correctly reacquire the license. This condition leads to thefollowing license error: ? Summary: Assertion error when calling a MATLAB or Simulink function with a vector output
Description: Stateflow throws an assertion error when you call a MATLAB or Simulink function in a Stateflow chart if all of the following conditions are true:? The chart uses C as the action language The function has a vector output The function has a boolean input argument The value passed in to the boolean input is a numeric literal or a variable of non-boolean type  The error message is similar to: failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_c\lowermatrixtypesforbackend.cpp at line 801: PointerType::isa(aCastExpr->type())??
Workaround: Pass in true or false instead of numeric literals for the boolean input argument, or cast the input to boolean type before passing to the function.

Summary: Test harness compilation and simulation generates warning: "Matching "Goto" for "From" <block> not found"
Description: The test harness rebuild operation deletes Goto and From blocks from the Input Conversion Subsystem and Output Conversion Subsystem, which produces this warning message during compilation and simulation: Matching "Goto" for "From" <block> not found

Summary: Incorrect results are possible for cascading Unit Delay blocks inside Switch Case Action Subsystem block
Description: The state for a Unit Delay block may incorrectly be a local variable in the generated code for a model that meets all of these conditions:  The Unit Delay blocks are inside a Switch Case Action Subsystem block. The output of one Unit Delay block feeds into another Unit Delay block. The Unit Delay block outputs are vectors or a matrices. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Enable local block outputs  parameter is selected.
Workaround: Unzip the attachment and save the workaroundForBugReport1796701.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.?
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 5, R2018b, R2019a

Summary: Expected output values in report differ from those produced by harness simulation
Description: After Simulink Design Verifier analysis, the expected output values included in the report and sldvdata differ from the output values produced by simulating the harness model. This occurs when the model satisfies both the following conditions:  1. The parameter Randomize data that do not affect the outcome is set to 'On'. 2. The model contains an input signal whose value does not influence the satisfaction of a test or proof objective.
Workaround: Use sldvruntest API to calculate the model output.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer when casting a negative literal integer larger than that of target integer size
Description: According to C promotion rules, a negative literal integer is cast to a given data type before wrapping (or saturating) it for the target integer type. However Stateflow incorrectly wraps the negative literal integer to the target data type before casting it to the given data type.  For example, consider the expression:?  ?myData = int32(-50000)  If the target integer size is 16-bit?and ?myData ?has type? int32 , then this expression should evaluate to -50000 because it fits in the range for  int32 : -2147483647 < -50000 < 2147483647.?However, the expression evaluates incorrectly to 15536 because it is first wrapped in the 16-bit target integer type range, that is, 15536 = 32768 - (50000 % 32768).?
Workaround: When casting negative literals to a data type larger than that of target integer size, either:Use negative floating point value instead of negative integer. For example, on a 16-bit target, use int32(-50000.0) instead of int32(-50000).?Place the negation operator outside of the cast expression. For example, on a 16-bit target, use -int32(50000) instead of int32(-50000).?

Summary: Display of the psia and psig units is incorrect in the Simulink Editor
Description: Simulink displays Pa  instead of the correct unit in the Simulink Editor when these conditions are true: The model Display  > Signals & Ports  > Port Units  option is enabled. The Unit  parameter contains psia  or psig .

Summary: Incorrect size of structures with Tasking compiler and Tricore target
Description: The Tasking compiler uses a specific packing for structures: halfword (2 bytes) packing unless the structure uses doubles, circular buffers, __laccum, or pointers.  Polyspace does not emulate this packing correctly. For example: struct {char c1, c2, c3; } => Tasking: 4 bytes; Polyspace: 3 bytes struct {char c1, c2, c3, c4, c5;}  => Tasking: 6 bytes; Polyspace: 5 bytes This incorrect size computation can lead to wrong checks, especially out of bounds array index and illegally dereferenced pointer.?

Summary: Drive Cycle Source block does not retain the Drive cycle source setting after you save and reopen the model
Description: In the Drive Cycle Source block, after you Install additional drive cycles ?from the support package, the block does not retain the?Drive cycle source ?setting. Instead, the block incorrectly reverts to the default FTP75  drive cycle after you save and reopen the model.?
Workaround: After you install the Powertrain Blockset Drive Cycle Data support package, start MATLAB as an administrator and execute these commands:libDir = which('autolibshared.slx');fileattrib(libDir,'+w'); open_system('autolibshared')save_system('autolibshared')close_system('autolibshared')fileattrib(libDir,'-w');clear libDirThere are no release restrictions or limitations associated with the workaround.

Summary: Assertion error when generating code for model containing Stateflow C action language chart containing subscript expression composed of?Stateflow?data?and custom code symbol
Description: Simulink might display an assertion error when you generate code for a model containing a Stateflow chart that meets all of the following conditions: The Stateflow chart uses C as the action language. The chart contains an assignment statement involving indexing The subscript expression of the indexing contains both Stateflow data and a custom code symbol   ?The assertion error contains the following text: CGIR assertion 'MatrixType::isa(type) || ComplexType::isa(type)' failed in 'b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\expr\indexexpranalysis\indexinfo.cpp:1138  For an example of the issue see the model 'issue_example_1786093.slx' in the attached 'issue_1786093.zip'. Within the Stateflow chart issue_example_1786093/Chart the assignment statement out = input[stateflowData - CUSTOM_CODE_SYMBOL];  contains an indexing subscript 'stateflowData - CUSTOM_CODE_SYMBOL' composed of Stateflow data and a custom code symbol.?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, cast the custom code symbol to a Simulink type. For example, the description section references an example with the statementout = input[stateflowData - CUSTOM_CODE_SYMBOL]; To apply the workaround a cast is added to CUSTOM_CODE_SYMBOL?out = input[stateflowData - cast(CUSTOM_CODE_SYMBOL,type(stateflowData))];?For an example please see the model 'workaround_example_1786093.slx' in the attached 'issue_1786093.zip'.

Summary: Folder access error during PDF report generation
Description: PDF report generation randomly fails with a folder access error. This generally occurs when another process, such as Microsoft OneDrive or a virus scanner, attempts to access the intermediate files that are used to generate a PDF report. The error message is similar to:? Error closing document package: Could not commit changes: Could not rename the temporary package to final location: Permission denied

Summary: Report.getReportLayout returns wrong layout
Description: The getReportLayout  method of an mlreportgen.report.Report  object returns a report layout object. The returned object is the correct report layout only if a reporter invokes?the method. Otherwise, the method returns a layout object that is not the current report layout. For example, in the following script, the getReportLayout  method should return the layout of the current chapter. However, it returns the layout that was in effect before the chapter was added to the report.    import mlreportgen.report.*  rpt = Report('myreport',?'docx');  add(rpt, Chapter('Title', 'Introduction'));  plo = getReportLayout(rpt);
Workaround: Use the following function to get the current report layout.function?layout = getLayout(rpt)    c = rpt.Document.Children;    layouts = c(arrayfun(@(child) isa(child, 'mlreportgen.dom.PageLayout'), c));    if ~isempty(layouts)        layout = layouts(end);    else        layout = rpt.Document.CurrentPageLayout;    endend

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Interpolation Using Prelookup block inlines symbolic dimensions for table data when specified via the Table data port
Description: The Interpolation Using Prelookup block inlines the symbolic dimensions for table data when the table data is specified via the Table data port (Source  set to port ), rather than with the block parameter (Source  set to Dialog ).
Workaround: To specify symbolic dimensions for table data:In the MATLAB workspace, define a Simulink.Parameter object that contains your symbolic dimensions.In the Interpolation using Prelookup block,?set the Source?parameter to Dialog and specify the Simulink.Parameter object in the Value parameter.?For more information, see?https://www.mathworks.com/help/ecoder/ug/implement-dimension-variants-for-array-sizes-in-generated-code.html.

Summary: Test case extension may cause the analysis to time-out before the Maximum analysis time
Description: Extension of test cases causes the test case?loading time to be added to the analysis time.This may cause Simulink Design Verifier to time-out before the specified Maximum analysis time.   For example: If the test case loading takes 10 mins and the?Maximum analysis time ?is specified as 5 mins then the?Simulink Design Verifier?would always time-out without performing any analysis.

Summary: Update does not modify "Modified on" of a requirement when only its custom attribute value is changed, thus failing to trigger the change information
Description: If you update a requirement set that uses custom attributes, and only the custom attribute value of a requirement is changed since the previous update, the update process does not change the "Modified On" timestamp for the corresponding requirement. As a result, the change information is not automatically triggered for the updated requirement. The requirement is updated, and the summary of the changes (in Comments) includes those updates.
Workaround: One possible workaround that involves some manual work is as follows: Look at the summary of the changes produced by the Update process.Note the IDs of the updated requirements.Go back to the external document, and locate the requirements with the above IDs.Manually modify at least one of the built-in attributes for those requirements, for instance by adding a period to the Summary or the Description attribute. Please note, whitespace-only differences are ignored by the Update process, so the modification must not include only whitespace.Rerun the Update process.The Update process will now re-update the requirements, and update the "Modified On" timestamp on those requirements, which will trigger the change information.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Silent wrong answer might occur due to sporadic data corruption on the target computer
Description: Sporadic data corruption on the target computer might result in intermittent incorrect results, without a warning or error. This data corruption occurs due to interrupt service activity on the target computer. Known conditions that increase the frequency of incorrect results include:? More periodic sample times in the application than cores on the target computer Leaving the SLRT Explorer open and connected to the target computer High levels of network traffic involving the target computer Running the model in external mode

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The bus elements of a variant virtual bus?are unconditionally generated in the code
Description: Variant bus signals with? GeneratePreprocessorConditionals? selected ?are unconditionally generated in the code when all of the below conditions are true: A subsystem containing a bus selector block has a? different variant condition than the incoming virtual bus. The option  Treat as grouped when propagating variant conditions ? or?Treat As Atomic Unit ? is selected for the subsystem.  Both the parent bus and the child signals get selected using the bus selector within the subsystem.  The child bus signals have an output port connection. The parent bus is left unconnected.

Summary: mlreportgen.reporter.Figure reporter always uses default font size when creating figure snapshot
Description: If you use the mlreportgen.reporter.Figure  reporter to create a snapshot of a figure, the figure's default font size is always used.  For example, if you create a figure and change the font size of its title, the figure snapshot taken by the mlreportgen.reporter.Figure  reporter will not reflect this change in font size.
Workaround: Use the PRINT function to print the figure as an image file. Then, create an?mlreportgen.dom.Image object with that image file as the input. Set the image height and width properties to fit the page, and add the image object to the report. After the report is closed, delete the image file.

Summary: Models containing Variant blocks may take long time to update.
Description: Models containing Variant blocks takes longer time to simulate/update as compared to a model without Variant blocks.This time difference is more profound in larger models with more number of blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Symbolic dimension specified for Output Size of Selector block is ignored in the generated code
Description: When generating code for a model containing a Selector block whose Output Size is specified using a symbolic dimension, Embedded Coder omits the symbolic specification and instead inlines the numeric value.?
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Test Sequence block inside a test harness is slow to open
Description: If you have a Test Sequence block with many steps inside a test harness with many blocks, the Test Sequence editor opens more slowly than expected.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may take longer time than the specified Maximum analysis time
Description: Simulink Design Verifier shows analysis in progress for long time even after the analysis time has exceeded the Maximum analysis time  specified for a model when any of the following is true: Simulink Design Verifier is launched from the UI menu  Using  sldvrun  API with  showUI  flag set to true   For such models, stop button may not respond for long time.
Workaround: Perform Simulink Design Verifier analysis by using sldvrun with showUI option set to false.

Summary: Some MATLAB tools might fail to open on Ubuntu 18.04
Description: These MATLAB and Simulink tools might fail to open on Ubuntu 18.04: App Designer Simulink Data Inspector Root Inport Mapping Simulink Template Gallery Export Model to Template Add-Ons Explorer Simulink Start Page  If a tool fails to open, follow these steps: Open MATLAB. Set the current folder to the  matlabroot .  Type? !./bin/glnxa64/MATLABWindow  in the Command Window.?   If MATLAB displays this error in the Command Window, then the issue is caused by a problem with the MATLABWindow application: MATLABWindow: symbol lookup error: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libatk-1.0.so.0: undefined symbol: g_log_structured_standard

Summary: Nested tables overflow the page
Description: Nested tables incorrectly inherit column width information from the parent table. For example, if the first column of the parent table has a width of 75%, then a nested table in that column inherits 75% width for all its columns. This causes the nested table to overflow.
Workaround: Nested tables must specify fixed table layout and specify column width.? For example:d = mlreportgen.dom.Document('myreport', 'pdf');nestedTable = mlreportgen.dom.Table({ ...    '1111111', '222222', '333333';    '1111111', '222222', '333333';    '1111111', '222222', '333333';    });% Specify column width (required)nestedTableCSG = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpecGroup();nestedTableCS1 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();nestedTableCS1.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('33%')};nestedTableCS2 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();nestedTableCS2.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('33%')};nestedTableCS3 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();nestedTableCS3.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('33%')};nestedTableCSG.ColSpecs = [nestedTableCS1, nestedTableCS2, nestedTableCS3];nestedTable.ColSpecGroups = nestedTableCSG;% Specify fixed table layout (required)nestedTable.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.ResizeToFitContents(false)};% Specify nested table width (required)nestedTable.Width = '100%';layoutTable = mlreportgen.dom.Table({...    'a', ...    'b', ...    nestedTable});layoutTable.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.ResizeToFitContents(false)};layoutTableCSG = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpecGroup();layoutTableCS1 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();layoutTableCS1.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('20%')};layoutTableCS2 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();layoutTableCS2.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('20%')};layoutTableCS3 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();layoutTableCS3.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('60%')};layoutTableCSG.ColSpecs = [layoutTableCS1, layoutTableCS1, layoutTableCS3];layoutTable.ColSpecGroups = layoutTableCSG;layoutTable.Width = '100%';append(d,layoutTable);close(d);

Summary: slreportgen.finder.DiagramElementResult getReporter returns empty reporter for?Simulink.Port?or?Simulink.Segment?object
Description: The?getReporter ? method of?slreportgen.finder.DiagramElementResult ?returns an empty reporter for a?Simulink.Port ?or? Simulink.Segment ?object.?It should return an?slreportgen.report.SimulinkObjectpProperties  reporter for each of these objects.
Workaround: Instead of calling the?getReporter method for Simulink.Port or Simulink.Segment objects, use slreportgen.report.SimulinkObjectProperties directly for those objects to get their properties.For Example?% get all the necessary headers?import slreportgen.report.*?import slreportgen.finder.*?import mlreportgen.report.*?model = "sf_car";?load_system(model)?rpt = slreportgen.report.Report('output','pdf');?chapter = Chapter();?chapter.Title = 'Diagram Element Reporter Example';?% Find Simulink.Port and Simulink.Segment objects from the model sf_car?elemFinder = DiagramElementFinder(model);?elemFinder.Types = ["Simulink.Port", "Simulink.Segment"];?elems = find(elemFinder);?for elem = elems?? ? section = Section("Title", ...?? ? ? ? strrep(elem.Type, newline,' '));?? ? ? ? % Calling SimulinkObjectProperties directly for Simulink.Port and Simulink.Segment objects to fetch the proerties? ? rptr = SimulinkObjectProperties(elem.Object);?? ? add(section,rptr);?? ? add(chapter,section);?end?add(rpt,chapter);?close(rpt);?rptview(rpt);?

Summary: Symbolic Math Toolbox hangs or issues an error after installing Windows 10 Spring Creator Update 2018
Description: After you install Windows 10 Spring Creator Update 2018, the symbolic engine does not respond to any input or reports this error: Internal error with symbolic engine. Quit and restart MATLAB.?  For example,?syms x ?does not resolve.

Summary: Stray processes after pop-up message:?Failed to connect to the license server. Polyspace will exit now.
Description: After being disconnected from the network, the following message pops up in the Polyspace user interface:   Failed to connect to the license server. Polyspace will exit now. After clicking OK , the Polyspace user interface closes. However, some processes associated with Polyspace, such as polyspace-code-prover.exe *32  or MATLAB.exe , continue to run in the Task Manager.
Workaround: Installation instructions using Polyspace R2016b  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.?  Exit MATLAB and Polyspace UI.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. On?Windows Systems,?you may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code is possible for scalar Data Store Memory block value that is read inside reusable subsystem
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: A Data Store Memory block?is read inside a reusable subsystem. The reusable subsystem has a single output. The Data Store Memory block is not written in the parent model of the subsystem. The Data Store Memory block variable is a scalar.  In the incorrect?code, the Data Store Memory block value may be incorrectly defined as a local variable which is not initialized.?
Workaround: Unzip the attached P-file,?workaroundForBugReport1755403.p.Insert the P-file on the MATLAB file path.For every new MATLAB session, in the Command Window, enter the following command:>> workaroundForBugReport1755403
Fixed In: R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 7, R2018a Update 2, R2018b

Summary: Figure caption styles for Simulink Report API reporters not applied correctly
Description: Changing the FormalImage reporter caption style also changes the figure caption styles for these Simulink Report API reporters:  Figure reporter Diagram reporter LookupTable reporter    For example, if you update the style of the FormalImage reporter caption, add that FormalImage reporter to the report, and then, add one of the above reporters, the caption style of that reporter is incorrectly changed to match the FormalImage reporter caption style.
Workaround: To specify the style of a FormalImage Caption, create a DOM Text object with the desired styles and assign it to the Caption of the FormalImage reporter. For example,?formalImage = mlreportgen.report.FormalImage(which('ngc6543a.jpg'));text = mlreportgen.dom.Text("Cat''s Eye Nebula or NGC 6543");?text.Color = "blue";?text.Italic = true;?formalImage.Caption = text;?

Summary: Simulation does not start when running a model in real time
Description: Real-time simulation of a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model might fail to start, freeze?at Time = 0, or report a timeout when trying to run the model in real time on specific CPU hardware configurations with Windows OS.

Summary: S-function with bus input stubbed unnecessarily during analysis
Description: If your model contains an S-function that has a bus input that itself contains an array of buses as one of its elements, the model might be incorrectly marked as partially compatible. Simulink Design Verifier will stub the S-Function block, which results in:?? Imprecise results for test-case generation or property proving in the model No test case generated for test objectives inside the S-function itself

Summary: Simulink model window opens without menus and toolbars
Description: The Simulink model window sometimes opens without menus and toolbars.
Workaround: To work around this issue, run sl_refresh_customizations at the MATLAB command line.

Summary: Every chapter in a Word report starts on page 1
Description: Every chapter in a Word report generated by the Report API starts on page 1. The correct default behavior is for only the first chapter to start on page 1. Succeeding chapters should continue numbering from the last page of the preceding chapter.?

Summary: Table title styles for Report API Simulink reporters not applied correctly
Description: If the?BaseTable ?reporter?Title ?style is changed, the table Title  styles for these Simulink Report API reporters are also updated: SimulinkObjectProperties  reporter  StateflowObjectProperties  reporter  LookupTable  reporter  MATLABFunction  reporter   For example, if you update the style of the?BaseTable   reporter?Title , add that?BaseTable   reporter to the report, and then, add one of the above Report API Simulink reporters, the?Title ?style of that reporter is incorrectly changed to match the BaseTable   reporter title style.
Workaround: To specify the style of a?BaseTable?Title, create a DOM Text?object with the desired styles and assign it to the?Title?of the BaseTable reporter. For example,?bt = mlreportgen.report.BaseTable(magic(5));text = mlreportgen.dom.Text("Title for Magic Square");?text.Color = "blue";?text.Italic = true;?bt.Title = text;?

Summary: Incomplete verify signal plots in Test Manager
Description: If you run a test case in Test Manager on a model that contains a Test Sequence block that uses verify , the ?test case result might show? incomplete? verify ?signal plots if there are a large number of points. ?
Workaround: If you see this issue on a?verify?signal in Test Manager, right-click and export the verify signal to a workspace variable and import it into Data Inspector. Data Inspector should be able to plot the entire verify signal. Note that you have to export the?verify?signal one at a time, e.g., export a single?verify?signal instead of the "Verify Statements" group.?

Summary: null is not an object error when?opening the Test Sequence editor
Description: If you have a model that contains a Test Sequence block, and the model start time is a variable (for example, START_TIME), opening the Test Sequence editor leads to a long error in the MATLAB console that begins with: Error using reactive>_setSimTimeAttr (line 315)TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'a.toExponential')  ...
Workaround: Set the model start time to a literal number (for example, 0.0).?

Summary: A block copy crashes MATLAB when the block has an output signal using a custom storage class
Description: When you copy a Simulink block, MATLAB crashes when both conditions are true: At least one block output is connected to a named signal line. The Signal Properties window was used to specify a custom storage class for the signal line.

The detailed error message indicates:? "Download Error: There was a problem downloading the third-party software. Summary: Margin, border, and padding formats corrupt PDF style sheet
Description: Specifying a border, margin, or padding format in a PDF style sheet corrupts the style sheet. The first use of a style containing one of these formats overrides their occurrence in any other style. For example, consider the following style sheet: p.GreenStyle {??? color: green;??? font-size: 12pt;??? font-style: italic;??? top-margin: 16pt;??? bottom-margin: 0pt;?}?  p {??? color:blue;??? font-size: 11pt;??? font-style: normal;??? top-margin: 0pt;??? bottom-margin: 10;}?  If a report?applies?GreenStyle  to?the first paragraph in a report, all subsequent paragraphs?have a 16pt  top margin and a 0pt  bottom?margin, including paragraphs that specify another style or?do not specify a style.?
Workaround: Specify CSS?border, margin (represented by the DOM OuterMargin format), and padding (InnerMargin) formats programmatically, for example,import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('myreport', 'pdf', 'mytemplate');pStyle = {OuterMargin('0pt', '0pt', '10pt', '0pt')};append(d, Paragraph('Green Style', 'GreenStyle'));para = append(d, Paragraph('Base Style'));para.Style = [para.Style pStyle];para = append(d, Paragraph('Base Style'));para.Style = [para.Style pStyle];close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);?

Summary: DOM API HTML and HTMLFile objects incorrectly convert?vertical-align?format
Description: The DOM API HTML  and HTMLFile  objects may incorrectly convert the? vertical-align:super ?or?vertical-align:sub ? format. These values can also corrupt a DOM-generated Word document.
Workaround:
Instead of using vertical-align:super or vertical-align:sub?format in?HTML and HTMLFile objects, use the HTML or tag, respectively. For example, instead of using

This text contains superscript text.

use

This text contains superscript text.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow absolute time temporal logic incorrectly evaluated for small integer types
Description: Stateflow transitions guarded by absolute time temporal logic might be evaluated as true early if all of the following conditions are true: The chart uses MATLAB as the action language. The first argument P  to the temporal logic operator is a Stateflow data of small integer type (for example, uint8). The time unit used in the absolute time temporal logic operator is greater than the sample time Ts  of the chart (for example, using after(P, msec)  with Ts  < 0.001).  Instead of staying P / Ts  number of chart wakeups in that state, the transition will be taken on the value of P / Ts  wrapped to the data type of P .
Workaround: In the temporal logic operator, cast the first argument?P?to uint32, for example:after(uint32(P),msec)?

Summary: Java error when using Document Index tab in Outgoing Link dialog with link to DNG
Description: When you try to create a requirements link with items in a DOORS Next Generation (DNG) project using the Outgoing Links dialog, if you switch to the Document Index tab and the selected DNG project does not have any Modules, a Java log window with a null pointer exception error appears.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect arguments passed across subsystem boundary for?atomic subsystem in referenced model
Description: When producing code for a referenced model that meets all of the following conditions, the code generator can omit pointer initialization from the generated step entry-point function for that model: Model configuration parameters Language  and Code interface packaging ?are?set to C++  and C++ class  , respectively.  Contains an atomic subsystem that connects to a root-level Inport or Outport block. For that atomic subsystem, block parameter Function packaging  is set to  Nonreusable function .  The missing pointer initialization causes the step function to not pass the?correct arguments across the subsystem boundary.?
Workaround: Set the subsystem block parameter Function packaging to Reusable function or Inline.

Summary: Error when updating a test harness following a clone or rename operation
Description: When you run or perform an Update Model on a cloned or renamed Simulink Test harness with Simulink Dashboard blocks, MATLAB might throw the following error:? Cannot change the 'StreamingClients' parameter while the model 'ClonedHarness' is running??

Summary: Cannot simulate termination event for Model block with periodic event ports in top model SIL mode
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it contains a Model block with the following parameter settings: Simulation mode:  Software-in-the-loop (SIL) Code interface:  Top model Show model initialize port:  on Show model periodic event ports:  on  The model produces an error similar to the one below when the Model block receives a function-call at its initialize port during simulation. Error reported by S-function 'ControllerJMAAB_sbs' in 'RTEModelJMAAB_NoEst/TmpSFcnForModelReference_SW Component1': pXILService is NULL!   Optionally, if the Model block also has the parameter Show model terminate port  set to 'on', the above error message?is thrown when the Model block receives a function-call at its terminate port during simulation.

Summary: Computer shuts down or does not respond when simulating a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model
Description: Real-time simulation of a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model might fail to start, report a timeout, or cause the computer to shutdown or restart?after updates are applied to the Windows operating system, or after the system has been upgraded to a newer release of Windows 10.
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017a Update 4, R2017b Update 7, R2018a Update 3, R2018b

Summary: Code generation?fails when top model contains protected model that?references a?model
Description: Suppose you have a?Model block that references a protected model, modelRefProtected . The protected?model?also references a child model, modelChild .?If you?insert the Model block into a model, modelTop ,?and then?try to generate code for the model, the code generation process produces this error:? ### Build procedure for model: 'modelTop' aborted due to an error.  Model 'modelChild' does not exist

Summary: Code Generation Advisor hangs when reviewingreferenced models.
Description: When the Code Generation Advisor reviewsmodels that contain referenced models, the process?stalls with an errormessage.

Summary: 'Indices' error when running a model with Selector and Assignment blocks if variable named int32 exists in base workspace
Description: Simulink throws an error when you run or update a model that contains Selector or Assignment blocks if a variable?named int32  exists in the base workspace. A sample error message is: Invalid parameter 'Indices' specified in 'untitled/Selector'?

Summary: Fast Block LMS Filter block errors for Reset port choice
Description: In Fast Block LMS Filter block, changing the? Reset port choices to "Rising edge", "Falling edge", or "Either edge" and then clicking OK or Apply button might result in an error: Output argument "strOut (and maybe others) not assigned during call to "dspblkfblms>transformResetString"

Summary: Orphaned section titles in Report API PDF report
Description: If a Section  title and its following content, such as a?SimulinkObjectProperties  reporter, cannot fit on the same page in PDF output, the section title can be orphaned. An orphaned section title appears at the end of a page and the section content appears on the following page.
Workaround: Create an mlreportgen.report.Report template and unzip it to customize. >> mlreportgen.report.Report.createTemplate('myreport.pdftx','pdf');>> unzipTemplate('myreport.pdftx');Edit myreport\stylesheets\root.css by updating the p.LinkTargetWrapper style to add keep-with-next format.      p.LinkTargetWrapper {??          line-height: 0pt;??          -fo-keep-with-next.within-page: always;      }Zip the custom template.      >> zipTemplate('myreport.pdftx','myreport');Use this custom template to generate the report.      >> slreportgen.report.Report('output','pdf','myreport.pdftx');

Summary: Unused variables might not be in the generated code even?though the Block reduction parameter is set to off
Description: Blocks and signals in an unused code path might not be in the generated code even though in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Block reduction  parameter is set to off.
Workaround: You can?try one of these?workarounds:You can designate the signal that you want to preserve in the generated code as a test point.?In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, you can clear the Enable local block output parameter.

Summary: generateAudioPlugin -win32 plugin generation not working with Microsoft Visual Studio 2017
Description: Attempting to generate a 32-bit plugin using?generateAudioPlugin -win32  with the Microsoft Visual Studio 2017 compiler fails with an error message similar to  NMAKE : fatal error U1052: file 'MyPluginClass_rtw.mk' not found

Summary: blastlocal fails with corrupted report error for some FASTA input files
Description: The blastall  command line tool, used by blastlocal , issues a warning when it encounters sequences containing Selenocysteine. These warnings result in errors when parsing the output of blastall. ?As a result,?blastlocal ?might error unexpectedly with the message "Report appears to be corrupted".

Summary: MATLAB Variables or Data Objects displayincorrect MATLAB expression for n-D data
Description: The string displayed in the Value field when exploringa MATLAB Variable or Data Object might have mismatched parentheses for n-D(n>2) data of non-double type. The underlying data for the object isunaffected, but evaluating the Value string shown on the UI in MATLAB wouldresult in an error.

Summary: Running Test Manager on model referencing multiple instances with coverage enabled throws error
Description: Running Test Manager on a model that references multiple instances of another model with coverage enabled might result in an error: "Error while initializing parameters"

Summary: Cannot link a range in?MATLAB code?to a selection in?the Requirements Editor, when a requirement link already exists for this location in the MATLAB file
Description: You cannot select a range of MATLAB code and execute "Link to Selection in Requirements Editor" if the selected range in MATLAB has existing outgoing traceability link(s).  You will encounter a hard error about mismatched structure fields during concatenation, for example: Number of fields in structure arrays being concatenated do not match.   Concatenation of structure arrays requires that these arrays have the same set of fields.??

Summary: Redirecting incoming links may crash MATLAB if you enable Change Information display
Description: There are two workflows to redirect incoming links that can cause this issue:  If you import requirements as references from an external document to a Simulink Requirements Set and select?Redirect Links to Imported References ?from the context menu of the Requirements Set in Requirements Browser.  If you have a Simulink Test license, you can redirect links that point to an internal test harness object to an external test harness object by exporting the test harness. All the links that point to Simulink objects in the internal test harness will redirect to the Simulink objects in the exported model.   In the above two workflows, MATLAB may crash if you choose to display Change Information for these links and access this information via the Requirements Editor  or the Requirements Perspective .

' is incorrectly generated outside of the for  loop 'for (s1_iter = 0;... '.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier takes long time to load existing test cases
Description: Simulink Design Verifier takes long time to load existing test cases when the user specifies Data file ?by selecting the option Extend existing test cases  in the Configuration parameters dialog box

Summary: Simulink Real-Time license is checked out when the Test Manager starts in Simulink Test
Description: On Microsoft Windows platforms, a Simulink Real-Time license is checked out when starting the Test Manager in Simulink Test. The license should be checked out when a Simulink Real-Time API is called, for example, in a Real-Time Test Case, when overriding parameters or running the test. The patch introduces the following change in behavior: In a Real-Time Test Case, in the "System Under Test" test case section, the drop-down list to select a target computer will remain empty until a Simulink Real-Time API is called (?Simulink Real-Time license is checked out) and the Test Case definition is re-opened.?
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Existing?requirements links from Simulink model to external requirements document?are not updated when the external document is imported into a Simulink Requirements Set
Description: When you import read-only requirements from an external document, existing navigation links from MATLAB/Simulink design elements to the imported requirements are not updated. When you click on a requirement link, the external document is loaded and the linked requirement is highlighted in the document's native application (for example, in an Excel worksheet) instead of highlighting the imported?content of the linked requirement in the Requirements Editor/Requirements Perspective.

Description: Windows 7 SP1 computer with updates from early January 2018 (KB4056894 and KB4056897) shuts down when you run a model with?Simulink Desktop Real-Time blocks. These updates apply security fixes that are incompatible with the real-time kernel code in Simulink Desktop Real-Time.?

Summary: Error in C++ compilation phase: IL entry write-read difference: region number 1
Description: Verification for C++ applications can sometimes fail during the compilation phase with an error message similar to this:? ? IL entry write-read difference: region number???1  ?????entry kind =??2 (constant),?  written = 31131,?  read = 31110  ????????missing entry = 184  ????????missing entry = 186  ????????missing entry = 187...? Internal error: read_memory_region: not all expected entries were read  ? Compilation aborted.  This can happen when an enum  class has been defined in one file and used in another file. The following code generates the error:  File a.cpp enum class E { EV }; File b.cpp enum class E;

Summary: Data cursors in the Simulation Data Inspector might show no value or incorrect value when first point is off-screen
Description: The data cursors in the Simulation Data Inspector show no value or an incorrect value when a signal with a nondouble data type is plotted and its first point is off-screen.

Summary: Cannot import multiple Excel worksheets from the same Excel file into the same requirements set
Description: You cannot import another worksheet from the same Excel file into the same requirements set.
Workaround: Import each additional worksheet into a separate?requirements set.

Summary: Invoking the Update functionality on a Requirement Set that uses custom attributes may lead to the corruption of the Requirement Set file
Description: After invoking the update functionality on a Requirement Set that contains external requirements that use custom attributes, the update process can corrupt the Requirement Set (.slreqx) file.?  The file corruption might not be apparent until a user restarts MATLAB and tries to reopen the Requirement Set?(.slreqx)?file. Opening the Requirement Set file can result in the following error in the MATLAB command window:  Warning: MF0 parse error in <<fileName.slreqx>>:?  Cannot establish association to a dangling reference to xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx.?   where x is some digit or lower-case letter.  This corruption is known to happen under the following conditions:  The external requirements are imported from an external data source and use custom attributes. For example, when importing from Microsoft Excel, users typically map some columns to custom attributes in the Requirement Set, or when using the command-line utilities to import from DOORS, users may also create custom attributes. Before Update, some rows were deleted in the external document. As a result, Update deleted from the Requirement Set some of the previously imported external requirements.

Summary: Not all required DLLs are packaged with packNGO for model with MIDI Controls block
Description: When you generate a standalone executable from a Simulink model that includes a MIDI Controls block,?not all dependencies are included in the .zip that is created with packNGo. Attempting to run the standalone executable fails with an error like    Could not open library: midi.dll.

Summary: Some custom library blocks don't appear in Library Browser
Description: Subsystems containing MATLAB Function blocks do not appear in the Library Browser tree. This issue occurs when these subsystems belong to libraries that are created or saved in R2017b or R2018a or libraries exported from a different release to R2017b or R2018a. ?

Summary: Custom FFTW callback with C++ code generation causes compiler error
Description: Compilation of the generated code?fails when you specify an FFTW?callback class and?the code generation target language is C++. An FFTW callback class specifies a custom library to use for fast Fourier transform algorithms.

Summary: The Quadcopter Project example does not support PARROT Mambo
Description: The Quadcopter Project example does not support PARROT Mambo.

Summary: Crash when opening test harness for a Stateflow block with requirement links
Description: MATLAB can crash when all of the following conditions are true: There is a test harness on a Stateflow chart, State Transition Table, Truth Table, MATLAB Function block, or Test Sequence block There is a Simulink requirement set with at least one requirement that is linked to an element (e.g., block) inside the above test harness The requirements set and the test harness are opened in sequence

Summary: Code errors in Create a Simulink Report Generator Report Programmatically example
Description: The code for the Create a Simulink Report Generator Report Programmatically example in the Getting Started of the documentation produces errors. The errors are caused by reusing the finder  and section  variable names. Also, the?StateflowDiagramElementFinder  is missing the  chart  input .

Summary: Sporadic error messages are displayed when Diagnostic Viewer attempts to add more than five tabs
Description: If the diagnostic viewer contains five tabs and a new tab is being added (due to any operation on the Simulink model),the oldest tab on the diagnostic viewer is deleted and a new tab is created on the Diagnostic viewer. While doing so, Diagnostic Viewer might not behave as expected and report errors in the MATLAB desktop. An example of the error message is as follows:  Index exceeds matrix dimensions?  Error in slmsgviewer/getTabInfo  Error in slmsgviewer/processStageEndRecord  Error in slmsgviewer/processStageRecord  Error in slmsgviewer.processRecord  This behavior can cause performance issue in model operations and any future diagnostics may not be reported.

Summary: Slow performance when adding many blocks in Simulink
Description: Adding many blocks to a Simulink model can be slow, such as when you use add_block ?in a MATLAB script,? or copy and paste many blocks. To diagnose this problem, use the MATLAB profiler to profile the MATLAB script or the copy-paste operation. In the profiler report, this problem manifests as a disproportionately large number of calls to the function getHarnessList .
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file. Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log?February 9, 2018:? attachment_g1707278_16b_2018-02-09.zip: initial version for R2016b.

Summary: Errors and inconsistencies in public command-line API for Simulink Requirements
Description: 1) After creating a new link object via link =  slreq.createLink(source, destination) , or?when using an existing link object, for example via link = requirement.inLinks() ,?you cannot delete a link: >> link.delete???% variable deleted in workspace but the link persists in Simulink Requirements session   >> link.remove?? % throws an error:??   No appropriate method, property, or field 'remove' for class 'slreq.Link'.? ? 2)??slreq.new('ReqSetName')  method does not create a file, and does not check whether a Requirements Set file with the given name already exists. This may cause loss of data when the new Requirements Set is saved. 3)??close()  method for the?slreq.ReqSet  class errors out?if you provide an optional argument to save or discard changes: >> reqSet.close()?? % works as expected?   >> reqSet.close(false)?? % DISCARD CHANGES use case - throws an error:??Too many input arguments    >> reqSet.close(true)?? %?SAVE CHANGES use case - throws an error:??Too many input arguments    4) ?reqSet.find()  method cannot?match results?by SID field value, for example: >> reqSet.find('type', 'Reference', 'SID', 3) ??? % returns empty, even though an slreq.Reference object with SID 3 exists? >> reqSet.find('type', 'Reference', 'SID', '3')? ??? % returns empty, even though an slreq.Reference object with SID 3 exists?

Summary: Diagram reporter does not hyperlink inactive variants
Description: The  Diagram  reporter does not create hyperlinks for inactive variant subsystem blocks in the top-level diagram. The hyperlinks should navigate to the corresponding subsystem diagrams.

Summary: Error when highlighting links to Excel and destination Range is missing or invalid
Description: MATLAB might throw the following error when you highlight requirement links to Excel, if the destination range is missing or invalid: In C:\?Program Files\MATLAB\R2016b\toolbox\? slrequirements\? +rmiref\? @ExcelUtil\? selectCell.m (selectCell) at 31 ?????hRange = hName.RefersToRange;?
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: During the Update operation, whitespace differences between documents are treated as real changes
Description: Whenyou edit the Microsoft Word or Excel document and import it into SimulinkRequirements as rich text content (HTML), the content may differ by the numberor position of various whitespace characters in the HTML. Some of thewhitespace characters include:? - Carriage Return (CR);? - LineFeed (LF) or Newline;? - Non-Breaking Space. In HTML, such whitespace is mostlyinsignificant and can be safely ignored, i.e., the HTML will render mostly asbefore, when viewed in an HTML browser. By not ignoring whitespace, theUpdate operation may indicate many more changes to the requirement descriptionsthan there really are in the documents, when some changes are caused onlybecause of whitespace characters inserted by Microsoft Word or Excel. There isno way, however, to distinguish between changes due to whitespace charactersfrom more meaningful changes. As a result, the change information is triggeredfor all requirements that the Update operation flags as having changed.?
Workaround: Use plain text rather than rich text when importing content from Microsoft Word or Excel. By using plain text, however, the user loses the ability to display tables and images in the requirement descriptions.

Summary: Copying to the clipboard stops working in the Live Editor in Windows 10
Description: Whencopying data to the clipboard from the Live Editor (for example, if you select code or text and type CTRL +C ),MATLAB might throw this warning: WARNING: Error in subscription handler java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: C:\?Users\?<username>\?AppData\?Local\Temp\ConnectorClipboard ... does not exist? After the warning occurs, copying and pasting no longer works in the Live Editor.? The problem occurs only in Windows 10, versions 1709 and above. In these versions, Windows 10 runs a SilentCleanup task?when the system enters an idle state.?SilentCleanup?tries to? free up disk space by removing empty temporary folders, which causes the problem.?
Workaround: To avoid this issue, stopSilentCleanup:1. Open a Command Prompt window as anadministrator.2. Runthis command to disable the?SilentCleanup task:schtasks.exe /change /TN "\Microsoft\Windows\DiskCleanup\SilentCleanup"/Disable3. Restart MATLAB.Note: When you disable the SilentCleanup task,unnecessary files such as temporary files are not automatically deleted. If you disabled the SilentCleanup task, make sure to regulary check for unnecessary files and delete them manually to avoid storing unused temporary files and running out of disk space.?To restart?SilentCleanup:1. Open a Command Prompt window as an administrator.2. Run this command to enable the?SilentCleanup?task:schtasks.exe /change /TN "\Microsoft\Windows\DiskCleanup\SilentCleanup"/Enable

Summary: Unable to generate HDL code for subsystems with no input ports in a design with clock-rate pipelining enabled
Description: When a Simulink? model has clock-rate pipelining enabled, and contains a Subsystem with no input ports, HDL code generation for the Subsystem can crash MATLAB? with a stack similar to this:? [? 0] 0x0000000352ef0613? ? ? ?bin\win64\pir_core.dll+00263699 pir::AbstractPortManager::reorderPorts+00000099?

Summary: Created link to IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation artifact points to initial stream version of artifact,not the one that was linked
Description: Artifacts link created with IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation in a Project Area under Configuration Management points to the base version of?artifact, instead of the current configured?version.Navigation from Simulink?displays the base version of artifact in the Initial Stream?configuration, instead of the expected linked configuration

Summary: When you import rich text requirements and select the option toignore section numbers, the descriptions of imported requirements still includesection numbers
Description: When you import rich text requirements and select the option toignore section numbers, the descriptions of imported requirements still includesection numbers. Each imported requirement is tied to a specific sectionnumber. When you try to synchronize your requirements set with the externaldocument by using the Update functionality, you will notice that manyrequirement descriptions have changed, including the requirements that werereordered in the external document.? The recommended practice is to ignore section?numbers, in order to handle changes to the external document such as having new requirements inserted before or after previously imported requirements, or having existing requirements moved around within the document, which may change the section numbering, but not change the descriptions of previously imported requirements.
Workaround: Import content from external documents as plain text instead of rich text, if the requirement descriptions do not have tables or images.

Summary: VideoReader?fails to read certain video files on Windows
Description: On Windows platform, reading certain video files using?VideoReader ?may cause MATLAB to crash or to return distorted data.? This has been observed with certain AVI and AVC encoded MPEG-2 TS video files.
Workaround: Turn the?Hardware Acceleration?option off using:?matlab.video.read.UseHardwareAcceleration('off')
