Summary: Unexpected warnings about model being saved in previous release while executing the DO Qualification Kit test procedure for?Simulink Verification and Validation
Description: When executing the qualification test procedure?qualkitdo_slvnv_tcp1.rpt? for Simulink Verification and Validation, the following warnings might be thrown in the command line window: The model <model name> was saved in a previous release. The Simulation range checking setting for Stateflow and MATLAB Function blocks is now set to 'none'  The model <model name>?was saved in a previous release. The overflow diagnostic setting for Stateflow and MATLAB Function blocks is now set to 'warning'
Workaround: Use one of these workarounds:Ignore these warnings because they do not affect the model coverage results and have no impact on the coverage reportOpen the test models that are causing the warning and resave in the current Simulink release

Summary: Signal object used in referenced model causes error when updating diagram or simulating
Description: If you have a referenced model that uses a signal object (such as Simulink.Signal )?in a Simulink data dictionary (sldd  file), simulating or updating the block diagram might generate an error like this: The Simulink signal object 'ABC' for output port 1 of block 'myModel/myBlock' was changed during simulation.
Workaround: Set the simulation mode of the Model block to Accelerator before updating the diagram or simulating.

Summary: Diagram reporter fails to report on?Stateflow.Box subchart
Description: The  Diagram  reporter fails to produce a snapshot of a Stateflow.Box  subchart and issues an error indicating that the object is an invalid source.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier takes longer than expected to display results in the Results Summary window after processing all objectives
Description: Simulink Design Verifier takes longer than expected to display results in the?Results Summary  window after processing all objectives

Summary: Calls to the power builtin with real gpuArray inputs sometimes silently returns wrong answers
Description: A call to the power  builtin function with a fractional double scalar exponent on negative inputs is expected to error out with the message: Error using .^POWER: needs to return a complex result, but this is not supported for real input X and Y on the GPU. Use POWER(COMPLEX(X),COMPLEX(Y,0)) instead.  However, if this call to power  is preceded by another call to power  with a integer scalar exponent, then instead of erroring the second call returns wrong results: >> % The following call returns correct answers and should not error.>> x = gpuArray(-1:10);>> x.^5ans =  Columns 1 through 6          -1           0           1          32         243        1024  Columns 7 through 12        3125        7776       16807       32768       59049      100000>> % The following call should error but does not, and silently returns a wrong answer.>> x.^(0.5)ans =  Columns 1 through 6       NaN         0    1.0000    1.4142    1.7321    2.0000  Columns 7 through 12    2.2361    2.4495    2.6458    2.8284    3.0000    3.1623  As shown in these reproduction steps, the second call to power  returns NaN results in the cases where the imaginary part is not zero.

Summary: Target computer sporadically crashes when model uses serial communication blocks
Description: A target computer running a model that uses serial communication blocks might crash with a bus error in fiforeadwrite.c. You can find out where a crash occurred in your model by running the host command SimulinkRealTime.crashInfo.display after the target reboots.

Summary: Error opening Model Configuration Parameters for models using Polyspace and TargetLink
Description: Opening the Configuration Parameters dialog box for a Simulink model results in an error when all of the following conditions are met:? dSPACE TargetLink is installed. The model is configured to use either Polyspace Bug Finder or Polyspace Code Prover. The model is not configured to use an ERT target.  The error message displayed in the dialog box is: An unexpected error occurred while creating the Configuration Parameters Dialog. This might be caused by functions in your MATLAB path that shadow build-in MATLAB functions.  Undefined function 'getparam' for input arguments of type 'double'. ?  In Simulink versions prior to R2017b, this error occurred only on the All Parameters tab of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Diagram reporter accepts non-subchart?Stateflow.State objects?
Description: The Diagram  reporter accepts?Stateflow.State ?object inputs regardless of whether or not they are subcharts. The Diagram  reporter should accept Stateflow.State  objects only if they are subcharts.

Summary: Report API cannot report on a model that cannot be compiled
Description: The Report API by default attempts to compile a model before reporting on it. If the model cannot be compiled, report generation terminates.?
Workaround: The Report API's Reporter object has a hidden property that disables this default behavior, thereby?allowing reporting on uncompilable models. To generate a report without compiling the model, set the hidden?CompileModelBeforeReporting property to false. For example,rpt = slreportgen.report.Report("Sample","pdf");?rpt.CompileModelBeforeReporting = false;?model_name = "sf_car";?ch =? mlreportgen.report.Chapter("Title","sf_car Model");load_system(model_name);?diagram = slreportgen.report.Diagram(model_name);?add(ch,diagram);?add(rpt,ch);?close(rpt);?close_system(model_name);rptview(rpt);?

Summary: Diagram reporter hyperlinks fail for Stateflow subchart blocks
Description: Hyperlinks for Stateflow Atomic subchart blocks in the top-level diagram may not navigate to the corresponding subchart diagram. Hyperlinks may also fail for other Stateflow subchart blocks, such as?Simulink.Function , if the subchart diagram is reported before the top-level diagram.

Summary: Figure reporter does not support?SVG as default snapshot format
Description: The Figure  reporter?auto  snapshot format?uses the ?"HG images" option in the MATLAB Report Generator panel of the MATLAB Preferences dialog box to specify the format of a figure snapshot. However, the "HG images" option does not allow you to specify SVG as a format.
Workaround: Explicitly set the?SnapshotFormat property of the Figure reporter to svg.

Summary: Diagram reporter does not support?SVG as a default snapshot format
Description: The?Diagram  reporter's auto  snapshot format?uses the image file format specified in the MATLAB Report Generator panel of MATLAB Preferences. However, this behavior is not consistent with the MATLAB Figure  reporter, which uses SVG as the?auto?snapshot format.
Workaround: Explicitly set the?SnapshotFormat?property of the?Diagram?reporter to?svg.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using units from a disallowed unit system in a Simulink.Parameter object
Description: MATLAB crashes when you run a model or update diagram?when these?conditions are true: The Unit  parameter of a Simulink.Parameter  object is set to a unit system that is not allowed. The Simulink.Parameter  object is passed as a parameter to an S-Function, Stateflow chart, or MATLAB Function block.   Allowed unit systems are specified by the AllowedUnitSystems ?configuration parameter or by a Unit System Configuration block.

Summary: ModelAdvisor exclusion is not filtering Stateflow library blocks
Description: ModelAdvisor incorrectly reports Stateflow library blocks that should?befiltered by?the user-defined exclusion.?

Summary: Unable to close MATLAB file linked to currently selected Simulink Requirements entity.
Description: When a code range in a MATLAB file is linked to a currently selected requirement in the Requirements Editor, it is not possible to close the file. When you close the file, it is reopened again after a brief moment.

Summary: "Illegal memory access" error when calling functions on gpuArrays with Fermi cards (compute capability of 2.x) using CUDA 9 drivers
Description: When calling some functions on gpuArrays, you can sometimes see the following error message. For example:  >> x = log(gpuArray(1:10))Error using gpuArray/ logAn unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:an illegal memory access was encountered  This has occurred on Fermi cards (compute capability of 2.x) using CUDA 9 drivers in R2017b.The affected list of functions is: logacosacoshatanhacothasinacscasecasech
Workaround: Downgrade to a CUDA 8 driver in the 37x range. Go to?NVIDIA's driver download page, select the link "Beta and Older Drivers", select your GPU, OS, and CUDA 8.0 as the Toolkit. Then look for a driver in the 37x range such as 376.33.Alternatively, precede the call that errors with a superfluous call to mod with your data:>> x = gpuArray(1:10); % Your data.>> mod(x,0);?????????? % Bring in correct memory location.>> y = log(x);???????? % Original erroring function.

Summary: System Requirements block in Simulink model appears empty
Description: If you have a System Requirements block in your Simulink model?for the purpose of displaying?links associated with the model or a subsystem, the block may appear empty when you open the model/subsystem.???
Workaround: Move the block slightly on the canvas to make its contents visible.

Summary: Cannot create requirements traceability links from more then one character range in MATLAB Function code
Description: When you?create a requirement traceability link from a range of code in MATLAB Function block, the first link is created correctly and linked range is highlighted in MATLAB Editor.? But when you try to link another range of characters in the same MATLAB Function, the new link overwrites the previously created link at the first linked range, and second range is not linked and not highlighted in the Editor.?? As the result, you can only link a single character range in a MATLAB Function.?? Unfortunately, this causes loss of data, because every next failed attempt overwrites the previously created links at the first character range.??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assertion error ?or?incorrect generated code might occur for a model containing a?ForEach subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code or an assertion might occur for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a Selector or Demux block The Selector or Demux block output connects to the input of a ForEach Subsystem, or the Selector or Demux block is inside the ForEach subsystem and a subsystem inport connects to the block's input.  The ForEach subsystem partitions the input signal that connects to the Selector or Demux blocks into elements or subarrays.   If the partitioned input signal has more than one dimension, instead of generating incorrect code, MATLAB might throw the following assertion error during the build process: assertion 'aMatSubXVec->size() == 1 && aMatSubXVec->at(0).fStepSizes.size() == 1'

Summary: Error using netcdflib?when attempting to read from some OPeNDAP servers
Description: The ncread  function returns an error?when trying to access certain OPeNDAP servers.??For example, the following returns an error: url = 'https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/thredds/dodsC/namanl/201609/20160929/namanl_218_20160929_0000_000.grb';?  x = ncread(url,'x');?  Error messages:? Error using netcdflib?  Unable to open file. File may be corrupt or filename may have invalid characters. If the data source was an OPeNDAP URL, see the?  OPeNDAP Troubleshooting section in the Users Guide.?    Error in netcdf.open (line 59)?  [varargout{:}] = netcdflib ('open', filename, varargin{1} );?    Error in internal.matlab.imagesci.nc/openToRead (line 1278)?  this.ncRootid = netcdf.open(this.Filename,'NOWRITE');?    Error in internal.matlab.imagesci.nc (line 121)?  this.openToRead();?    Error in ncread (line 53)?  ncObj = internal.matlab.imagesci.nc(ncFile);

Summary: Compilation error: class has no member "type"
Description: When running Polyspace Code Prover on C++?source code, the compilation phase can fail?with an error message such as this: C:\compiler\?usr\? include\? c++\? 4.8.2\? functional,?  line 1697: error: class "std::result_of<std::_Mem_fn<void (std::__future_base::_State_base::*)(std::function<std::__future_base::_State_base::_Ptr_type ()> &,?  bool &)> (std::__future_base::_State_base *,?  std::reference_wrapper<std::function<std::__future_base::_State_base::_Ptr_type ()>>,?  std::reference_wrapper<bool>)>" has no member "type"?   The issue can happen when analyzing two C++ files that include the <functional>  header file.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you confirm closure of a MATLAB app
Description: MATLAB crashes when you confirm closure of an app which uses questdlg  in its close request function (i.e.,?UIFigureCloseRequest  or EditorFigureCloseRequest ).
Workaround: Use?uiconfirm instead of questdlg in the close request function.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: FPGA-in-the-Loop generates wrong reset logic when set to "active-low"
Description: When the reset asserted level is set to"active-low", FPGA-in-the-Loop generates incorrect control logic which?sets reset value?to "low" during simulation. This causes the HDL design to enter?a reset state and generate the wrong simulation result.

Summary: Unable to see aggregated coverage data in Simulink Test Manager after consistency errors
Description: When running tests in Simulink Test Manager, the MATLAB command window displays consistency errors, and aggregated coverage does not appear in the Coverage Results  section.

Summary: MATLAB can freeze when calling matlab.addons.installedAddons in certain conditions
Description: MATLAB can freeze when calling matlab.addons.installedAddons  in these conditions:  MATLAB is started with the -r  option ?and the function passed as a parameter in turn directly or indirectly calls matlab.addons.installedAddons . In this case, MATLAB freezes at startup while the Command Window is still initializing. A MATLAB script, function, or class installs an add-on using a?matlab.addons  function? (such as matlab.addons.toolbox.installToolbox ) and then immediately calls matlab.addons.installedAddons .
Workaround: If the freeze occurs when running a MATLAB script, function, or class that installs an add-on using a matlab.addons function, avoid?running the?matlab.addons install function?and?matlab.addons.installedAddons?back to back within the file. Instead call them as two separate commands in the Command Window.

Summary: Some source files seem to disappear from module when opening project from a previous product release
Description: When you open a project created in a previous release, for instance, open a pre-R2017b project in the R2017b Polyspace user interface, some source files seem to disappear from a project module. The issue typically happens when you add a new include folder to the project.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results and code generation for handle class instances in embedded MATLAB Function blocks
Description: When two (or more) handle class instance variables appear in a MATLAB Function (that is, k1  and k2  below). function y = problematic(u)??? k1 = handleClass(u);??? k2 = handleClass(2*u);??? y = [k1.getValue k2.getValue];  In simulation and the generated code, optimizations may merge these improperly into a single instance of the handle class (note k2  disappears). function y = problematic(u)??? k1 = handleClass(u);??? k1 = handleClass(2*u);??? y = [k1.getValue k1.getValue];  The result is a wrong answer. For an example of this issue, please see the file 'issue_example/issue_1688298_example.slx'  in the attached zip file 'issue_1688298.zip' .
Workaround: To workaround this issue force the constructor of the handle class to be inlined using the coder.inline('always') directive. For an example of the workaround, please see the file 'workaround_example/handleClass.m' and the corresponding model 'workaround_example/workaround_1688298_example.slx' in the attached zip file??'issue_1688298.zip'.

Summary: U1052?build error when using the Visual Studio 2017 compiler to build code generated using the TMF approach
Description: If you use the Microsoft Visual Studio 2017 compiler to build code generated from a model configured to use the Template Makefile approach, and either "VSCMD_START_DIR" is defined in the environment or?the "%USERPROFILE%\Source" directory exists, Simulink reports a build failure with the following error message: ?NMAKE : fatal error U1052: file '<model_name>.mk' not found?  See?Bug Report ID 1649009 ?for a similar issue when building code from a model configured to use the Microsoft Visual Studio 2017 toolchain.
Workaround: Run the following command after opening MATLAB:>> setenv('VSCMD_START_DIR',?'%CD%');?For more details, please refer to the following link:?https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/content/problem/26780/vsdevcmdbat-changes-the-current-working-directory.html?

Summary: Pressing Control+S while editing requirements clears all unapplied changes in the current edit field
Description: In the Requirements Editor, any edits made to requirements content are applied automatically when the active focus is moved from the field being edited to another. If you use the Control+S key combination to save your work, your changes in the current edit field are cleared. However, any changes you made in other fields will be saved in the requirements set file.?
Workaround: Click a different area in the Requirements Editor to move the active focus before using Control+S to save the requirements set.

Summary: Execution of real-time models that contain interrupt triggered function-call subsystems can skip execution steps
Description: Real-time model execution steps can be skipped if the following conditions are all true: The model contains a function-call subsystem that is triggered by hardware interrupts The execution mode for the model is interrupt mode, not polling mode The model configuration enables Allow tasks to execute concurrently on target .

Summary: Incorrect caching of imported rich text content may lead to stale data being used for Import or Update from Microsoft Word or Excel
Description: Simulink Requirements might use stale data for descriptions of requirements?(text, tables, or images) that do not match the current document on disk, under the following scenario: Import requirements as rich text content from a Microsoft Word or Excel document of some timestamp t1. Replace the previously imported document with another document of timestap t2, for example, by reverting a checked-out document, or by extracting it from an archive.  During Import, if t2 is older than t1, Import might read stale cached data that was produced when importing the document at timestamp t1, instead of getting the rich text content directly from the document at timestamp t2.?  Similarly, the Update operation might read the stale cached data, and indicate that there are no changes, even when there are content changes in the document between timestamps t1 and t2.
Workaround: After making changes to your requirements document(s):Close MATLABDelete the folders <TEMP>\RMI\MSWORD and <TEMP>\RMI\MSEXCEL, where <TEMP> is your Windows temp folderRestart MATLABPerform the Import or Update operationYou can locate your Windows temp folder by running the following command at the Windows command prompt:echo %TEMP%

Summary: Heading truncated when?importing requirementscontents from IBM Rational DOORS
Description: DOORS Object Heading values that are used to populate the Summary field values of imported requirements are truncated to about 50 characters.

Summary: After importing requirements from some Microsoft Word document, loading a previously saved Requirement Set file produces an error
Description: After importing requirements from some Microsoft Word documents, typically ones that contain special characters such as vertical tabs, the user is able to save the Requirement Set successfully. Attempting to load the Requirement Set file (with the .slreqx extension) later on, either through the User Interface or through the command-line, however, may produce an error and the file may fail to load with an XML parser error.  This is because some of these special characters are not valid inside XML files, which are used internally in the slreqx file. If an XML parse error occurs, Simulink Requirements invokes some error recovery code to detect special characters that may have prevented the loading of the file, and replace each such character with a whitespace. The error recovery code, however, did not handle exhaustively all character occurrences. When the error recovery code is triggered, the user may see the following warnings in the MATLAB command-window: Warning: XML parser errors while loading <<REQSET_FILE.slreqx>>. Attempting to recover... Warning: Recovery successful. Replaced each illegal character with a space while loading?<<REQSET_FILE.slreqx>>. Save the Requirement Set to accept changes.

Summary: Update diagram during simulation can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB can crash under the following conditions: A?model contains a block that converts a parameter with an enumerated data type to an alias of the enumerated data type.  While simulating the model (to update parameter values), you do an update diagram.  This?issue can also occur if you run multiple simulations in fast restart on the same model described above.
Workaround: Use enumerated data types directly in your models instead of?aliases to enumerated data types.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check errors for non-scalar Boolean operations in assignment statements in C Charts
Description: Beginning in R2017b, scalar Boolean operations in assignment statements (such as those found in Stateflow states and transitions) are analyzed for coverage as well as for test generation via Simulink Design Verfier.? Simulink Coverage does not support Boolean operations with one or more non-scalar operands, and assignments containing such operations are not analyzed for coverage. Note that non-scalar Boolean operations are not valid MATLAB syntax but can be found in Stateflow C Charts. Due to a bug in Simulink Design Verifier, the following error will occur when attempting to perform a Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check on a model containing a non-scalar Boolean operation in an assignment statement in a C Chart. Checking compatibility...  Errors occurred during translation:  fromPointerType   ?As such, some models that were compatible with Simulink Design Verifier in previous releases are shown as incompatible in R2017b.?

Summary: No "backlink" from DOORS Next Generation requirement artifacts to design objects in MATLAB/Simulink when trying to use bi-directional linking from MATLAB/Simulink side
Description: When creating traceability link from MATLAB/Simulink to a requirement in DNG (DOORS Next Generation), the "backlink" from DNG to Simulink is not automatically created when DNG Requirements Project Area has Configuration Management option enabled. There is no error or warning,? one-way link from Simulink to DNG is successfully created, as if the 2-way linking option is disabled.

Summary: Incorrect terminate event logging in accelerate mode when a test harness model containing IRT?uses dataset logging
Description: If you simulate in Accelerator mode a test harness model containing Initialize Reset Terminate subsystems, dataset logging will incorrectly log terminate events.

Summary: DMR client's dictionary function threw std::exception 'Invalid access'?error during simulation
Description: When you simulate a model with a dashboard Display block bound to a signal with dimensions 1x1, Simulink throws the following error and then MATLAB becomes unresponsive. DMR client's dictionary function threw std::exception 'Invalid access'?
Workaround: Change the signal dimensions bound to the dashboard Display block to scalar 1 using the Reshape block.

Summary: writetable displays a?popup window asking for permission to overwrite Excel file
Description: When writing a table?to an?XLSM  file (a spreadsheet file that contains macros) using writetable, ?Excel? displays a popup window?asking for permission to overwrite the file.  For instance, if you have an XLSM  file called test.xlsm  and you use writetable  to write a sample table to this file, Excel? displays a popup?asking for permission to replace the file.? >> T = table({'M';'M';'F';'F';'F'},[38;43;38;40;49],[71;69;64;67;64],[176;163;131;133;119]);?  >> writetable(T,'test.xlsm');

Summary: Toolchain definition in?Build 32-bit DLL on 64-bit Windows? Platform Using MSVC Toolchain example contains an error
Description: In the example?Build 32-bit DLL on 64-bit Windows? Platform Using MSVC Toolchain , the toolchain definition?contains an error. This error can cause generated code to crash. The?toolchain directs the?MSVC compiler to be invoked with preprocessor definitions, which should only be used when compiling 64-bit objects and not?32-bit objects.?This?results in?unexpected runtime behavior, such as wrong answers or access violations.?In addition, if you use the toolchain to link an executable, the toolchain directs the MSVC linker to link against Windows 64-bit system libraries instead of 32-bit system libraries. This linking?results in run-time access violations. A previous version of this example?appeared in the Simulink Coder documentation entitled Add MSVC Toolchain on 64-bit Windows? to Build 32-bit DLL .

Summary: MATLAB might?crash when custom storage classes are used in transition labels
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation for a Stateflow chart that meets all of the following conditions: Two or more transitions leave a junction or state. Those transitions compare the same expression to different constants. At least one of the constants is a custom storage class. At least one of the transitions does not contain condition-actions.
Workaround: There are two possible work arounds:?Provide a condition-action (such as a comment) instead of leaving the condition-action empty.Avoid using custom storage classes in transitions whose conditions share expressions with other transitions.

Summary: Invalid or deleted object?error message for Model Advisor check "Check Stateflow charts for strong data typing"
Description: Model Advisor check "Check     Stateflow charts for strong data typing" (check IDs mathworks.do178.hisf_0015      and mathworks.iec61508.hisf_0015 ) errors with message Abnormal exit:     Invalid or deleted object .

Summary: fread?and binblockread are slow when reading large data sizes
Description: Using fread  or binblockread? with interface objects (serial, tcpip, udp, bluetooth, gpib  and  visa ) to read a large amount of data is slow if the Terminator   is not manually set to empty (''). For example, reading a 5 MB data block can take up to 7 seconds.??
Workaround: Set the Terminator?property to '' (null) before using fread or binblockread to ensure efficient throughput of binary data. For example:t = tcpip('localhost',4020);t.Terminator = '';

Summary: Figure reporter produces image too big to fit on report page
Description: A Figure reporter can produce an image of a MATLAB figure too big to report on a page, depending on the size of the figure.

Summary: Diagram reporter produces images too big to fit on a page
Description: The Diagram reporter can produce images of block diagrams and charts that are too big to fit on a report page, depending on the size of the block diagram or chart. The resulting image is cut off in Word reports and flows onto the next page in PDF reports.

Summary: MATLAB tools might fail to open when using a proxy server
Description: Some MATLAB tools might fail to open when MATLAB is using an authenticated proxy server. These tools include: App Designer Simulink Data Inspector Root Inport Mapping Simulink Template Gallery Export Model to Template Add-Ons Explorer Simulink Start Page

Summary: MATLAB fails to launch with Windows user profile containing Unicode characters
Description: If?you have?Chinese, Japanese, or Korean (CJK) characters in your user profile, then MATLAB cannot be launched on a Windows machine. Nothing happens when you double click the MATLAB.exe file in Windows Explorer

Summary: Dragging a signal from a Bus Selector can cause?Simulink to respond very slowly
Description: While dragging a signal from a Bus Selector block, Simulink can respond very slowly and appear to stop responding. This condition occurs when the bus is fed by custom S-functions.
Workaround: Install and extract the attached file, as described in the instructions. Then run the command disable_automatic_port_creation at the beginning of each MATLAB session (you can automate this by adding it to your startup.m script).Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Saving project throws "file not found on disk" error
Description: Saving a project might present an error dialog that prevents the project from being saved. The error dialog contains the following text: Cannot save project file. File <filepath>.view was not found on disk. ?

Summary: Error when using arrayfun and some element-wise functions withgpuArray input arguments and GPUs withcompute capability 2.0 or 2.1
Description: If you call arrayfun  with a gpuArray  as input argument and have an NVIDIA? Fermi GPU  with computecapability 2.0 or 2.1 selected, then arrayfun  returns the following error: Error using gpuArray/ arrayfun?  Requested code-table for unsupported compute-capability: 2.1  or the equivalent for GPUs with compute capability 2.0. Also,a significant number of element-wise functions, including?sqrt , power , log10,   and eps   cause the following error whenused with a gpuArray   i nput argument: An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:  CUDA_ERROR_UNKNOWN  To check the computecapability of your GPU, enter gpuDevice   at the command line.  Examinethe?ComputeCapability f  ield of theoutput. If this value is 2.0 or 2.1, then your GPU is affected.

Summary: Error when trying to generate code for deep learning networks on Windows
Description: On Windows, MATLAB throws an error when you try to generate code for networks that contain layers such as leaky ReLU or batch normalization. For example, using the codegen  function to generate code for a YOLO network results in the following error: ?? >> codegen -config coder.gpuConfig('mex')? yolo_predict.m? -args {ones(448,448,3)}  ------------------------------------------------------------------------  gmake: *** No rule to make target '$codegen_dir\MWLeakyReLULayerImpl.obj', needed by 'mnisteg_mex.mexw64'.? Stop.  ------------------------------------------------------------------------  ??? Build error: C++ compiler produced errors. See the Build Log for further details.  Code generation failed.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running SIL/PIL simulation of AUTOSAR model that contains QueuedExplicitReceive ports
Description: If an AUTOSAR?model contains?a mix of QueuedExplicitReceive input ports and other data ports, MATLAB might crash when you run a SIL or PIL simulation of the model.

Summary: Library block OpenFcn using open_system() call with 'parameter'?as second input errors when saving
Description: If a library contains a block whose OpenFcn  callback is set to an open_system()  call with??'parameter' ?as the second input argument, saving that library causes an error.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when closing an externally saved Test Harness
Description: In the absence of a Simulink Test license, closing an externally saved Test Harness will cause a crash if the Test Harness was opened using the user interface, opened using?open_system , or loaded using load_system.
Workaround: Use either sltest.harness.open or sltest.harness.load?to open or load the externally saved test harness. In the absence of a Simulink Test license, this will correctly load the externally saved harness without any linkage to the main model.

Summary: Attempting to set the rate above 1E+6 for MCC analog input channels generates an error
Description: If you try to set the data acquisition session Rate  property value above 1E+6 while using analog input channels from Measurement Computing devices such as a USB 2020, an error results.? For example: >> s = daq.createSession('mcc');>> addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Board0','Ai0','Voltage');>> s.Rate = 1e8Rate cannot exceed 1000000 in the current configuration.
Workaround: For MATLAB R2017a, a fix is provided as an update to "Data Acquisition Toolbox Support Package for Measurement Computing Hardware" (version 17.1.1). Update your support package installation to this latest version.To check for updates to support packages, on the MATLAB? Home tab, in the Environment section, click Add-Ons > Check for Updates > Hardware Support Packages.

Summary: After updating a Requirement Set from an external source, some users may encounter an error while using the application; or links may get resolved to incorrect requirements
Description: After invoking the Update feature on a Requirement Set, some users may encounter an error while continuing to use the application.This situation may occur if some requirements were deleted in the external source, and there are links to these now deleted requirements. The following errors (an error dialog as well as errors displayed in the MATLAB command window) may be encountered while continuing to use the application, in particular, when switching from the Requirements View to the Links View, or when saving the updated LinkSets either directly (File > Save All ) or by saving the model. The error dialog may say:? Error:slreq.gui.Toolbar.viewChangeCallback  Insufficient number of outputs from right hand side of equal sign to satisfy assignment.  If the user restarts MATLAB, and re-loads the updated Requirement Set and the Link Sets, previous links to the now deleted requirements may get resolved to refer to incorrect requirements instead of appearing as invalid or dangling links Those links will have to be deleted and re-created to refer to the correct requirements.

Summary: C/C++ code generation from the System objects?dsp.UDPSender or dsp.Receiver might fail due to compiler warnings or errors
Description: C/C++ code generation from the System objects?dsp.UDPSender  or dsp.Receiver  might fail due to compiler warnings or errors on some C/C++ compilers. This failure happens due to a missing? #ifndef (header file guard) code in a .hpp file used in the code generation process for these System objects. The header file is used during host machine code generation for these specific System objects. An example of this compilation failure occurs in a Simulink model using MATLAB Function blocks that contain code using dsp.UDPSender  or dsp.Receiver  System objects.  Certain C/C++ compilers on Linux platforms using recent Ubuntu?installations might fail when building the generated C/C++ code, in particular. One such Simulink build error looks similar to the following:? ? ??HostLib_rtw.o: In function `DestroyHostLibrary':?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x15): undefined reference to `dlclose'?????HostLib_rtw.o: In function `CreateHostLibrary':?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x120): undefined reference to `dlopen'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x15b): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x16e): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x181): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x194): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x1a7): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.o:HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x1ba): more undefined references to `dlsym' follow?????collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status??????????gmake: *** [testUDPsend_rec_sfun.mexa64] Error 255? ? ??Error:Unable to create mex function 'testUDPsend_rec_sfun.mexa64' required for simulation.

Summary: Previous import statements are not recognized when running individual sections
Description: When a section is run in the Editor using the Run Section ?buttons,?import? statements from previous sections are not recognized. For example, if a class is imported in the first section and then used in later sections, MATLAB throws an Unrecognized function or variable  error.

Summary: Apps that previously shared callbacks with deleted components do not open
Description: App Designer apps created in R2016b might not open in App Designer using R2017b. The problem occurs when the R2016b app has a shared callback, and one of the components sharing the callback is deleted. When you try to open the app in R2017b, a dialog box displays the following message:Error loading filename .mlapp.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during simulation or update diagram of a model hierarchy containing Variant Source blocks connected to global Data Store Read/Write blocks
Description: MATLAB may crash during simulation or update diagram of a model hierarchy if all of the following are true: A referenced model in the hierarchy has a global data store read block connected to an inactive input port of a Variant Source block The top model of the hierarchy has a global data store write block fed by an inactive Variant Source block. The Data Store Read/Write blocks specify the same Simulink.Signal object for the "Data store name" block parameter.??
Workaround: Turn off 'Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate pre-processor conditionals' for the Variant Source/Sink blocks in the model?Add Data Store Memory blocks to top and reference models referring to the commonly named data stores

Summary: Metric Engine may fail to collect compile-time metric data
Description: When collecting metric data for a model containing a Unit System Configuration block inside of a?library-linked subsystem, the model may fail to compile even though it compiles fine?when you are not collecting metric data. You will get the?following warning:? The model <model name> has changed between the time when two instances of this model were compiled.??This is not allowed for models referenced in Normal Mode.?  Compile time metrics like mathworks.metrics.CyclomaticComplexity  will fail to report metric data.?

Summary: Cannot?update imported references to DOORS requirements
Description: The Update  button in the?Requirements Editor does not update?referenced requirements imported from IBM Rational DOORS. After the original import from the DOORS module, the structure and contents?displayed in the Requirements Editor match the?original content in DOORS. But if you modify the DOORS module?(add, remove, or edit objects?or adjust parent-child relationships), using the update functionality in the Requirements Editor fails.?Previously imported references do not refresh to match the modified contents in DOORS.?

Summary: Table entries might overflow table boundaries in Report Explorer PDF reports
Description: For?Acrobat (PDF)  file format reports, table components, such as?Simulink Automatic Table , might fail to hyphenate the table contents. This can cause long, unbreakable content to overflow the table entry boundaries.

Summary: Invalid Cidentifiers generated for enumerations not derived from int32
Description: For?code generation, enumerations in MATLAB can be derived from these integer types:? int8,?uint8,?int16,?uint16, int32.    In R2017b, when using enumerations that?do?not derive from int32 , it is possible to generate invalid C identifiers. As a result, the generated C code does not compile.  This problem?can occur when?the same?matrix or structure of enumeration constants appears in two different functions, and the matrix or structure becomes a single constant. The attached example demonstrates such a ?matrix of enumeration constants.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Use enumerations that derive from int32.Use integer casts around enums if they do not derive from int32. See attached example:For workaround 1, use codegen sample1 -args {enum_int32(0)}For workaround 2, use?codegen sample2 -args {enum_uint16(0)}

Summary: Error when using GPUs with compute capability 7.0 or above with convolutional neural network functions such as?trainNetwork,?classify?and?activations
Description: Neural Network Toolbox uses the cuDNN Deep Learning library to train and execute convolutional neural networks on the GPU. The version of this library used by MATLAB does not work on GPUs with a compute capability of 7.0 or above, including the latest Volta architecture cards. If you try to train or run a network using one of these GPUs, you see an error. This occurs with the function?trainNetwork  and?the?classify ,?predict ?and?activations ?methods of?SeriesNetwork . It also occurs with functions in Computer Vision System Toolbox that use convolutional neural networks. The error usually has the ID 'nnet:cnngpu:CuDNNInitFailed'  and the message 'Failed to initialize the cuDNN handle. Return code was CUDNN_STATUS_INTERNAL_ERROR.'  Other error messages are possible, depending on the exact way the error was triggered. To see if your GPU is affected, in MATLAB, enter:   gpuDevice Examine the field?ComputeCapability . If this value is 7.0 ?or higher, your card will not work with convolutional neural networks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Merging output?from an?Initialize, Reset,?or Terminate Function block to a root Outport block does not work as expected in generated code
Description: Generated code from your model does not work as expected when the following conditions are met: The model contains an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block with an output port. The output of?the Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block is combined with an output of another block using a Merge block. The Merge block drives a root Outport block.  The intent of the above modeling pattern is to use an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block to update the output of?a model during an initialize, reset, or terminate event. However, Simulink creates separate buffers for the Merge block and the root Outport block, causing the model output to remain unchanged during a call to?a model?initialize, reset, or terminate function. This causes a wrong?answer when the generated code is integrated with the target run-time environment.?A wrong answer can also?occurs when using a code generation based target such as rapid accelerator for top model simulation or model reference simulation in software-in-the-loop (SIL) mode with top model code interface.

Summary: "Runs cannot be compared" error in Fixed-Point Tool
Description: In the Fixed-Point Tool, after completing the workflow to convert a model to fixed point and clicking the Compare Results  button to examine the system behavior in Simulation Data Inspector, the Fixed-Point Tool throws the error message,? "Runs cannot be compared. Ensure that signals have been logged in more? than 1 runs." The tool throws this error even when more than one run is available if the 'SignalLogging ' parameter of the model is set to 'off' .
Workaround: To compare results in the Fixed-Point Tool, turn on the signal logging parameter of the model.For example, in MATLAB, enterset_param(model_name, 'SignalLogging', 'on');

Summary: dsp.HighpassFilter or?dsp.LowpassFilter?objects generate IIR filter representations with incorrectly scaled magnitude responses
Description: In rare cases,?dsp.HighpassFilter? and?dsp.LowpassFilter  might generate IIR filter representations with incorrectly scaled magnitude responses.

Summary: Solver Profiler error when encountering Simscape Multibody blocks with multidimensional states
Description: The Solver Profiler does not count integration failures correctly when encountering Simscape Multibody blocks with multidimensional states, and throws the following error: Error while evaluating TimerFcn for timer 'timer-1'?  Subscript indices must either be real positive integers or logicals.

Summary: Cannot open reference applications from Simulink Start Page Examples tab
Description: You cannot open the Powertrain Blockset reference applications from the Simulink Start Page Examples tab.?
Workaround: To open the reference applications, at the MATLAB command line, you can type:autoblkConVehStart (opens?Conventional Vehicle Reference Application)autoblkHevVehStart (opens?Hybrid Electric Vehicle Multimode Reference Application)autoblkHevIPSVehStart?(opens Hybrid Electric Vehicle Input Power-Split Reference Application)autoblkEvVehStart (opens Electric Vehicle Reference Application)autoblkCIDynamometerStart (opens CI Engine Dynamometer Reference Application)autoblkSIDynamometerStart?(opens SI Engine Dynamometer Reference Application)

Summary: Simple Heat Exchanger (G-TL) block ignores changes to initial conditions of gas
Description: The initial conditions of the gas volume in the Simple Heat Exchanger (G-TL) component are internally set to their default values at the start of each simulation. Changes to these conditions, made through the Gas 1 initial temperature  and Gas 1 initial pressure  block parameters in the Effects and Initial Conditions  tab, are ignored.

Summary: Standardization of edge case behavior for?log,?log2,?log10,?and?log1p
Description: In R2017b there is consistent handling for real values stored in a complex array for log ,  log2 ,  log10 ,  and log1p . In previous releases, the answer could change depending on whether you use a loop or a vectorized method. For example, calculating logarithm values in a loop produces?y1 : >> x = complex(-1, [1, 0, -0]).'x =? -1.0000 + 1.0000i? -1.0000 + 0.0000i? -1.0000 + 0.0000i >> y1 = zeros(3,1);>> for i = 1:numel(x);      y1(i) = log(x(i));    end;>> y1y1 =?? 0.3466 + 2.3562i?? 0.0000 + 3.1416i?? 0.0000 + 3.1416i For element-wise functions, vectorization of a for-loop should produce identical answers. However, in releases R2017a and earlier this is not true for  log ,  log2 ,  log10 ,  and log1p , since the sign from  -0  in the creation of  x  propagates to the output:  >> y2 = log(x)y2 =?? 0.3466 + 2.3562i?? 0.0000 + 3.1416i?? 0.0000 - 3.1416i  In R2017b, the values in y2   match those of y1 .?

Summary: Copy and paste of Model block in AUTOSAR composition model might generate access violation
Description: In a Simulink model configured as an AUTOSAR composition, under certain conditions, copying and pasting a Model block generates an access violation error message.
Workaround: To work around this bug, instead of copying and pasting a Model block, add a new Model block to the AUTOSAR composition model.? You can use quick insert or drag a Model block from the Simulink Library browser.

Summary: Spectrum Analyzer crashes MATLAB in filter bank mode
Description: When you use the Spectrum Analyzer block or the?dsp.SpectrumAnalyzer   System object, MATLAB crashes if you use the following settings together:? Method ?property set to 'Filter bank'  FrequencySpan ?property set to either?'Start and stop frequency'  or  'Span and center frequency'

Summary: Replacing a table or content placeholder corrupts PowerPoint presentation
Description: Replacing a table or content placeholder might corrupt a PowerPoint presentation, causing PowerPoint to display an error when it tries to open the file.

Description: When you configure FIR Interpolation block to implement multipliers as CSD or Factored CSD, and the multiplier input or output pipeline settings are not zero, the generated HDL code is missing a declaration for a vector signal. This results in errors during compilation in an HDL compiler and will result in a synthesis error as well.

Description: If you select thousands of?model elements in the Simulink Editor and then click the Analysis menu, Simulink appears to stop responding. However, the system will eventually respond.
Workaround: Clear your selection?before?clicking the Analysis menu.

Summary: State variable for Unit Delay incorrectly overwritten for a subsystem whose function interface includes arguments
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? Incorrect code generation might occur when all of the following conditions are true: Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results ?or?Minimize global data access  Reuse local block outputs ?configuration parameter is enabled. The model contains a subsystem in which the?Function packaging ?parameter set to?NonReusable?and Function interface ?is set to  Allow Arguments.   A Unit Delay block's input or output is connected to that subsystem's output signal.  Errors might be thrown during the build process.?
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Description: Using the?signalbuilder  function to delete signals from a?Signal Builder block in a loaded Simulink model unnecessarily forces the Simulink model to?open.

Summary: U1052?build error when using Visual Studio 2017 toolchain
Description: When you try to build the generated code from a model using the Microsoft Visual Studio 2017 toolchain, and either "VSCMD_START_DIR" is defined in the environment or?the "%USERPROFILE%\Source" directory exists, Simulink reports a build failure with the following error message: ?NMAKE : fatal error U1052: file '<model_name>.mk' not found?  See?Bug Report ID 1686741 ?for a similar issue when building generated code from a System Target File that uses the template makefile approach, such as Simuilink Real-Time.?

Summary: Model block with periodic event ports does not update its states when simulating in accelerator mode
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it meets the following conditions: Your model contains a Model block which references another model containing blocks with states such as a Unit Delay block. The Model block has the?'Show model periodic event ports' parameter selected. The Model block or its parent model?has simulation mode set to 'Accelerator'.  When all of the above conditions are met, the states in the referenced model remain fixed at their initial values, leading to an incorrect simulation result.

Summary: Some AUTOSAR examples fail to open
Description: MATLAB is unable to?open some examples from the Embedded Coder Support Package for AUTOSAR Standard, and displays an error in the command window about missing supporting files.

Summary: mldivide operator A\b can be very slow when A is a sparse symmetric indefinite matrix
Description: The function call?mldivide(A,b) ,where A  is a sparse symmetric indefinite matrix, does not use an approximate minimum degree permutation (see function amd ) as it should. Furthermore, if the function call spparms('autoamd',tf)  is used, then the value of tf  has the opposite of the documented effect for a sparse symmetric indefinite matrix A .
Workaround: To avoid the slowdown caused by this bug, you can change a global option with spparms prior to solving A\b. This ensures that a sparse non-symmetric solver (provided by UMFPACK) is used for sparse symmetric indefinite matrices, instead of MA57.spparms('usema57',0);A\b

Summary: HDL code generation might fail for models with Switch blocks inside reference models
Description: HDL code generation might fail and emit this error message under certain circumstances when the model contains Switch blocks inside referenced models: ?Invalid Simulink object name: <modelname>/<SwitchBlockNameInport>

Output port 1 of '<path_to_streaming_partition_subsystem_in_generated_model>' is a one dimensional vector with 1 elements. ? The error occurs because the streaming optimization serializes the Constant block and outputs a scalar value. Summary: Merges error or never finish for models that contain Simscape connection lines
Description: If you try to merge a Simscape connection line using the Simulink two-way or three-way merge tools, you see an error (in R2011a - R2016a) or enter an infinite loop (R2016b - R2017b).? For Simulink three way merge this can mean the merge tool will not finish the initial comparison. In releases R2011a - R2016a, you see an error like the following: ? Could not merge line [...] because block [...] does not have port 'connection'. Try merging blocks before merging lines
Workaround: In releases R2016b - R2017b, you can press CTRL+C in the MATLAB Command Window to exit the infinite loop. You can then continue using the merge tool, as long as you avoid trying to merge any Simscape lines. You cannot merge Simscape lines.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when Stateflow States with requirement annotations are aligned vertically
Description: MATLAB crashes when two Stateflow States are moved into vertical alignment in the canvas if the following conditions are met: Requirements perspective is active Both of the States have a linked requirement A requirement annotation is shown on the canvas for at least one of the States

Summary: Possible MATLAB?crash for MEX?with JIT compilation
Description: In some cases, if?you run a MEX function that was generated with just-in-time (JIT) compilation, MATLAB?crashes with an assertion failure like: Assertion `isa<X>(Val) && "cast<Ty>() argument of incompatible type!"' failed.?
Workaround: Disable JIT compilation for MEX function generation by using one of these methods:At the command line, set the EnableJIT configuration parameter to?false. In the MATLAB Coder app,?on the Generate page, clear the Use JIT compilation?check box.??

Summary: Parallel build of model with non-default MakeCommand parameter value?might fail
Description: A parallel build of a model with a non-default MakeCommand  parameter value might fail with the error: 'Struct contents reference from a non-struct array object'

Summary: Memory overhead when running simulations with signal logging on a cluster using MATLAB Distributed Computing Server
Description: Running parallel simulations that use signal logging on a cluster using MATLAB Distributed Computer Server will result in increased memory usage on the client MATLAB when using interactive pools. The memory overhead depends on the amount of data logged, number of simulations and number of workers used. This might result in performance degradation and MATLAB crashes depending on the client machine's system resources. This also affects parallel simulations run using PARSIM.
Workaround: After loading Simulink and before running the PARSIM command or starting your parallel pool, run the following command:>> Simulink.sdi.enablePCTSupport(false)

Summary: Data Store?Memory blocks do not simulate correctly or generate code when inheriting sample time
Description: During sample time propagation, it is assumed that Data Store Memory blocks with an inherited sample time specification receives a correct sample time from a Simulink.Signal   object. However, when a Simulink.Signal  object does not exist or has an inherited sample time specification, the Data Store Memory blocks and their corresponding reader and writer blocks?are not given a sample time. As a result, these blocks do not simulate correctly and MATLAB will crash when you try to generate code for the model.

Summary: AUTOSAR model fails to generate?Rte?API calls to write vector type values to root outport
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model fails to generate?Rte ?write API calls if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to?ExplicitSend . The model is configured to write a vector type value to the root outport. The root outport is fed by a Merge block.  Under these conditions,?Rte ?API calls to write vector type values to the root outport are not created in the generated C code.

Summary: Unexpected project root entry in Simulink Project modified files view
Description: If the project root has an SVN property modification, then the project root is shown in the modified files view when in list mode.?This project root folder contains a entry which also corresponds to the project root. You can ignore this folder, and you do not see it in tree view mode.
Workaround: You can ignore the folder entry in the list of modified files. You do not see the folder in the default tree view mode for the modified files view, so you can use the tree view mode to view other modified files.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for AUTOSAR target when an S-function sets?ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW?to?TRUE
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for a model when all these conditions are met:? The system target file is set to AUTOSAR.tlc, AND The system has an S-function with the ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW(SimStruct *S, int idx, bool val)  function having the bool  argument val set to 1 (TRUE)? for at least one Outport block, AND The S-function must be connected to a root input port.  In the generated code, the input variable for the S-Function may be incorrectly reused.
Workaround: There are three workarounds, each of which will have impact on code efficiency (increasing stack size):Disable the configuration parameter Reuse local block outputs.?Set the bool?argument of ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW to 0 (FALSE) for all the identified S-function blocks.??Insert a Signal Conversion block at the input of all affected S-Function blocks that set the bool argument of ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW to 1 (TRUE). Enable the parameter?Excludethis block from ˇ®Block reductionˇŻ optimization on the Block Parameters?dialog box?for each insertedSignal Conversion block. Regenerate and inspect the code to confirm that theproblem is averted.?
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 6, R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 6, R2018a

Summary: TLC parameter with initial value of zero does not appear in Configuration Parameters dialog box

Workaround: Before opening the Configuration Parameters dialog box, temporarily change the parameter to a nonzero value.

Summary: AUTOSAR model fails to generate Rte?API calls to write vector type values to root outport
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model fails to generate Rte ?write API calls if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to?ExplicitSend . Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a MATLAB Function block that has a pair of in-place variables and has at least one other input or output variable.
Description: Incorrect generated code might occur for a MATLAB Function block that has a pair of in-place variables (that is, specifies the same variable name for a pair of input and output variables) and has at least one other input or output variable. For example, this bug could occur for a MATLAB Function block that has this code: [x, y] = foo(x)  or  x?= foo(x,z).  When this bug occurs, y and x in the first example or z and x in the second example could be wrongly merged in the generated code.

Workaround: You can avoid this crash by setting the storage class of AUTOSAR.Signal objects to a value other than Auto such as SimulinkGlobal. ?However, this change will likely reduce the efficiency of the generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect VHDL code may be generated when a branched signal is later merged using a Mux block, Vector Concatenate block, or a For Each Subsystem
Description: If the original Simulink model contains a branching signal (a signal which drives 2 or more blocks), and those signals are later recombined using a Mux block, HDL Coder can generate incorrect code. There are several conditions which must be met to trigger this bug:? Target Language must be VHDL Native Floating Point technology must be turned on  The branching signal must have a fixed-point or integer type At least one of the branches of the signal must lead to a Subsystem Downstream, the branches must be re-combined using a Mux or Vector Concatenate block.  This bug can also occur using a For Each Subsystem, which contains a Mux block implicitly at the output. In this case, there are similar conditions to trigger the bug: Target Language must be VHDL Native Floating Point technology must be turned?on  A single signal must drive 2 or more inputs of a For Each Subsystem At least one of those inputs must be non-partitioned by the For Each Subsystem This signal must have a fixed-point or integer complex ?type  In both cases, this bug leads to VHDL code that contains uninitialized values, resulting in wrong answers from the generated code.

Summary: Some parameters are not automatically updated in the Configuration Parameters dialog box when they are changed externally
Description: If the Configuration Parameters dialog box is open and you modify the value of any of the parameters listed below using set_param   command, the Model Explorer, or any other means outside of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the values shown in the Configuration Parameters dialog box?might not be automatically updated. ?Affected parameters available with the Simulink license:? DefaultUnderspecifiedDataType EnableMultiTasking ExtrapolationOrder MaxOrder ModelReferencePassRootInputsByReference SampleTimeProperty SimReservedNameArray Solver SolverJacobianMethodControl SolverType StrictBusMsg UseDivisionForNetSlopeComputation ZeroCrossAlgorithm ZeroCrossControl  Affected parameters?available with?a Simulink Coder license: CodeInterfacePackaging CodeReplacementLibrary EnableMemcpy ExtModeTransport GenerateReport MaxStackSize ObjectivePriorities ReservedNameArray RTWUseSimCustomCode SupportNonFinite UseSimReservedNames  Affected parameters?available with?an Embedded Coder license:? CodeCoverageSettings PurelyIntegerCode ZeroExtrernalMemoryAtStartup ZeroInternalMemoryAtStartup?
Workaround: Close the Configuration Parameters dialog box and reopen it to?display the current parameter values.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable functions generated from subsystems connected to root inports with different bus types can?get reused?incorrectly in generated?code
Description: If you?configure multiple atomic subsystems?to use the same reusable function for code generation (Function packaging  set to?Auto , Reuseable?function ) and the subsystems are connected to root inports that have different bus types that have some overlapping elements, the code generator?might produce one function definition and a call to that function for each subsystem. The code that results can produce silent wrong answers.  The code generator should produce a separate function definition for each subsystem for which the inport data type varies.
Workaround: For?atomic subsystems that are connected to inports that are buses with different data types, set the subsystem block parameter Function packaging to Inline.?Although the code size increases, the code generator inlines the correct function body for each subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect counter input measurement when channel Name is set
Description: When using a session containing counter input channels from National Instruments DAQ devices, setting the?Name  property for any of?these channels results in incorrect readings returned by inputSingleScan . ?
Workaround: Two possible workarounds?are:Avoid setting the Name property for counter input channels?orUse the startForeground command instead of inputSingleScan.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Semaphore protection for rate transition buffer access might be incorrect in generated code
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect semaphore protection logics for a rate transition buffer when all of the following conditions are met:  The transition rate is from fast to slow. On the Rate Transition block parameters dialog box, the Ensure data integrity during data transfer  parameter is selected. On the Rate Transition block parameters dialog box, the Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  parameter is not selected.  On the All Parameters  tab of the Configuration Parameters dialog box,  the Allow tasks to execute concurrently on target  parameter is not selected. The output buffer of the Rate Transition block is local variable in the generated code due to an optimization or because the configuration parameter?Optimize global data access  is set to Minimize global data access.   ?This issue does not affect single-tasking execution.
Workaround: Executing the generated code in single-tasking mode does not lead to incorrect answer. To execute in single-tasking mode, clear the model configuration parameter Treat each discrete rate as a separate task.

Summary: 'Executable not found' error occrus?when running parallel simulations using parsim with?RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck set to 'off'
Description: Parallel simulations can be?run by creating an array of Simulink.SimulationInput  objects and passing them as an input argument to the parsim  command. However, if ?the model is set to run in rapid accelerator mode and any of the Simulink.SimulationInput  objects have RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck? parameter set to 'off' , those simulations error out with an 'Executable not found ' error as can be seen on the ErrorMessage ?property of the returned Simulink.SimulationOutput  object. The error will not  be encountered if each of the parallel workers builds the rapid accelerator target as shown in the following example? ?https://www.mathworks.com/help/simulink/examples/rapid-accelerator-simulations-using-parsim.html ??

The?Initialize output (Y) ?parameter is set to?Specify size for each dimension in table.  In the Unit?Delay block parameters dialog box, the?Initial condition ?parameter has initial values.

Summary: Relational Boundary coverage not recorded for some Stateflow states/transitions with multiple sections
Description: Relational Boundary coverage objectives might not be recorded for Stateflow states and transitions which contain multiple sections. As a result, the total number of Relational Boundary objectives reported for the Stateflow Chart will be less than it should be. This affects states containing two or more of the following sections: entry during exit?  This affects transitions with a condition as well as one or more of the following sections:? condition action transition action comments?  Note that this issue only affects Stateflow Charts set to use MATLAB Action Language. Stateflow C Charts are not affected.
Workaround: There are no workarounds for affected states.For affected transitions, this issue can be worked around by removing comments and splitting up multi-section transitions into a series of separate transitions, as necessary.For example, consider a transition which has a condition (containing a relational operation) as well as an action.[in1 < in2] {out = 1;}? ?To ensure that this relational operation is analyzed for Relational Boundary coverage, split this into two separate transitions connected in series.[in1 < in2]?{out = 1;}??This configuration is functionally equivalent, but ensures that the transition containing the condition does not have any other sections.??

Summary: On macOS 10.13 (High Sierra), MATLAB default character encoding is incorrect for many locales
Description: On macOS?10.13 (High Sierra), the default character encoding in MATLAB is incorrect for many locales. Character data created or loaded into MATLAB and re-saved might be corrupted in the process. MATLAB files containing non-ASCII characters might not execute as expected. Examples of affected characters include non-Latin characters, accented characters, mathematical symbols, trademark symbols, and monetary characters such as the Euro symbol. The following language settings are compatible with macOS 10.13 (High Sierra) and no further action is required: English Simplified Chinese Japanese Korean  If your language setting is not listed above, please refer to the Workaround section.?
Workaround: For MATLAB R2015a and prior releases, please contact Technical Support.?Installation Instructions for MATLAB R2015b through R2017aTo install this patch, you need the username and password of an account with Admin privileges.Download the appropriate attachment for the version of MATLAB you wish to patch.Look for the file where your web browser saves downloaded files.If your web browser did not unzip the patch file for you, double-click the file in the Finder to unzip it.Double-click the Disk Image (.dmg?file) to mount and open the disk image.At the top level of the mounted disk image, double-click the?R20XXx_patch_1621180.app?to launch the installer.Locate and select your MATLAB installation (for example,?MATLAB_R20XXx.app.), and click?Choose.?Click?OK?to confirm the release of MATLAB you are patching.Enter the name and password of an account with Admin privileges.When the upgrade is complete, you can launch MATLAB.?

Summary: Using multiple channels on Measurement Computing devices can result in incorrect rate limits
Description: Adding more than one analog output channel from a Measurement Computing DAQ device?to a session will result in an incorrect maximum rate limit. For example, the maximum specified rate for a USB 1608GX-2AO device is 500 kS/s. Adding two analog output channels from this device to a session, and then setting the session rate to 500000, results in the following error:? >> s=daq.createSession('mcc')>> addAnalogOutputChannel(s, 'Board0', 0:1, 'Voltage')>> s.Rate = 500000;Rate cannot exceed 250000 in the current configuration.>> s.RateLimitans =           1      250000  The upper RateLimit  value should have remained at 500000 after adding the two channels.?
Workaround: For MATLAB R2017a, a fix is provided as an update to "Data Acquisition Toolbox Support Package for Measurement Computing Hardware" (version 17.1.1).Update this support package to the latest version.To check for updates to support packages, on the MATLAB? Home tab, in the Environment section, click Add-Ons > Check for Updates > Hardware Support Packages.

Summary: Slide lacks background graphics
Description: The background graphics?defined by a slide's layout can fail to appear in slides based on the layout. This occurs when the slide's master also defines background graphics?and?the layout's Hide Background Graphics  option is enabled in the PowerPoint editor. In this case, neither the master nor the layout graphics appear in the slide based on the layout.
Workaround: Remove the background from the slide master and define slide backgrounds in the slide layouts.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code is not regenerated for referenced models with data dictionaries when custom storage classes change
Description: If there are changes to resources that a referenced model depends on, the code is automatically regenerated. For example, if a model depends on data objects in the base workspace, the code is regenerated when?custom storage class definitions for a corresponding package change. However, for models that use data objects in data dictionaries, code is not automatically regenerated when custom storage class definitions change.
Workaround: If you make significant changes to your build environment, such as changing the definition of custom storage classes, delete your slprj directory and regenerate code for the whole model reference hierarchy.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing or incorrect function call in generated code for Reset Function block inside nested export function models
Description: When all of the following conditions are true, the function <parent>_<reset>() in the generated code for the parent model might not call the function <child>_<reset>() in the generated code for the referenced model or might call the wrong function: The model is an export function model The model contains a Model block referencing another export function model The referenced export function model contains a Reset Function block  where <parent> is name of the parent export function model <child> is the name of the referenced export function model <reset> is the identifier for the Reset Function block in the referenced model

Summary: Moving Simulink lines can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Moving lines that connect to branched signals?or connecting a port to a signal can cause?MATLAB to crash. The crash sometimes occurs during the next operation after?moving or creating?the segment.

Summary: ?Inefficient code produced for structure initialization?
Description: For MATLAB code containing nested structure initialization code that meets all  of these criteria, an optimization inadvertently duplicates the function call for every field in the structure: A function returns a substructure. Typically, such a function is written to initialize a structure. The function has no visible effect other than its return value. The resulting structure is assigned as a field into another structure.  This situation typically results in many calls to the initialization function, and many temporary variables. This bug?can cause inefficient generated code. It does not lead to wrong answers.
Workaround: ?To prevent the optimization from duplicating calls, make it appear that the function calls custom C code. Create the following function on the path:?function fakeSideEffect    coder.ceval('(void)',1);?end?Then, add a call to this function from the bottom-most structure initialization function.?
