Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR model with reusable code interface packaging generates incorrect argument types to Rte function calls
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model with schema version 3.x  generates an incorrect argument type in an Rte_Read  function call if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root inport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to ExplicitReceive . The model reads a vector type value to the root inport. Model configuration parameter Code interface packaging ?is set to Reusable function .  Under these conditions, in the generated C code, Rte_Read  function calls to read vector type values contain incorrect data types. Building an AUTOSAR model with schema version 4.x  generates an incorrect argument type in an Rte_IWrite  function call if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to ImplicitSend . The model writes a vector type value to the root outport. Model configuration parameter Code interface packaging ?is set to Reusable function .  Under these conditions, in the generated C code, Rte_IWrite  function calls to write vector type values contain incorrect instance types.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rate Transition block may generate incorrect code for the use in multitasking environment
Description: The Rate Transition block may generate incorrect code for the use in multitasking environment, when all of the following conditions are met:-The generated code for the Rate Transition block and/or the immediate surrounding logics?is in one fused?for  loop rather than in separate for  loops. -The loop fusion optimization is not disabled in effect as a part of certain code optimization configuration. -The Rate Transition block has these?conditions:? The input sample time of the Rate Transition block is?faster than the output sample time. The output sample time?is a multiple of the input sample time. The output of the Rate Transition block must connect to an output port directly. In the Rate Transition block parameters dialog box, the?Ensure data integrity during data transfer? parameter is selected. In the Rate Transition block?parameters dialog box, the?Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)? parameter is not selected. The Rate Transition block displays?buf ?on the block icon.  -If you select?Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer? and?Whenever Possible? or?Never (minimum delay) ?for?Deterministic data transfer? on the?Solver ?pane in?the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Rate Transition badge displays ?buf ?on the badge. When these conditions are met, the Rate Transition block input and output code may be incorrectly moved out of their semaphore protection context in the generated code. For?example this is the?incorrect code?pattern:? ??? RateTransition_semaphoreTaken = 1;??? RateTransition_semaphoreTaken = 0;??? for (i = 0; i < 25; i++) {????? Y.Out[i] = RateTransition_Buffer0[i];??? } This is the correct code pattern:? ??? RateTransition_semaphoreTaken = 1;??? for (i = 0; i < 25; i++) {????? rtb_RateTransition[i] = RateTransition_Buffer0[i];??? }??? RateTransition_semaphoreTaken = 0;??? for (i = 0; i < 25; i++) {????? Y.Out[i] = rtb_RateTransition[i];??? } In the correct code pattern, the read access of? RateTransition_Buffer0 is protected.
Workaround: Create an atomic subsystem and put the Rate Transition block in it.?Set the?subsystem Function packaging parameter to Nonreusable.

Summary: DOM API does not honor both height and width dimensions for an image in PDF output
Description: If you specify both Height  and Width  dimensions for an Image , these dimensions are not honored in the PDF output. For example, the image generated with the below script will not have the specified dimensions. import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('MyReport', 'pdf');open(d);append(d, Paragraph('Image should have 1in width and 5in height'));image = append(d, Image(which('ngc6543a.jpg')));% specifying Image Width to be 1inimage.Width = '1in';% specifying Image Height to be 5inimage.Height = '5in';close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);
Workaround: Specify the scaling to be nonuniform for the Image, for exampleimport mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('MyReport', 'pdf');open(d);append(d, Paragraph('Image should have 1in width and 5in height'));image = append(d, Image(which('ngc6543a.jpg')));% specifying Image Width to be 1inimage.Width = '1in';% specifying Image Height to be 5inimage.Height = '5in';% specifying the scaling to be non-uniformimage.Style = [image.Style {FOProperties(FOProperty('scaling', 'non-uniform'))}];close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB global variables not synchronized between MATLAB and MEX function during Check for Run-Time Issues step of MATLAB Coder app
Description: In the?Check for Run-Time Issues  step of the MATLAB Coder app, MATLAB global variable values are not synchronized between MATLAB and the generated test MEX function. As a result, the global variables can have unexpected values in the generated code causing the Check for Run-Time Issues ?step to have?unexpected behavior.
Workaround: This issue only occurs on the?Check for Run-Time Issues step of the MATLAB Coder app. To work around the issue, advance to the Generate Code?step and choose MEX?in the Build Type?setting. Generate the MEX file and click the VERIFY CODE?button in the top bar to run the generated MEX file.

Summary: Simulink models that contain a Simulink Test test harness with Signal Builder as source do not load
Description: Simulink models that contain a Simulink Test test harness with Signal Builder specified as the source do not load?when MATLAB is running in -nodisplay ?mode. ?The error occurs even?if you do not load or open the harness.
Workaround: To work around this issue, either:?Use sltest.harness.delete to delete the harnesses that have Signal Builder sources. Then?save the model to a new name.??Export the harnesses that have Signal Builder sources using sltest.harness.export.? This will delete the test harness from the model, but retain the harness as an external file.??

Summary: ASAP2 file generation error for model that uses Simulink dual-scaled parameters
Description: If you generate ASAP2 files for a model that uses Simulink dual-scaled parameters, code generation might fail with a message similar to one of the following: Error: File: <MATLAB>\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc Line: 3127 Column: 78Attempt to divide by zero Error: File: <MATLAB>\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc Line: 1767 Column: 37The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: False error about enabled subsystem being multi-rate when outport block is driven by Stateflow Chart
Description: An error is seen when all the following conditions are true:  An enabled subsystem outport block has its Initial output  parameter set to ' []' ?and Source when disabled ?parameter set to ' held' .  This outport block is driven by a Stateflow Chart whose output has an explicit initial value.  The enabled subsystem does not have multiple rates, but does contain a Constant block with sample time parameter set to ' Inf' .   The error is incorrect because the enabled subsystem is being incorrectly flagged as a multi-rate subsystem. Such a modeling pattern should not lead to any errors.  The error seen is as follows:  Outport block 'model/Enabled Subsystem/Outport' is inheriting its initial output value from output port 1 of Stateflow block 'model/Enabled Subsystem/Chart' in the multi-rate enabled subsystem 'model/Enabled Subsystem'. Consider one of the following modifications:  (1) Change 'States when enabling' parameter of Enable Port block 'model/Enabled Subsystem/Enable' to 'held'. (2) Make the enabled subsystem single-rate.   (3) For the Outport block, set the parameter 'Source of initial output value:' to 'Dialog' and ensure that the parameter 'Initial output' is not empty ([]).  (4) Insert a Signal Conversion block before the Outport block.

Summary: In simulation normal mode, blocks that use theelapsed sample time (such as Discrete-Time Integrator or Weighted Sample Time Math) behave incorrectly?in a triggered function running at?sample time?with non-zero offset
Description: Blocks that use the elapsed sample time (such as Discrete-Time Integrator or Weighted Sample Time Math or SFunction block uses ssGetElapseTime) behave incorrectly in?simulation normal mode if all  of these conditions exist:? The block is inside a triggered or function-call subsystem.? The block uses one of the?integration modes (not a accumulator mode). The Sample time?type ?parameter at trigger port?is set to triggered .?? The subsystem?runs in a sample time with non-zero offset. ? The sample time offset is 50% of the?sample time?period. ?
Workaround: To avoid this problem, use one or both of these workarounds:At the trigger port, change Sample time type to periodic.Avoid using?sample time offset that is 50% of?the sample time period. ?

Summary: cd command fails?to change to a One Drive directory
Description: When running MATLAB on a PC with the Windows 10 Fall Creators Update, the cd  command might?fail?to change the directory to your One Drive directory: Error using cd    Cannot CD to C:\Users\<user_name>\OneDrive (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a MATLAB function block in which a variable is written to in and after a for loop
Description: Incorrect code may occur for the following code pattern in a MATLAB function block: A variable A  is written to in a for  loop.? The variable A  is copied from variable B  right after the same for  loop. The variable B  is written before the for  loop.  For the above code pattern, the copy to variable A  after the for  loop may be incorrectly optimized away in the generated C code. For example, this C code is correct for the above code pattern ? B = ...  ? ...  ? for (P1 = 0; P1 < 9; P1++) {  ? ? for (P5 = 0; P5 < 5; P5++) {  ? ? ? A = rtb_mpswitch[35] * 5.0;  ? ? }  ? }  ?A = B;  This C code contains the bug for the above code pattern. A = ...  ...  for (P1 = 0; P1 < 9; P1++) {  ? for (P5 = 0; P5 < 5; P5++) {  ? ? A = rtb_mpswitch[35] * 5.0;  ? }  }?
Workaround: Unzip the attachment and save the workaroundForBugReport1609506.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: Simulating a model in Accelerator mode will crash MATLAB when simulation artifacts from a previous release are present
Description: Simulating a model in Accelerator mode in R2017b or later releases will crash MATLAB if the slprj/accel folder generated in R2016b or R2017a is present. The expected behavior is to prompt the user to delete the slprj folder generated in another release. This only affects top model simulation in accelerator mode. Referenced models simulated in Accelerator mode are unaffected.
Workaround: Delete the slprj folder or slprj/accel folder generated in R2016b or R2017a.

Summary: Word document with shapes in header or footer fails to open??
Description: A Microsoft Word .docx ? document, based on a DOCX  template, that contains a shape object in a header or a footer fails to open. This occurs if the report generator program added DOM Image objects to the Document object.

Summary: Duplicate signals in test results when running in SIL mode
Description: If you have code execution profiling enabled in your model and you run a test in SIL mode, duplicate signals are created in the test results.
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: Simulink stops responding when dragging a block from Library Browser
Description: If both File > Print > Enable Tiled Printing  and File > Print > Show Page Boundaries ?are enabled, dragging a block from the Library Browser onto the model canvas causes?Simulink to stop responding.

Summary: Custom EDA script commands specified through the CLI not reflected in ModelSim script
Description: When the  SimulationTool ? property is set to 'Mentor Graphics ModelSim' , the simulation and compilation scripts do not reflect custom EDA script commands specified using the CLI.
Workaround: Set the SimulationTool property to 'Custom'.

Summary: Memory section used for a fixed point utility function declaration may differ from the memory section used for its definition
Description: If a model sets Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface > Shared code placement  to Auto , the memory section used for a fixed point utility function declaration may differ from the memory section used for its definition. The declaration may use the Execution memory section, while the definition may use the Shared utility memory section.
Workaround: Set Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface > Shared code placement to Shared location.

Summary: Incorrect initialization in Normal simulation mode for Model block with model initialize port referencing a model containing blocks with constant sample time
Description: Simulating a model in Normal mode may give incorrect results under the following conditions: The model contains a Model block with parameter Show model initialize port  selected and parameter Simulation mode ?set to Normal . The referenced model contains blocks with constant sample time. (To check whether this is the case, while viewing the referenced model, enable display of Sample Time Legend by typing Ctrl+J. This will trigger a diagram update. If you see a sample time entry with the description "Constant," then your referenced model has such blocks.)  Under these conditions, the blocks with constant sample time may not be initialized correctly when calling the initialize port on the Model block, which is a function-call input port, and continue to output the value zero after initialization.

Summary: Expected output may not be generated after Simulink Design Verifier analysis with data store logging?enabled
Description: When running Simulink Design Verifier analysis with?Include expected output values  enabled,?the simulation output values are not included in the below SLDV results if?Configuration Parameters > Data Import/Export > Save to workspace or file > Data stores? is enabled. Generated MAT-file that contains sldvData structure Simulink Design Verifier report

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models referenced multiple times in normal mode might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: Simulink might produce incorrect results when you run a model for which all of the following conditions are true: A model hierarchy references a model multiple times in normal mode The referenced model has callbacks that execute during update diagram Those callbacks modify the referenced model Those callbacks make decisions based on the name of the referenced model

Summary: In the Test Sequence Editor, deleting and undo-deleting a step breaks incoming requirement links
Description: In the Test Sequence Editor, if you perform these actions, the incoming requirement link will be broken (for example, following the link from the other block causes an error).Add a new step. Create an incoming requirement link to the new step (for example, from another block). Delete the new step. Undo-delete the new step.
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: After importing an existing test result, signals cannot be plotted in Test Manager
Description: If you import an existing test result into Test Manager and then try to plot a signal, the signal does not get plotted.
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: After deleting a library instance that has internally logged signals, logging badges appear on other signals
Description: If you delete an instance of a library that includes logged signals, logging badges can appear on signals in the top level of your model that were not previously logged.?
Workaround: Remove the logging badges from the signals within the library instance before you delete the library instance. To stop logging signals, select the logged signals and then click Stop Logging Selected Signals?from the Simulation Data Inspector drop-down menu on the Simulink editor toolbar.?

Summary: In generated code, order of assignments to Stateflow outputs might change without any model changes
Description: The order of assignments to Stateflow chart output variables in the generated code might change from one build to the next, even if there are no changes made in the chart itself.
Workaround: See?Bug Report ID 1580490?for a workaround for this and another issue in R2017a.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Merge block generates global variable output in a Simulink model with Inline Variants
Description: Presence of an Inline Variant block (Variant Sink or Variant Source) anywhere in the model hierarchy causes output of a merge block to be global.  This behavior is independent of Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditional ? parameter option.  Example of generated code:  B_repro_T repro_B;  /* Model step function */  void repro_step(void)  {  ? switch (repro_U.In2)  {  ? ? case 1:  ? ? repro_B.Merge = repro_P.Constant_Value;  ? ? break;  ? ? case 2:  ? ? repro_B.Merge = repro_P.Constant1_Value;  ? ? break;  ? ? default:  ? ? repro_B.Merge = repro_P.Constant2_Value;  ? ? break;  ? }  repro_Y.Out2 = repro_B.Merge;  }

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Store Memory block or?block input or output?accessed across different sample times may lead to data integrity issue in the generated code
Description: ?A model that meets all of these conditions may?result incorrect generated code: The model contains a Data Store Memory block?or block?input and output?accessed across different rates. The model configuration parameter Optimize Global Data Access  is set to Use global to store temporary results  value.  In the generated code, the the global variable corresponding to the DSM or block IO may be incorrectly reused and cause the access from the slower rate to receive incorrect values.

Summary: rptview??returns an error?in a compiled application
Description: rptview  returns an error and does not open the generated report when used in a compiled application.
Workaround: Use this function to open a report in a compiled application instead:?function viewrpt(fullFileName)if (ispc) winopen(fullFileName);elseif ismac ? ?cmdToExecute = ['open ' fullFileName]; ? ?[status path] = system(cmdToExecute);else ? ?% using xdg-open to open a file in Linux. Some Linux systems might not have ? ?% xdg-open .In that case displaying as error with the file path ? ?cmdToExecute = ['xdg-open ' fullFileName]; ? ?[status path] = system(cmdToExecute);endend

Summary: Wrong answer when a subsystem containing an unconnected output is placed between a Variant Source and an Outport block
Description: Wrong simulation and code generation results are seen when all  the following conditions are true: An atomic or virtual subsystem block is driven by a Variant Source block. The subsystem contains an Inport block directly connected to an Outport block. The subsystem also contains an unconnected Outport block. The output port of the subsystem corresponding to the direct connection between the Inport block and the Outport block is connected to an Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem.

Summary: False colors for standard library string manipulation functions strncpy, strncat, strncmp or strndup
Description: In very specific situations, Polyspace Code Prover can produce incorrect colors for standard library functions related to strings.? Example1   : ? The? Invalid use of standard library routine ?(STD_LIB )?check on the call to  strncmp  is green. The check is supposed to be red because the last cell  s[2]  is not initialized?.  ?char s[3];?char s2[3]="ab";?s[0] = 'a';?s[1] = 'b';?strncmp(s2, s, 3);?  Example 2:?   The? Invalid use of standard library routine ?(STD_LIB )?check on the call to? strncmp? is red because the second argument is NULL.? The check is supposed to be green because the number of bytes compared is also 0. The situation in this code example is not likely to happen in practice.? ?char s2[3]="ab";?strncmp(s2, NULL, 0);?  Example 3  :  A ?red Non-terminating call ?occurs?because s  is not NULL-terminated. However, the check is not supposed to appear because the number of bytes compared is also 0. The situation in this code example is not likely to happen in practice.? ?char s[3];?char s2[3]="ab";?s[0] = 'a';?s[1] = 'b';?strncmp(s2, s + 1, 0);?

Summary: Simulink might crash during code generation for models that allow tasks to execute concurrently on a target
Description: Generating code for models that allow tasks to execute concurrently on a target may produce a crash if the model uses a global datastore and the name of the datastore is longer than 16 characters.
Workaround: Change the name of the datastore to something shorter than 16 characters.

Summary: Cannot specify both PDF?table width and column specification group
Description: Specifying both a table width and a column specification group, without specifying the group's Span  property, in a PDF report causes a Java error to appear at the MATLAB command line. For example, this script causes a Java error. import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('MyReport','pdf');% Span property is not specified for the groupgroups(1) = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpecGroup;groups(1).Style = {Color('red'), Border('solid') };tbl = append(rpt, Table(magic(5)));tbl.ColSpecGroups = groups;tbl.Width = '5in';close(rpt);
Workaround: Specify the Span property for the TableColSpecGroup, for exampleimport mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('MyReport','pdf');groups(1) = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpecGroup;% Specifying the Span property for the groupgroups(1).Span = 1;groups(1).Style = {Color('red'), Border('solid') };tbl = append(rpt, Table(magic(5)));tbl.ColSpecGroups = groups;tbl.Width = '5in';close(rpt);

Summary: A MATLAB crash or error message occurs when multiple instances of MATLAB use signal logging simultaneously in Linux
Description: On Linux systems, if any of the following actions occur simultaneously in two or more instances of MATLAB: Running a simulation in Simulink that uses signal logging Plotting signals in the Simulation Data Inspector Panning or zooming in the Simulation Data Inspector Running a signal or run comparison in the Simulation Data Inspector  MATLAB can crash or display the error message "Undefined function or variable. "
Workaround: Run the?startupMultiInstanceSDI.p?script from the attached archive in the command window upon starting MATLAB.

Summary: Applications packaged using MATLAB Compiler cannot exceed 2 GB on Windows systems.
Description: The Windows operating system imposes a 2 GB limit when embedding resources into an executable.?The packaging process used by the Application Compiler and Library Compiler apps embeds the binaries into a pre-built install application. If the binaries are larger than 2 GB in size, the embedding process fails, and may result in MATLAB crashing.?

Summary: Fail to open models containing Test Sequence blocks with the error: "failed assertion ...: fParserOldIds.find(make_pair(fCurrentChart, id)) == fParserOldIds.end()"
Description: In rare cases, models containing Test Sequence blocks can become corrupted, and opening such models leads to the following error in the diagnostic viewer: failed assertion ...: fParserOldIds.find(make_pair(fCurrentChart, id)) == fParserOldIds.end()  The corruption can occur when copying a whole step (action, transitions, and substeps) from one Test Sequence block to another.
Workaround: To open a corrupted model, press Ctrl-C in the command line when the error occurs (you may have to press Ctrl-C more than once). The Test Sequence blocks in the model will remain corrupted and will have to be deleted and reconstructed.Alternatively, you can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.?

Summary: Code generation with preprocessor conditionals causes assertion for a model with nested variant subsystem blocks.
Description: Code generation assertion with message??dict->getKey() != 0  during model build  when all the following conditions are true : Model has nested variant subsystems. Block parameter Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  is selected.?
Workaround: Uncheck the variant subsystem block parameter option Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation: Referenced model that includes Simulink function interfacing with local root inport signal and?used in?reusable top model can?produce incorrect code
Description: The code generator can produce incorrect code when using a referenced model that meets the following conditions:?? Contains a Simulink Function block.  In the local environment of the Simulink Function block, the block interfaces to a root inport signal of the model. Is used in a top model that is configured?with: Code interface packaging  set to Reusable function . Pass root-level I/O  set to Individual arguments . Multi-instance code error diagnostic  model parameter set to Warning  or None .   Under these conditions, generated top-model code passes a double reference to the input argument of the start function for the Simulink Function, instead of?the expected single reference input argument. For example, the code generator can produce the following incorrect code for this scenario. The call to Bot_Start , passes argument rtU_upper?as a double  ?reference. ?/* Model initialize function */ void Top_initialize(RT_MODEL *const rtM, real_T * rtU_upprt, real_T *rtY_output){? ? ?Bot_initialize(rtmGetErrorStatusPointer(rtM);? ? ?Bot_Start(&rtU_upper, rtY_output);? }? Instead, t he code generator  should produce  the follow ing code, where Bot_Start   passes rtU_upper   by a single reference. ? /* Model initialize function */ void Top_initialize(RT_MODEL *const rtM, real_T * rtU_upprt, real_T *rtY_output){? ? ?Bot_initialize(rtmGetErrorStatusPointer(rtM);? ? ?Bot_Start(rtU_upper, rtY_output);? }?
Workaround: For the top model that includes the reference model, make one of the following model configuration parameter changes:Set Pass root-level I/O to a value other than Individual arguments.Set Code interface packaging to Nonreusable function.

Summary: Assertion error when accessing custom code structure arrays in a C Action Language Stateflow chart
Description: Simulink might display an assertion error when you run or do an update diagram on a model that accesses a custom code structure array in a Stateflow chart. The Stateflow chart must meet all of the following conditions for this to happen: The chart uses C as the Action Language. The custom code structure array access in the Stateflow chart combines indexing and structure field reference in the same expression (e.g. writing "aCustomCodeStructArray[0].field1 "). The Stateflow chart contains input or output variables of bus types. The bus types do not need to be related to the custom code structure arrays used in the chart.
Workaround: Split the indexing and the structure field reference of a custom code structure array into different expressions. One way this can be achieved is to introduce a custom code variable to store the address of the custom code structure.For example, given the following custom code:typedef struct?{?double aField;?}?MyStruct;MyStruct *myStruct_ptr;MyStruct?aMyStructArray[2];The following action in a Stateflow chart:data = aMyStructArray[0].aField;can be converted into:myStruct_ptr = &(aMyStructArray[0]);data = myStruct_ptr->aField;

Summary: MATLAB crash when generating code for coding pattern that indexes into a matrix using unsigned integers
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a MATLAB coding pattern that indexes into a matrix using unsigned integers. When MATLAB crashes an assertion error message like the following might be displayed: failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\expr\indexexpranalysis\indexinfo.cpp? at line 147:?  ConstTools::fitsInTypePrecisely(fxpDV, typeForConst)    For an example see the following function:function Y = issue_g1593534(X,idxVec)    coder.inline('never');    Y = zeros(32,1);    for k1 = 2:19        Y(:) = 2*X(idxVec-1) + double(k1);    endend When the following codegen  command is executed:codegen issue_g1593534 -args {coder.typeof(0,[1,64]),coder.typeof(uint32(0),[1,32])} The crash occurs because idxVec  is specified as uint32 .
Workaround: To workaround the issue instead index into the matrix using signed integers. In the example described in the problem section the following?codegen?command does not crash.codegen issue_g1593534 -args {coder.typeof(0,[1,64]),coder.typeof(int32(0),[1,32])}

Summary: Test harness construction fails for a component with more than 500 input or output ports
Description: If you create a test harness for a component that contains more than 500 input or output ports, the harness construction fails. The harness will contain unconnected or missing (or both) conversion subsystems, and fails to compile.
Workaround: R2017a: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.R2016b: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1671801?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2016b.

Summary: Dashboard Scope block cannot connect to signals from blocks whose names include a colon
Description: When you open the Dashboard Scope block's dialog box and select a block with a colon in its name, signals originating from the block are not included in the signal connections table.
Workaround: Remove the colon from the block name.

Summary: In the Test Sequence Editor, the Run button disappears after running a simulation with Fast Restart enabled
Description: In the Test Sequence Editor, if you run a simulation with Fast Restart enabled, the Run  button in the toolbar disappears after the simulation finishes, and only reappears when Fast Restart is disabled.
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: In the Test Sequence Editor, the Run button is disabled after running a simulation in rapid accelerator mode
Description: In the Test Sequence Editor, if you run a simulation in rapid accelerator mode, the Run  button in the toolbar remains disabled after the simulation finishes.
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for signal with Reusable custom storage class
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains an Assignment block that performs a partial write. The same Reusable custom storage class is on the Assignment block output and at least one other block's output port.  In the incorrect generated code, the value of the signal at the Assignment block output is incorrectly overwritten by the?original, imported?value of the Reusable  custom storage class.

Summary: Magnitude-Angle to Complex block generates incorrect fixed-point HDL code
Description: The Magnitude-Angle to Complex Simulink block generates HDL code that does not produce the correct result when fixed-point operands are used.
Workaround: Use the Native Floating Point mode of  HDL Coder to generate the HDL code for this block.? If this is undesirable, the behavior of the "Magnitude-Angle To Complex" block can be replicated by using the "Trigonometric Function" block in sincos mode. The cos output is the real portion of the complex result and the sin output is the imaginary portion. To include the magnitude in the calculation, multiply the complex output with the magnitude input by using a "Product" block.

Summary: Error using Support Package for GigE Vision Hardware because of missing dependencies
Description: When using the Support Package for GigE Vision Hardware on a fresh installation of Windows, certain dependencies can be missing. The Support Package for GigE Vision Hardware depends on Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Redistributable Package. If this dependency is missing, an error is thrown similar to the following:   >> gigecamlist  Unexpected unknown exception from MEX file..  Error in imaq.supportpackages.gige.internal.gigecamlist  Error in gigecamlist (line 31)  cams = imaq.supportpackages.gige.internal.gigecamlist;
Workaround: Installation instructions    Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Navigate to the folder <MATLAB>\toolbox\imaq\imaqextern\drivers\win64\genicam\GenICam_v2_4\redist\.  Install Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Redistributable Package by double-clicking the file Microsoft_VC100_CRT_x64.msi. You might need administrative privileges to install this file.Restart your computer after the installation is completed.

Summary: ROS Node Deployment and SSH Connection to localhost Fail on Ubuntu Linux
Description: Any ROS-related functionality that tries to establish an SSH connection to "localhost" or "127.0.0.1" will fail with the following error message: Invalid MEX-file?'/usr/local/MATLAB/R2016b/toolbox/robotics/robotsimulink/robotslros/+robotics/+codertarget/+internal/private/ssh2client_mex.mexa64': libssh2.so.1: cannot open shared object file: No such file or?directory.   You can see this error when using the following functionality: ?MATLAB rosdevice function  Simulink When testing the connection to "localhost" in the "Connect to ROS Device" dialog When deploying a model containing ROS blocks to "localhost" ("Build and Load" or "Build and Run" modes)   ?This issue only occurs when MATLAB is installed on a Ubuntu Linux system. Installations on Windows or Mac machines are not affected.

Summary: Loops run slower than expected in the Live Editor
Description: A live script containing a loop with many iterations can run significantly slower than expected in the Live Editor.
Workaround: Move loops with many iterations into a separate script or function file and call the file from your live script.

Summary: Variant conditions may guard shared utility code causing compilation error
Description: A compilation?error may occur for a model that meets these conditions: The?model contains at least one Variant block. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Shared code placement ?parameter is set to Shared location .  The variant control variable is a Simulink.Parameter  with a Define  or an?ImportedDefine  custom storage class.  The compilation error occurs when code in the shared code folder has variant preprocessor conditionals.
Workaround: Change the setting of the Shared code placement parameter to Auto or for the variant control variables, change the custom storage class to CompilerFlag.

Summary: MATLAB Live Editor does not work on Ubuntu versions 17.04 and newer
Description: The Live Editor does not work on Ubuntu versions 17.04 and newer. When running MATLAB in one of these versions, there is no Live Script ?option in the New ?menu. ?In addition, attempting to open a live code (*.mlx) file by double clicking on the file in the Current Folder Browser, using the edit  command, or using the Open  menu produces no action. This occurs because Ubuntu versions 17.04 and newer do not ship with one of libraries required to run the Live Editor.
Workaround: Install the libgconf-2.4 library:apt-get install libgconf-2.4Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when displaying a MAT-file in the Current Folder browser
Description: If the current folder contains a corrupted v4 MAT-file, MATLAB might crash when the Current Folder browser attempts to display a preview of the file content. The preview is triggered when the?file is selected in the Current Folder Browser and the Details Panel is?expanded.
Workaround: Collapse the detail panel in the Current Folder browser prior to selecting the offending MAT-file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model hierarchy simulation in SIL or PIL mode might produce incorrect results
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy might produce incorrect results when all of these conditions apply: A Model block is configured for either software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation. That Model block?references a model that contains at least one other Model block. Both of these referenced models write to global data store memory. The top model reads from global data store memory.

Summary: Unnecessary rebuild of referenced model targets when enumerated data types are used
Description: If a referenced model uses a?variable of enumerated data type, Simulink?can unnecessarily rebuild the model reference target:? When you start a new MATLAB session and simulate the parent model. When?you run parallel simulations for the parent model (using Parallel Computing Toolbox).? When you build the referenced models in parallel (using Parallel Computing Toolbox).

Summary: When an Export-function model is nested in more than three levels of harness models, a combination of using Constant sample time?might produce wrong answer
Description: An?atomic subsystem does not execute correctly in normal mode simulation and the wrong answer appears when all of the following?conditions are true:  There are at least three levels of model hierarchy  The bottom model is an Export-function model  There exists a constant block inside an atomic subsystem within a function-call subsystem in the Export Function model ?
Workaround: Run attached p code, exportedFcnWorkaround, in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: MATLAB can crash after connecting lines to new ports in Simulink Editor
Description: In a model?in which model elements are very close together, MATLAB can crash if you branch a line?and?connect?it to a new port on a block that is already connected in the model. This crash can also occur in tight models?when you reposition a block programmatically that is already connected in the model and then try to connect a branched line to an empty port.  Suppose you have a Sum block that is connected in a model in which the model elements are close together. Using the block dialog box, or using set_param  at the command prompt, you add another sign, which adds a port to the block. When you drag a branched?line to the new port, MATLAB crashes. Similarly,?suppose?a block has two?inports. One is already connected to a model element and the other is not. You reposition the block using the Simulink API at the command prompt. When you drag a branched?line to connect to the empty port, MATLAB crashes. If you move the block interactively, the crash?does not occur.
Workaround: Move the block interactively after you add the port.

Summary: Referenced model containing a Test Sequence block fails to compile
Description: If you have a referenced model, and the model contains a Test Sequence block with a?verify() ?statement that uses format string specifiers in the error message (e.g.,verify(x == y, '%d ==?%d', x, y)?) , the model fails to compile with the following error: error C2143: syntax error: missing '{' before '*'?
Workaround: In the referenced model containing the?verify()?statement:open the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box;navigate to Code Generation -> Custom Code;under "Insert custom C code in generated", select Source file;type the following into the Source file text box:?#include "rt_mxclassid.h"Alternatively,?you can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: Unit Delay Input and State incorrectly use the same variable when different initial conditions are specified on a custom storage class
Description: In code, the state variable for a Unit Delay block might be incorrectly reused, leading to wrong answers, if all of the following conditions are true:? Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results ?or?Minimize global data access . Reuse local block outputs ?configuration parameter is enabled. A Unit Delay block's input has a custom storage class with an initial condition different than the block's state.  The variable for the Unit Delay state may be replaced with the storage class variable, which results in an incorrect answer because the two variables need different initial values.
Workaround: To workaround around the issue, do either of the following:See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727. That report describes a different but related issue, and the same workaround applies.Remove the?reusable custom storage class?specification from the input of the Unit Delay block.

Summary: Assertion error when running a Stateflow chart with custom code expressions in state entry actions
Description: If you have a Stateflow chart that assigns a custom code expression to a chart output in a state entry action, Simulink might display an assertion error when you try to run or update the model.
Workaround: To work around this and another issue in R2017a, see?Bug Report ID?1580490.

Summary: Crash when specifying a vector in RF Blockset's Configuration block 'Step size' parameter.
Description: RF Blockset accepts only a scalar as 'Step size' in the Configuration block, as it uses a common envelope bandwidth for all carriers. Before R2017a, entering a vector into that field resulted in an error upon model update/simulation. In R2017a, entering a vector and exiting the 'Step size' field in the block parameter dialog causes a core dump.
Workaround: Do not use a vector in 'Step size' field?of RF Block's Configuration block.

Summary: For a model with library-linked Stateflow charts, MAABcheck "Format of Entries in a State Block" gives different results?when run fromthe MATLAB command line versus the Model Advisor app
Description: Whenrunning MAAB check "Format of Entries in a State Block" (ID mathworks.maab.jc_0501 ) on models withlibrary-linked Stateflow charts, analysis behavior differs if thecheck executes from the MATLAB command line versus the Model Advisor app. If you open themodel using open_system? (or theSimulink Editor)?and run the check from the Model Advisor app (Analysis> Model Advisor > Model Advisor ), the analysis includes Stateflowcharts that are linked to a library. However, if you use load_system   to load the model and the ModelAdvisor.run  API to execute the check, the analysisdoes not include library-linked charts.
Workaround: To include library-linked charts in the analysis when executing the check?from the command line, run?find_system(<model_name>,'FollowLinks','on')before calling the?ModelAdvisor.run?command.

Summary: Generating?target hardware code from a Simscape model?using Simulink Real-Time and Visual Studio 2012 errors
Description: Generating code for target hardware from Simscape models using Simulink Real-Time and a Visual Studio 2012 mex C compiler results in an error message.?Typical error messages?are: error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol __dtoui3  fatal error LNK1120: 1 unresolved externals NMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio 11.0\VC\BIN\link.EXE"' : return code '0x460'
Workaround: Before you generate code for target hardware:Open the Configuration Parameters dialog box of the?Simscape model.In the left-hand pane,?select?the Code Generation node.For the System target file parameter, specify slrt.tlc.For the Make command parameter, specify make_rtw OPT_OPTS="/W3 /Z7 /O2".Click OK.

Description: The Unnecessary type conversions  configuration parameter in the Commonly Used Parameters  tab provides the two supported options:?none  and warning .?? However,?the All Parameters  tab provides a third, but unsupported option: error . This parameter should only accept none  and warning .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code?replacement for Add/Subtract/Sum blocks connected with inline variants generates incorrect code
Description: Code replacement library generates incorrect code?when  all   of the following conditions are met:  The model contains one or more Add/Subtract/Sum blocks.   The model uses inline variants, and the inline variants (for example, Variant Source/Sink block)?are directly connected to the output of one or more Add/Subtract/Sum blocks.   The model that generates code uses a Code Replacement Library (CRL) that contains RTW_OP_ADD ? or? RTW_OP_MINUS  entries.   The input/output types on the Add/Subtract/Sum block?connected with the inline variants match the input/output types of the  RTW_OP_ADD   or  RTW_OP_MINUS   code replacement?entry. Code replacement occurs for the Add/Subtract/Sum blocks during code generation.
Workaround: Follow these guidelines when you generate code for a model that uses a custom code replacement library and contains Add/Subtract/Sum blocks as well as?inline variants:Do not?directly connect the output of an Add/Subtract/Sum block to a Variant Source/Sink block or other blocks that produce inline variants.Instead, connect the output of an Add/Subtract/Sum block to a No-Op block, and connect the output of the No-Op block to the Variant Source/Sink block or other blocks that produce inline variants.A No-Op block can be, but not limited to, one of?the followings:A Gain block with "Gain=1", and the properties "Output data type", "Saturate on integer overflow", "Integer rounding mode" all the same as the?properties on the Add/Subtract/Sum block.A Data Type Conversion block with the properties "Output data type", "Saturate on integer overflow", "Integer rounding mode" all the same as the?properties on the Add/Subtract/Sum block.A Signal Conversion block with "Output" property set to "Signal copy".

Description: When generating code for models that include Simscape Multibody blocks with runtime parameters, link errors might occur.?The exact form of the error messages can vary, but they all refer in some way to the symbols isinf  and isnan , referenced by files that include the strings asserts.c  or setParameters.c  in their names.?Here is an example of what the errors might look like: testSfun_75c10581_1_setParameters.obj : error LNK2019: unresolved external symbol isinf referenced in function testSfun_75c10581_1_computeAsmRuntimeDerivedValuestestSfun_75c10581_1_asserts.obj : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol isinftestSfun_75c10581_1_setParameters.obj : error LNK2019: unresolved external symbol isnan referenced in function testSfun_75c10581_1_computeAsmRuntimeDerivedValuestestSfun_75c10581_1_asserts.obj : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol isnanC:\X\Event\20170718_Xyz\testSfun_sf.mexw64 : fatal error LNK1120: 2 unresolved externals
Workaround: A workaround might be possible depending on the particular code-gen target and compiler being used.?For generating an S-function using Microsoft Visual Studio 2012, one workaround is:1.??????????? Open the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box.?2.??????????? Select Code Generation?> Custom Code. 3.??????????? Under Additional build information,?select Defines and enter:???????????????isinf=rtIsInf isnan=rtIsNaN?5.??????????? Click OK.6.??????????? Regenerate the S-function.?For other failing combinations of code generation targets and compilers, the text might be slightly different. Please contact technical support if you need more assistance.

Summary: Calling the built-in subsasgn, while modifying the property of a handle object that is returned by a class method, can crash MATLAB
Description: Calling the built-in subsasgn  when modifying the property of a handle object that is returned by a class method can crash MATLAB. For example, the following code can cause a crash:    cl = TestClass;   cl.getFigure().Units = 'normalized'; given the following conditions: TestClass  overloads subsasgn  and? implements it with a call to built-in subsasgn . TestClass  provides a method, in this case getFigure , ?that returns a handle to one of the TestClass  properties. The field returned by getFigure  is assigned to. getFigure  has more than one parameter, being the default "obj" parameter. getFigure  is invoked with empty parentheses.  Here is a possible implementation of TestClass :     classdef TestClass < handle      properties          FigHandle = figure;       end      methods          function val = getFigure(obj, a)              val = obj.FigHandle;          end          function out = subsasgn(obj, ss, rhs)               out = builtin('subsasgn', obj, ss, rhs);           end       end    end
Workaround: To avoid the crash, you can use one of the following approaches:Break the indexing expression into individual indexing operations:>> fig = cl.getFigure();>> fig.Units = 'normalized';Invoke getFigure without parentheses:>> cl.getFigure.Units = 'normalized';Create set and get methods for FigHandle.?Detect when getFigure is invoked in the subsasgn definition. Instead of forwarding the entire assignment to built-in subsasgn, forward the indexing expression from after the call to getFigure:?  function obj = subsasgn( obj, s, b )    if (numel(s) == 1 && isCallToGetFigure(s(1))) || ...          (numel(s) == 2 && isCallToGetFigure(s(1)) && isEmptyParens(s(2)))        obj.FigHandle = b;    elseif (numel(s) > 2 && isCallToGetFigure(s(1)) && isEmptyParens(s(2))        obj.FigHandle = builtin( 'subsasgn', obj.getFigure, s(3:end), b );    elseif numel(s) > 1 && isCallToGetFigure(s(1))        obj.FigHandle = builtin( 'subsasgn', obj.getFigure, s(2:end), b );    else        error('invalid subsasgn syntax.');    endend

A MATLAB client communicating with such a scheduler will hang if the scheduler has insufficient memory to complete the communication. To diagnose the problem, open the jobmanager log files in the folder?LOGBASE/jobmanager_YOURJOBMANAGERNAME.*.*.log .  LOGBASE  and YOURJOBMANAGERNAME  are defined in your mdce_def  file. If these log files reference "OutOfMemoryError" , then an operation ?caused the scheduler to run out of memory.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Chart outputs might be unconditionally re-initialized in a state's during action if they are conditionally initialized in the entry action
Description: In MATLAB Action Language charts, if you conditionally initialize outputs to literal values inside the entry action of a state, Stateflow might produce wrong answers in some cases. For example, if you write the following in the entry action of a state:?  if (u > 0)? ?y1 = 1;else? ?y2 = 1;end?  Stateflow might unconditionally initialize both y1 and y2 in the states during action.
Workaround: To work around this issue, run the attached function?BugReport_1580490_WorkAround() in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when using Function Caller block inside Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block in a referenced export function model
Description: A model may produce incorrect simulation results under the following conditions: The model contains a Model block referencing an export function model The referenced export function model contains an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block The Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block contains a Function Caller block The Simulink Function block associated with the Function Caller blocks resides in the top model  Under these conditions, the return value for each output port of the Function Caller block is always zero, because the Function Caller block cannot find the appropriate Simulink Function block to call in the top model.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when running a referenced model containing a Stateflow Chart or MATLAB function block that use MATLAB structures or bus objects?
Description: MATLAB might crash when you update diagram or run a model that references another model containing a Stateflow Chart or MATLAB function block, if the referenced model defines a MATLAB struct or a MATLAB bus object which?you created using?commands like Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct  or?Simulink.Bus.createObject .
Workaround: To reduce the possibility of crashing, run the attached p-file when you start your MATLAB session.

You can control the block parameterization using the Basic Parameters > Model parameterization  block parameter.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem with State Reader or State Writer block generates wrong reusable function code
Description: If a Subsystem block contains a?State Reader or State?Writer block and the block parameter Function packaging  on the Code Generation tab is not set  t o  inline ,  Simulink generates wrong code. Reusable functions for subsystems containing State Reader or State?Writer blocks are not supported.?However, because there are no diagnostics for this problem, Simulink silently generates wrong code. The problem only happens with Subsystem that containing the State Reader Writer block but not the corresponding State Owner block. So if a subsystem containing both,  reusable function will work and the right dwork argument will pass in.For the subsystem that only contains State Reader Writer block but not the corresponding owner block,  our fix will disable the wrong reusable functions to be generated. In particular,  if customer specified "Inline" or "Nonreusable function",  we will honor the option; if customer specified "Auto",  we will make it inline; if customer specified "Reusable function",  we will change the generated code to be inline,  and report an error in the code generation report just as what Simulink does for other cases when reusable option cannot be honored.?
Workaround: Analyze the model?hierarchy for subsystems containing State Reader or State Writer blocks. Change the subsystem block parameter?Function packaging from Reusable function or Auto to Inline.

Summary: Code generation may fail after you change the Initial value or Dimensions parameters for a Data Store Memory block
Description: Code generation may fail if you change the Initial value  or Dimensions  parameters for a Data Store Memory block as part of this event sequence: You simulate or generate code?for a model containing a Data Store Memory block with an Initial value  parameter that is a?scalar?and a Dimensions  parameter that is a vector or matrix. This specification requires that the software perform scalar expansion, which is a method of converting scalar data to match the dimensions of vector or matrix data. For the Data Store Memory block, you change the Initial value  or Dimensions  parameters, so that the software no longer performs scalar expansion. That is, you change the Initial value  parameter, so that it has the same dimensions as the Dimensions  parameter or you change the?Dimensions  parameter so that it has the same dimensions as the Initial value  parameter. You generate code for this modified model as part of the same MATLAB session.?  If code generation fails, you get an error message that includes this string:? ? ? CGIR assertion 'propType.isMatrix()' failed in 'utils/rtwcg_prop.cpp
Workaround: After changing the Data Store Memory block parameters so that the block?no longer?requires scalar expansion, you can avoid this bug by closing the model and then opening?it before?generating code.

Summary: Exiting MATLAB with Simulink open causes a crash after loading the Simulation Data Inspector with specific preferences
Description: If the Simulation Data Inspector preferences are corrupted, manually or otherwise, exiting MATLAB with Simulink open causes a crash. The generated stack trace contains the error message: Assertion in bool __cdecl mnShutdownMatlabInternal(bool, bool, const class boost::optional<int> &, int *, bool, bool) at b:\ matlab\ src\ mcr\ runtime\ shutdown.cpp line 188:?Unexpected exception during MATLAB shutdown: Syntax error?
Workaround: Open MATLAB and enter Simulink.sdi.clearPreferences at the command line to restore default preferences.

Summary: Incomplete effect when tuning parameter in model workspace of multi-instance referenced model
Description: You can store a piece of parameter data (for example, a numeric workspace variable?or a?Simulink.Parameter  object) in the model workspace of a model. With model referencing, you can refer to the model multiple times in a model hierarchy (through multiple Model blocks). If you set the simulation mode for all of the Model blocks to Normal  (the default), when you simulate the model?hierarchy and?change the value of the parameter data during?the simulation, the change takes effect for only one of the instances of the referenced model (only one of the?Model blocks). The?Model blocks?also suffer this incomplete effect when you change the parameter value between Fast Restart simulations instead of during a simulation.

Summary: Newly added rows in the source Excel spreadsheet are missed when updating previously imported requirements
Description: If you have imported requirements from a Microsoft Excel spreadsheet, the Update operation in the Requirements Editor captures changes you make to existing requirements but does not update the requirements set to reflect new rows that you added to the source spreadsheet.
Workaround: Re-import?the updated Excel spreadsheet into a different requirements set, so that the new result contains all rows to match the current version of Excel document.?? Discard the results of previous import.?This has a disadvantage if?there are existing?traceability?links?TO or FROM?the previously imported items - some of the links may need to be re-created.?The Summary field value malfunction can be avoided if the column selected for Summary has more text, for example, complete sentences rather than 2-3 word titles.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multiple signals may incorrectly use the same Reusable custom storage class
Description: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing the same?Reusable  custom storage class on a root input port signal, a root output port signal, and an internal signal. This bug occurs for models in which the code generator?can reuse the same variable for the root inport and internal signals but not for the root outport signal. Instead of generating a two different variables, the code generator uses the same variable for all three signals causing incorrect code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible?for a subsystem that produces?reusable code and?contains a Merge block driving an output
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a subsystem in which a Merge block drives an output. The subsystem function packaging meets one of these conditions: The Function packaging  parameter value is Reusable function .?  The Function packaging  parameter value is  Auto, and there are multiple instances of the subsystem, so that the code generator produces a reusable function.    The Function packaging  parameter value is Nonre usable function, and the Function interface  parameter value is Allow arguments .    In the incorrect generated code, the subsystem output from the Merge block?may?incorrectly merge with another variable producing a wrong value.
Workaround: Change the subsystem Function packaging parameter to inline or Nonreusable function?with a Function interface parameter value of void_void.

Workaround: To specify the position of a subplot, use either a grid position or the?Position?parameter. Do not specify both values. For example:subplot('Position',[x y w h])

Summary: Simulink might crash if a model containing a Simulink Function block is placed in another Simulink Function block
Description: If you place a Simulink Function block at the root level of a model, and if that model is referenced from within a Simulink Function block?in an export function model, then Simulink might crash while simulating the top model. ?
Workaround: Change the referenced model such that it does not contain a Simulink Function at its root level.? For example, you can place the contents of the referenced model within a virtual subsystem.

Summary: Unable to connect to and retrieve historical data from Yahoo! in MATLAB
Description: When you execute the yahoo? function, MATLAB displays the following error: Error using yahoo (line 65)  Unable to connect or retrieve data from given URL.  When trying to retrieve historical data from Yahoo!, MATLAB displays the following error:? Error using yahoo/fetch (line 387)  Unable to return historical data for given security.
Workaround: Yahoo! has reportedly changed its API and site structure,which has resulted in connection errors. According to Yahoo!'s forum, ichart.yahoo.com will undergo structural changes that will notcover existing functionality. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch between MATLAB and generated code resuts for S-function based System object with two or more inputs if input sizes change to unknown
Description: MATLAB Coder requires either known size or upper limit on size for each input to a method at the compile time. If the second or subsequent inputs to an s-function based System object change from a known size to an unknown size (for example, variable size input), the generated code might produce incorrect results.  In the following example, the second input to dsp.VariableFractionalDelay  changes from a scalar to a column vector of size L, which is an input to the function. function [y1,y2] = foo_varsize(L)  ? persistent obj  ? if isempty(obj)  ? ? obj = dsp.VariableFractionalDelay;  ? end  ? y1 = obj((1:L).',7);  ? y2 = obj((L+1:2*L).',(1:L).');  end   For the above example, the generated code result does not match the MATLAB function output; the value of y1 will not be equal to y1_cg and y2 will not be equal to y2_cg. codegen foo_varsize -args {1}?  [y1,y2] = foo_varsize(100);?  [y1_cg,y2_cg]= foo_varsize_mex(100);?

On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Creating a test harness for a model containing a commented Simulink Function block fails with the error "Could not compute compiled signal attributes for output port 1 of the component under test.'"
Description: If you create a test harness for a model containing a commented Simulink Function block, the test harness creation fails with this error message: Could not compute compiled signal attributes for output port 1 of the component under test. This is most likely because the block is part of an inactive variant and was removed during model update. Activate the variant or choose the 'Prototyping' harness objective to create a harness for this component ?
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch between Simulation and Code generation for multiratemodels containing Variant Subsystem blocks
Description: A multirate model containing variant subsystem may have a potential simulation and codegeneration mismatch when all of the following conditions are true. Model reference block is used as as variant choice, with different sample rate when compared to other variant choice.? Configuration Parameters>Diagnostics>Advanced parameters>Combine output and update methods for code generation and simulation  option is checked. Command line :set_param(bdroot,'ForceCombineOutputUpdateInSim','on')

Summary: Nested masked Subsystems generate Unconnected block error during update diagram
Description: When blocks from certain Simscape libraries (For example, SimRF library) are placed inside a masked Subsystem of a model,  Unconnected block  error might be displayed during model update diagram.  An example of the error form one such model with SimRF library is:  Error compiling Simscape network for model.  Caused by:  [block]: Physical port is not connected to any other Simscape blocks. Connect this port to another Simscape block.
Workaround: Look under the mask of the parent subsystem before trying to run your model or updating the diagram.

Summary: Sample time independent model in a Function-Call Subsystem?may cause a strong error message
Description: When a model block referencing a sample time independent model is placed in a Function-Call Subsystem whose Trigger Port's sample time type is set to be periodic  and sample time to be -1 ,?updating?the model diagram may cause the following error: The S-function 'XX_Model/XX_Function/XX_REF' has improperly set the sample time of input port?YY when placed in the function call subsystem 'XX_Model/XX_Function'.
Workaround: To work around the error message,?change the Trigger Port's sample time to?be equal to the triggering signal's sample time. For example, if the Function-Call Subsystem is controlled by a signal with sample time 0.2, set the Trigger Port's sample time to 0.2?by opening the block parameter dialog of the Trigger Port block in the Function-Call Subsystem and change the Sample time?in the tab Main to 0.2.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible with Casting modes set to Standards Compliant and Replace multiplication by powers of two with signed bitwise shifts set to on
Description: Incorrect generated code may occur for a model that meets all of these conditions:In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Casting Modes  parameter is set to Standards Compliant . In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Replace multiplications by powers of two with signed bitwise shifts  is set to on.   The model must also meet one of these conditions: 1. The model contains a Rate Transition block that meets these conditions:  The Rate Transition block displays Db_buf . The input signal to the Rate Transition block is at a slower rate than the output signal. For the Rate Transition block, in the Block Parameters dialog box  the Ensure data integrity during data transfer  parameter is selected. For the Rate Transition block, in the Block Parameters dialog box, the Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  parameter is not selected. The Rate Transition block input is not a scalar.        2. *The model contains a MATLAB Function Block in which a logical expression is an operand of the multiply operator, and the other operand is a power of 2. For example:?a = (b > c) * 8 3. The model contains a Numerical Oscillator block (DSP Toolbox) and the Add internal dither  checkbox is selected, with Number of dither bits  with a value?greater than 1       4. The model uses fixed point binary point conversion in which the input and output have the same Real World Value.  *This bug may also occur for MATLAB coder if a logical expression is an operand of the multiply operator, and the other operand is a power of 2. (For example:?a = (b > c) * 8)?and the Casting Modes  parameter is set to Standards Compliant and the  Use signed shift left for fixed-point operations and multiplication by powers of 2   is set to  on.    Note that the combination of parameter settings leading to incorrect code generation is not recommended by the Code Generation Advisor for the  MISRA objective.  The recommendation is   The Casting Modes  parameter set to Standards Compliant  The  Replace multiplications by powers of two with signed bitwise shifts  or Use signed shift left for fixed-point operations and multiplication by powers of 2  is  set to off.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model block with initialize port produces wrong simulation result in rapid accelerator mode when the referenced model contains blocks with constant sample time.
Description: Your model is subject to this bug if it meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a Model block with the parameter ?'Show model initialize port' selected. The Model block is referencing a model that contains blocks with constant sample time such as a Constant block. Simulation results from normal and rapid accelerator modes do not match.  When all of the above conditions are met, only normal mode simulation produces the correct result.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds to produce the correct simulation result:Set the referenced model to use accelerator mode. Simulate the top model in normal mode. If you want to simulate in rapid accelerator mode, change the sample time of all Constant blocks in the referenced model from 'Inf' to '-1'.

Summary: Crash after modifying and using?enumerated type defined?in data dictionary
Description: After you modify?an enumerated data type whose definition resides in a data dictionary, MATLAB can later crash depending on the technique you used. Using?either of these techniques?leads to a?crash?when you?later attempt to use the type in a model: You revert changes that you made to the definition. You use the?programmatic?interface?of the data dictionary (for example, the methods of a Simulink.data.dictionary.Section   object) to modify the definition.
Workaround: Depending on the technique that you used to modify the type definition, use one of these workarounds:After?you revert?changes that you made to the type definition, save and close the dictionary.Before using the programmatic interface to modify the type definition, save the dictionary.

Summary: Deployed applications using ?modbus error out
Description: Executing a MATLAB Compiler deployed application using modbus  functionality results in the following error: Undefined function or variable 'instrument.interface.modbus.tcpip.Modbus'.  ? or?  Undefined function or variable 'instrument.interface.modbus.serialrtu.Modbus'. ?
Workaround: When building your application using the MATLAB Compiler, use the "-a" flag to manually include 'instrument.interface.modbus.tcpip.Modbus' or 'instrument.interface.modbus.serialrtu.Modbus' with the mcc command or with?deploytool, as shown in the examples below:1 . When using the modbus function with 'tcpip'>> mcc -m myapp.m -a instrument.interface.modbus.tcpip.ModbusIf using deploytool, click on "Settings" on the top-right of the Application Compiler window and under "Additional Parameters passed to MCC" enter the following:-a instrument.interface.modbus.tcpip.Modbus2. When using the ?modbus function with 'serialrtu'>> mcc -m myapp.m -a instrument.interface.modbus.serialrtu.ModbusIf using deploytool, click on "Settings" on the top-right of the Application Compiler window and under "Additional Parameters passed to MCC" enter the following:-a instrument.interface.modbus.serialrtu.Modbus

Summary: Nested calls to fmincon cause a crash
Description: When a call to the?fmincon 'sqp'  algorithm is nested within another call to the same algorithm, then MATLAB can crash. This nesting?occurs, for example, when the objective or nonlinear constraint function calls the?fmincon 'sqp'  algorithm.
Workaround: Use another fmincon algorithm, such as 'interior-point' or 'sqp-legacy'. For example,options = optimoptions(@fmincon,'Algorithm','interior-point');?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you import a file into Test Manager
Description: Importing a file into Test Manager crashes MATLAB if the Simulation Data Inspector was not opened first.
Workaround: Open the Simulation Data Inspector before importing files into Test Manager.

Summary: graydist crashes MATLAB when the "ind" input variable is not double
Description: graydist  crashes MATLAB when using the following syntax: ? ? T = graydist(A,ind)  and "ind"  is a numeric vector of type other than double .
Workaround: Convert "ind" to double before passing it to graydist:???ind = double(ind);??? T = graydist(A,ind);?"ind" must be a double vector of linear indices, i.e. it must only contain positive and whole doubles.

Summary: Adding a document part to itself can crash MATLAB
Description: Adding a document part to itself? can crash MATLAB. For example, this script crashes MATLAB. dp = mlreportgen.dom.DocumentPart('docx');append(dp,dp);?
Workaround: Do not add a document part to itself.

Summary: Matrix dimension errors in Simulink point-by-point models exported with boundary constraints
Description: When you export point-by-point models with boundary constraints, the models have incorrect matrix dimensions. If you try to validate the model with test data, the validation can error with a message similar to: "Matrix multiply dimensions propagation error. Error occurred while setting input port 2 of 'Validation_models_Simulink_point_by_Point/TORQUE/Constraint/Constraint-conswitch/Constraints/Constraint1/Constraint - conconvexhull/Product' to have the dimensions [9]. A possible cause for this error is that these dimensions do not agree with the partial dimensions information present on other ports"?
Workaround: On the Export Models dialog box, clear Export boundary constraint. You can also follow these instructions to install the attached file to work around this issue.Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Casting tological from a fixed-point?type with slope bias scaling?that cannot represent zero canproduce the wrong answer
Description: In MATLAB, you candefine a fixed-point?type with slope bias scaling?that cannot represent zero and cast thistype to a logical type. In this operation, the correct logical result is true ,since the source value can never be zero. However, code generation produces false .This issue can occur in MATLAB Coder, or for a MATLAB Function block or a MATLAB System block in Simulink.  Here is an example that illustrates the issue:   x ?=cast(fi(1,?1,?32,?2,?-1),?'logical');?  This cast operationproduces true  in MATLAB, but generated code can compute false .?
Workaround: Avoidcasting to logical type for fixed-point types with slope bias scaling?that cannot representzero.

Summary: MATLAB Production Server Dashboard setup fails on Windows
Description: When setting up MATLAB Production Server dashboard using the command setup.bat install ?you may get the following error:server error: Command failed: C:\ Windows\ system32\ cmd.exe / s / c ""C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ MATLAB Production Server\ R2017a\ dashboard\ PostgreSQL\ bin\ pg_ctl" -w -D "C:\ mps\ dashboard\ data" init
Workaround: Verify whether your system has the?Visual C++ Redistributable Packages for Visual Studio 2013. If your system does not have these packages, download and install them from?here.?You can also fix this issue by appending your system path with $MPS_ROOT/bin/win64 where $MPS_ROOT is the directory where MATLAB Production Server is installed.

Summary: Code generation assertion when AUTOSAR model inport with explicit data access mode is connected to a subsystem within an exported function
Description: Code generation fails with an assertion if all of the following conditions exist: An AUTOSAR model contains an Inport block. In the Configure AUTOSAR Interface dialog box, in the?Simulink-AUTOSAR Mapping ?view, the Simulink inport is mapped to an AUTOSAR port and configured for any explicit data access mode; for example ExplicitRead ?data access mode. At least one of the Inport block destinations is a subsystem within an exported function
Workaround: To work around this bug:For each Inport block signal connected to a subsystem within an exported function, add a Signal Conversion block between the Inport block and the subsystem.For each added Signal Conversion block, open the block parameters dialog box and select the option Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.
