Summary: Creating a test harness for an If Action or a Switch Case Action subsystem crashes MATLAB
Description: If you create a test harness for an If Action or a Switch Case Action subsystem, and the harness verification mode is set to Software-in-the-Loop (SIL) or Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL), MATLAB crashes.
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Loop induction variable used in matrix assignment might result in incorrect code
Description: Incorrect results are possible in rare situations for a model design that results in the following generated code: The generated code contains a nested for  loop. Inside the nested for  loop body, the value of the loop induction variable is used in a matrix assignment.?  In the generated code, the two nested for  loops are incorrectly interchanged.  For example, the correct code contains this nested for  loop: ?for(i=0,i<3;i++) {??? for(j=0, j<2; j++) { ?????? a[i][j] = j;?? } }       In the incorrect code, the for  loop statements are interchanged as follows:  ?for(j=0,j<2;j++)? {??? ?for(i=0, i<3; i++)? {????????? ?a[i][j] = j;???} }

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for an inlined For Each subsystem with separate output and update functions
Description: Incorrect generated code may occur for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a For Each subsystem block. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Single output/update function  parameter is deselected, the Language  is set to C  (not C++ ), and Function Prototype Control is not?configured for the model. The For Each subsystem is?inside of another subsystem that is a Function-Call, Enabled, or Triggered subsystem. The For Each subsystem has an internal state because it uses an?Integrator block or a Delay block, for example. The For Each subsystem Function packaging  is set to Inline  or Auto  with a single instance so that the code generator inlines the For Each subsystem code.  When a model meets these conditions, the parent subsystem function code contains two for  loops. In the incorrect generated code,?a?scalar variable (in the below example, INPUT_TO_DELAY )?is used to?calculate the next state in the first loop and store it for the second loop. Using a scalar variable causes the state calculation to use the last array element for the entire array as shown here: void Subsystem() {?  ??? real_T INPUT_TO_DELAY;  ??? for (ImplicitIterator_itr = 0; ImplicitIterator_itr < 5;???????? ImplicitIterator_itr++) {  ????? INPUT_TO_DELAY = ...  ??? }  ??? for (ImplicitIterator_itr = 0; ImplicitIterator_itr < 5;???????? ImplicitIterator_itr++) {????? debug1_DW.CoreSubsys[ImplicitIterator_itr].UnitDelay_DSTATE = INPUT_TO_DELAY;??? }?}  In the correct generated code, an array is used to store each state element. That way, each state element gets?the correct calculated value.  void Subsystem() {?  ??? real_T INPUT_TO_DELAY[5];  ??? for (ImplicitIterator_itr = 0; ImplicitIterator_itr < 5;???????? ImplicitIterator_itr++) {  ????? INPUT_TO_DELAY[ImplicitIterator_itr] = ...  ??? }  ??? for (ImplicitIterator_itr = 0; ImplicitIterator_itr < 5;???????? ImplicitIterator_itr++) {????? debug1_DW.CoreSubsys[ImplicitIterator_itr].UnitDelay_DSTATE = INPUT_TO_DELAY[ImplicitIterator_itr];??? }?}
Workaround: To avoid this bug, select the Single output/update function parameter.? If your model requires separate output/update functions, for the For Each subsystem block, change the Function packaging parameter?to Reusable function or Nonreusable function.

Summary: Interpolation using Prelookup block Output >?Data type?> Inherit: Inherit from 'Table data' setting does not always enforce the output data type of the block to be same as the?Table data type
Description: Interpolation using Prelookup  block Output >?Data type?>  Inherit: Inherit from 'Table data'  setting does not always enforce the output data type of the block?to be same as the?Table data type. This mismatch can happen when all these conditions are true: Input port data type of Interpolation using Prelookup block  is underspecified. Interpolation block uses the Source of interpolation points >  Specify via dialog  setting.? Input port data type of block succeeding Interpolation Using Prelookup  block is specified.? Table data type is different from input port data type of block succeeding Interpolation Using Prelookup  block.?  These conditions cause?the Interpolation Using Prelookup  block?to select the data type via back propagation, which?does not enforce that the output data type match the Table data type.
Workaround: Use one of these workarounds:Set the input port data type of the Interpolation Using Prelookup blockSet the?Interpolation Using Prelookup block Specification > Source parameter to Inport port and specify the Table data.Set the input port data type of the?succeeding block to be the same as the Table data type?of the Interpolation Using Prelookup block.

Summary: DOM API fails to apply image style name to wrapper paragraph
Description: The DOM API fails to apply the style name of an image that it wraps in a paragraph to that wrapper paragraph.?The DOM API wraps an image in a paragraph?when you append the image to the body of a Word or PDF document or to a table entry in a Word or PDF document. This wrapping is necessary because these document types do not allow images to be appended directly to a document body or table cell. When the style name fails to be applied to the wrapper paragraph, you cannot specify the wrapper paragraph's format by assigning the name of a style defined in the document's style sheet to the image style name property. For example, suppose that a Word template named ReportTemplate defines a style named CenteredImageWrapper that centers a paragraph between page margins. You want to use this?script to center an image added to the body of a Word document: import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('myreport','docx','ReportTemplate');img = Image(which('b747,jpg'));img.StyleName? = 'CenteredImageWrapper';append(d,img) close(d);?  This script fails and the image is not centered in the generated document
Workaround: Wrap an?image?in a paragraph before assigning?it to a Word or PDF document body or table entry, for example,import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('myreport','docx','ReportTemplate');img = Image(which('b747,jpg'));p = Paragraph();p.Style = {HAlign('center')};append(p,image);append(d,p) close(d);?

Summary: Simulating a multi-layer model referencing and export-function model with discrete sample times?might crash Simulink
Description: When all ?of these?conditions are met, simulating a top model crashes Simulink: The top model references a mid-layer model that is not an export-function model.? The mid-layer non-export-function model references an export function model. The bottom export-function model has?function-call root level Inport blocks that have specified discrete sample times.??
Workaround: Avoid the modeling pattern as described. Two examples of ways to do this are:Change Sample time parameters?of all function-call root level Inport blocks inside the bottom layer export-function model to -1' (inherited). Avoid referencing such an export-function model directly from the top model.

Description: If you create a test harness for a subsystem or model that has an output with an enumerated data type, and choose?Test Sequence ?as the source, the test harness creation fails with?this error message: Memory block does not have a parameter named 'InitialCondition'.?
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: Custom Storage Class Designer corrupts csc_registration.m file
Description: If?you?use the Custom Storage Class designer to create a custom storage class with Type  set to Other , you add your own custom attributes to the storage class by deriving a MATLAB?class from Simulink.CustomStorageClassAttributes . If?you set such a custom attribute to an invalid value (such as a cell array or a structure), when you save your work (by clicking Save  in the Designer), the Designer generates an error and corrupts the csc_registration.m  file?in the package. If you then close the Designer: When you next open?the Designer?again, you cannot inspect or edit storage class definitions for the affected package.  You cannot use data objects from the affected package to apply custom storage classes and memory sections to data items in a model.
Workaround: If you click Save and the Designer corrupts the csc_registration.m file, do not close the Designer. Instead, determine which custom attribute has an?invalid value, correct the value, and click Save again. To determine which custom attribute has an invalid value, open the corrupted file and inspect the last line of code.If you close the Designer after it corrupts the csc_registration.m?file, you must?fix the file yourself. Manually revert or delete the file, or determine which custom attribute has an invalid value and manually?fix the file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Absolute time temporal logic in atomic subcharts might evaluate as true earlier than specified
Description: Stateflow might evaluate absolute time temporal logic incorrectly (evaluating as true earlier than the specified time) for atomic subcharts when all of the following conditions are true: A Stateflow chart contains multiple input data with different sample rates This Stateflow chart contains an atomic subchart The atomic subchart uses only a subset of the input data passed to the main chart The greatest common divisor of the sample rates of the input data the atomic subchart uses is different from the greatest common divisor of the sample rates of all the inputs of the container chart The atomic subchart uses absolute time temporal logic (temporal logic functions such as 'after' and 'before' with the 'sec', 'msec' or 'usec' keywords)  For example, consider a main chart with inputs u1 and u2 with sample rates 0.2 and 0.3. The greatest common divisor of these sample rates is 0.1. This then becomes the sample rate of the chart. Now suppose the atomic subchart only has a single input u1. The sample rate of the atomic subchart will be computed as 0.2. When the main chart sample rate is different than the atomic subchart sample rate, the model might be affected by this bug.?

Summary: Junctions and transitions enclosed by text or image annotations  can vanish in the editor view

Workaround: Avoid enclosing Junctions/Transitions inside of annotations.

Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly indicate a model with block replacements as incompatible
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly indicate a model with block replacements as incompatible. This incompatibility is caused during block replacement, which errors out upon finding artificial algebraic loops in the model. The error message advises to clear the 'Minimize algebraic loop occurrences' parameter from the diagnostic pane in the Model Configuration Parameters Window. However, even after setting this parameter to 'none' or 'warning', the model fails the compatibility check.

Summary: MATLAB may crash while generating C code that contains unrolled matrix assignments
Description: MATLAB may crash during code generation for a model that results in generated code that contains unrolled matrix assignments (that is, matrix accesses that do not use an induction variable to grab an element.). The unrolled matrix assignments are unrolled inside of another for  loop. Either of these scenarios?may produce this code: (1) The model contains a MATLAB Function block with?the?coder.unroll ?function inside of a nested for  loop such as the following:   for i=1:5?? for j=coder.unroll( 2:10 )?????????? M[1][j] = N[i][j];?  (2) In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Loop Unrolling Threshold? parameter value is higher than the size of?the?nested for ?loop that contains?the matrix accesses. Matrix assignments must be one of these forms: Copying from one matrix element to the other, where one or both the accesses do not use induction variables to access an element. For example: M[0] = N[i]  M[0] = N[0]  M[i] = N[0]   Initialization of matrix elements: M[0] = 10;    For an example, please see the attached model. When MATLAB crashes, you may get this error message:  CGIR assertion 'destVaryingCompIdx() < destAccessCompArray->size()' failed in 'b:\ matlab\ src\ cgir_xform\ dom_core\ lowering\ memfcn\ memcandidate.cpp:56' ?
Workaround: To workaround this?issue, you can?try one of these workarounds?:?1.?Clear?the?Use memcpy for vector assignment?parameter:? ? ?set_param('modelName', 'EnableMemcpy', false);2. Set the Memcpy threshold (bytes) parameter to a very high value such as the?following:?? ? ?set_param('modelName', 'MemcpyThreshold', int32(intmax));2.?Set the Loop unrolling threshold parameter to??zero:? ? ?set_param('modelName', 'RollThreshold', 0);3. ?Do not use the?coder.unroll function.The attached zip file contains?examples of the above?workarounds.

Summary: Duplicate points with?add_line can prevent line from being added
Description: in R2017a, duplicate points specified with add_line  are ignored when the second point immediately follows the?one it duplicates. If,?without the duplicate points,?the list does not specify at least two points, the function returns a warning and the line is not added to the diagram. Scripts that include add_line  functions that end up with fewer than two points might not run as expected. Suppose?that you specify these?points with?add_line : [100,200; 100,200; 250,250; 300,300; 300,300; 100,200]  The?software treats add_line as if you specified these points: [100,200; 250,250; 300,300; 100,200]
Workaround: If you use points with add_line, you can either:Make sure that the list of points?does not have duplicates.Make sure that the list contains at least two points after duplicates are ignored.If you cannot make these changes, download and install the attached patch to revert the behavior to R2016b.Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Summary: Unable to print figure that contains GraphPlotobject with custom line widths
Description: Printing a figure that contains a?GraphPlot ?object with custom line widths causes an error when both of these conditions are true: 'LineWidth' ?property of the? GraphPlot  object is set to a vector of values. Requested output size is larger than the screen size.?  An error message similar to the following appears in the Command Window:??? Error using alternatePrintPath>printSetup (line 239)??? All of the input arguments must be of the same size and shape.??? Previous inputs had size 2 in dimension 1. Input #3 has size 4  ???Error in alternatePrintPath (line 98) ? ?[pj, pt, setupComplete] = printSetup(pj);  ?? Error in print (line 102) ? ?pj = alternatePrintPath(pj);?   For example, if you are using a monitor with 1920x1200 resolution, then these two cases result in an error when printing the figure. Case 1: PaperPosition  size is larger thanscreen size. f ?= figure; ?s = [ 1 2 3]; ?t = [3 1 2]; ?weights = [10 20 30];?G = graph(s,t,weights);?hp = plot(G);?hp.LineWidth = [1 2 3];?f.PaperUnits = 'inches';?f.PaperPosition(3:4) = [20 20];?print('test.png','-dpng');? ? Case 2: Screen display scaling for the operating system is not set to 100% (the default).? f ?= figure; ?s = [ 1 2 3]; ?t = [3 1 2]; ?weights = [10 20 30];?G = graph(s,t,weights);?hp = plot(G);?hp.LineWidth = [1 2 3];?print('test.pdf','-dpdf','-fillpage');
Workaround: Use one of these workarounds:Set the 'LineWidth'?property?of the GraphPlot?object to a scalar value (not a vector of values).Set the screen display scaling to 100% and print to an output size that is less than the screen size. For example, on Windows 10 systems, you can set the screen display by selecting Display Setting > Change the size of text, apps and other items.?

Summary: Code generation for nested objects?might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation when all of the following conditions are true: The program creates two objects of the same MATLAB class.? Both objects are passed to the same function. This function assigns another object to a property of the input. One of the?input objects already had an object assigned to the same property. The new property assignment belongs to a different class.  For example, this function crashes in code generation: function foo ??? obj1 =?MyClass; ??? obj2 = MyClass; ??? assignProp(obj1); ??? obj2.p = MyOtherClassA; ??? assignProp(obj2); end?  function assignProp(obj) ??? obj.p = MyOtherClassB; end?

Summary: Page layout auto setting ignored in PDF reports
Description: The Auto  options in the Report Explorer's Page Layout component does not use the settings defined by the corresponding page layout specification in the report template.
Workaround: Change the Auto setting to Specify.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for an inlined MATLAB function that uses PARFOR loop
Description: MATLAB Coder can generate incorrect C code for a MATLAB function when both  of?these conditions are true: A parfor  loop in the?function modifies an output variable. Where the function is called, at the call site,?the output from the function is not assigned to a variable. Instead, it is assigned to a?structure field,?array element,?or similar expression.  In this case, if the code generator inlines the function, it?might incorrectly place the assignment from the function output on the left side of the assignment. Therefore, it?produces incorrect C code,?which results in a? wrong answer. This example illustrates the issue. This MATLAB code produces a different result in generated code than the result produced in MATLAB. function y = foo(n)z.a = zeros(n);  if n > 0? ? z.a = baz(n);end  y = z.a;end  function s = baz(n)coder.inline( 'always' );s = ones(n);parfor i = 1:n??? s(i) = i + 100;endend ?
Workaround: Place coder.inline('never') in functions that use parfor. For example:function y = foo(n)z.a = zeros(n);if n > 0? ? z.a = baz(n);endy = z.a;endfunction s = baz(n)coder.inline( 'never' );s = ones(n);parfor i = 1:n??? s(i) = i + 100;endend?

Summary: PDF cascading style sheet vertical-align property does not align content
Description: The cascading style sheet (CSS) vertical-align  property for PDF output does align the content vertically. For example, this style does not align content with the bottom of a table entry in a PDF report: ??td {vertical-align:bottom}
Workaround: Use the XSL formatting object (FO)?display-align?property to align content vertically. For example, to aligns table entry content with the bottom of the entry, use:?? td {-fo-display-align:after}

Description: You might get incorrect coverage results for external MATLAB functions when both of the following conditions are true:  The model contains multiple MATLAB function blocks that call the same external MATLAB function The model is analyzed for coverage using multiple simulations   The first simulation of such a model produces correct coverage results. This issue only affects subsequent simulations.?

Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage

Summary: Code Generation Advisor check of model configuration settings generates error?

Configuration Parameters  > Optimization  > Use division for fixed-point net slope computation ?is set to Use division for reciprocals of integers only. Workaround: Select On for?Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Use division for fixed-point net slope computation, which is the recommended setting for MISRA C:2012 guidelines.

Description: If you export a model to a previous version in SLX format and the model contains any truth tables, then the contents of the truth tables are corrupted. Workaround: To work around this issue:Select the MDL file format when exporting the model to the previous version.Open the exported model using the previous version of Simulink and save it in SLX format.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Improper access to a private or protected property not detected during code generation
Description: MATLAB does not permit?the use of a private property by functions or methods outside of its class. An attempt to execute a read of such a property generates an error. In certain cases, MATLAB Coder might fail to detect such improper access?and permit?the access of a private property. As a result, the?code generation or simulation proceeds as if the property was public, instead of producing an error. Specifically, MATLAB Coder might ignore the private or protected?attribute when accessing a property of an object if that object itself is?the value of a constant property of another class. For example, generating code for this line: ?x = obj.p.value;  may fail to trigger an error if obj  is an instance of the?class Foo , where Foo  is defined as:? classdef Foo   properties (Constant)    p = Bar  end end  Here the?class Bar  is defined as:? classdef Bar   properties(Private)      value = 1   end end  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Generating code for a model containing an unconnected variant subsystem outport may fail with an unclear error message
Description: ?Generating code for a model that meets all of the following conditions may fail with an error message: The model contains a variant subsystem with at least one outport block. The outport block is not connected from inside the subsystem.  When code generation fails, you may see an unclear error message similar to the following:    Code generation assertion 'blkIOIdx >= 0' failed in 'b:\matlab\src\simulink\sl_engin\rtwcgsignal.cpp:361'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code is possible for Element-wise Product block when using symbolic dimension signals
Description: The generated code may fail to preserve symbolic expression when the following conditions are met:In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Allow symbolic dimension specification  parameter is selected. The model contains a Product block in which the Multiplication  parameter is set to Element-wise(.*) . A signal with a symbolic dimension is an input to the Product block.

Summary: When using the Vision Zynq Hardware Support Package with blocks from Vision HDL Toolbox, some blocks do not function correctly at reset and never recover
Description: Vision HDL Toolbox blocks that contain line buffer memories do not function correctly when used in the Vision Zynq work-flow if the FPGA powers up or resets in the middle of a video frame. Normally these blocks would recover at the next vStart pulse, but due to an error they never recover. Symptoms of this error are that the HDMI video output of the board does not appear to have sync signals and the monitor typically shows a black image and a "no sync" error message.  The blocks that do not function correctly are: In Analysis & Enhancement: Edge Detector Median Filter  In?   Conversions: Demosaic Interpolator   In Filtering: Image Filter Median Filter  In Morphological Operations: Closing Dialation Erosion Grayscale Closing Grayscale Dilation Grayscale Erosion Grayscale Opening Opening ?
Workaround: Precede the blocks that error with a block that does not error in these circumstances and does not pass the erroneous pixel control signals to the affected blocks.The best workaround block to use is the ROI Selector. The ROI Selector block will ignore the partial pixel control signals and wait for the next valid vStart to begin sending its output to the rest of the system.?You need only one workaround block at the beginning of the pixel stream.For use with Vision Zynq, configure the ROI Selector block with these parameter settings: Regions source: Property Regions: [1,1,1920,1080] In general, configure the region size to be the full image frame size. If your model uses RGB or YCbCr format, use three similarly configured ROI Selector blocks, one on each channel, to ensure that the delay inserted by the ROI Selector block is matched across the channels.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when you delete the parent subsystem of a signal connected to a Dashboard block and then select the Dashboard block
Description: For some Dashboard blocks, MATLAB crashes when you delete the parent subsystem of a signal connected to a Dashboard block and then select the Dashboard block. Affected blocks include the Gauge, Half Gauge, Linear Gauge, and Quarter Gauge blocks.
Workaround: After deleting the subsystem but before selecting the Dashboard block, save and re-open the model.

Summary: Code generation assertion error for AUTOSAR model when calibration parameter connects to root outport or to type conversion that requires shift operation
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR model generates an assertion error if both of the following conditions are true: A constant block references an AUTOSAR.Parameter  data object, for which the storage class is set to InternalCalPrm  or CalPrm.  The constant block either (a) connects directly to a root outport, or (b) connects to a type conversion block and the type conversion requires a shift operation.  ?
Workaround: To work around this bug, do either of the following:?Set the storage class of constant block output signal to SimulinkGlobal. For example, right-click the signal to the right of the constant block and open the Signal Properties dialog box. Enter a value for Signal name and, in the Code Generation tab, set Storage class to SimulinkGlobal. Insert a unit delay block?immediately after the constant block.

Summary: In a test harness created for a model, signal lines connected to the outputs of the Model block in the test harness do not have 'ShowPropagatedSignal' turned on
Description: If you create a test harness for a model with one or more Outport blocks, the signal lines connected to the outputs of component under test Model block in the test harness do not have ShowPropagatedSignals  turned on.
Workaround: After creating the test harness, open the test harness, select each signal line connected to the outputs of the component under test Model block, use the Property Inspector and select Show propagated signals.Alternatively, you can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: regionprops crashes MATLAB when the input label image is invalid
Description: regionprops  crashes MATLAB when using an invalid input label image with the 'Perimeter'  property: ? ? stats = regionprops(L, 'Perimeter')  The crash occurs if the input label image L  contains decimal numbers between 0 and 1, instead of only containing whole numbers.
Workaround: Only pass a valid label image to regionprops. A valid label image only contains whole, non-negative numbers and is of type double. In particular, be mindful of passing a grayscale image to regionprops when you mean to pass a logical image instead.

Summary: Test Manager report error for test cases using a logical parameter override
Description: Test Manager returns following error when generating a report if the test case used a logical (Boolean) parameter override: ?Could not append child which had existing parent

Summary: Digital DATCOM Forces and Moments?block outputs incorrect aerodynamic force and moment results when dynamic derivatives are?defined?in?Digital DATCOM structure parameter
Description: The Digital DATCOM Forces and Moments?block outputs incorrect aerodynamic force and moment results when a data structure that contains a non-zero value for dynamic derivative CLQ is specified for the?Digital DATCOM structure  parameter. The block incorrectly interprets the DATCOM derivative CLQ?as Clq (rolling-moment derivative due to pitch rate) instead of CLq (lift force derivative due to pitch rate).

Description: The generated code may contain an uninitialized local variable for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a Discrete State-Space block. Summary: A model reference block with Reset and Terminate ports could have the wrong ordering of its input ports
Description: On a model reference block using the Initialize/Reset/Terminate event?ports, incorrect ordering of the ports can be seen when all the following conditions are true: The names of any of the Reset events are sorted after 'Terminate' alphabetically (e.g., the Reset event 'undoChanges') Both the Reset event ports and Terminate event ports are enabled on the model reference block  In such cases, the Terminate and Reset event ?ports could incorrectly switch places on the model reference block. This could also potentially lead to incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: Change the names of the Reset Functions such that they would be sorted before 'Terminate' alphabetically.

Summary: intlinprog can return an?incorrect answer when bounds contain NaN
Description: If the lower bounds or upper bounds (lb  or ub)  contain NaN  entries,?intlinprog ?can return an incorrect answer. This problem occurs because intlinprog  does not check for NaN  entries in bounds.
Workaround: Ensure that the?bounds lb and ub do not contain any?NaN entries before calling intlinprog.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator can fail to detect unassigned property for object?in dynamically sized cell array?
Description: In some cases, the code generator fails to detect that a property of an object is?not assigned before it is used. This issue can occur in MATLAB Coder, and for?a MATLAB Function block or MATLAB System block in Simulink.? For code generation from MATLAB code, before you use a variable, you must assign a value to the variable. Similarly, before you use a property of an object, you must assign a value to the?property.?If a property is unassigned on some execution path, MATLAB treats the unassigned property as an empty matrix. The?code generator should treat the unassigned property as an error condition. However, in some cases, the code generator fails to detect that the property is unassigned. Therefore, it is possible that? the generated code?produces a wrong answer.?? Specifically, this problem can occur for a dynamically sized cell array of objects, with an element that is assigned from an object that has an unassigned property on some execution path. For example: ??function res = foo(n)?x = cell(1,n);?obj = MyClass;if n == 5?? obj.prop =? 1;end?for?i= 1:n?? x{i} = obj;?end?res = x{1}.prop;end   In this example, obj.prop  is not assigned when n  is not equal to 5. ?
Workaround: Make sure that? all object properties are assigned before the object is used.

Summary: Protected model Interface Report?might document parameters that are protected
Description: Parameters with storage class Auto  that are inlined by protected models into the generated code might be documented in the protected model Interface Report . These parameters do not form part of the model interface and should not be documented. Only parameters with a non-Auto  storage class should be documented in this report.
Workaround: Turn off report generation?using the 'Report' Name-Value pair argument in the?Simulink.ModelReference.protect API, by setting the value of 'Report' to false,?so that these parameters are not documented.?Alternatively, rename parameters to names that do not reveal intellectual property before generating the report.

Summary: Use before assignment of property in cell array sometimes not detected by code generator
Description: In some cases, the code generator fails to detect that a property of an object in a cell array is?not assigned before it is used. This issue can occur in MATLAB Coder and for?a MATLAB Function bock or MATLAB System block in Simulink.? For code generation from MATLAB code, before you use a variable, you must assign a value to the variable. Similarly, before you use a property of an object, you must assign a value to the?property. When an object is assigned (directly or indirectly) to a cell array element,?and the cell array is indexed with a variable, the code generator might fail to detect and report that a property is unassigned before use. For example, consider the following pseudocode:  if?condit ion   ??? obj.property = 1;?  end?  x = a cell array;  x{i} = obj;   use(x{i}.property);?  When the condition is false, obj.property  is unassigned. Therefore, the last statement reads an unitialized value. The code generator might fail to detect this error condition.
Workaround: Before you assign an object to a cell array element, assign values to all properties of the object.?

Summary: Simulink may crash while running the generated code from a model with?Simulink.LookupTable or Simulink.Breakpoint objects that?contain fi objects
Description: Simulink may crash while running the generated code?from a model with one or both of these conditions:  An n-D Lookup Table block that contains a Simulink.LookupTable   object: The Specification  parameter of the Simulink.LookupTable   object set to:?either?Explicit values  or Reference . The?Breakpoints > Value  property of the object contains a vector specified in the form of a fi object The Support tunable size  check box in the object is selected.  A Prelookup block that contains a Simulink.Breakpoint  object: The?Breakpoints > Value  property of the object contains a vector specified in the form of a fi object.  The Support tunable size  check box in the object is selected.?   ?  ?
Workaround: As a workaround, separate the fi object specification?into the?Value and DataType properties of the Simulink.LookupTable or Simulink.Breakpoint?object, for example:These?functions set up?a Simulink.LookupTable object with a fi object specified in?the lookup table object?Breakpoints Value property. This configuration?may cause the issue:>>?lutObject = Simulink.LookupTable;>> lutObject.Breakpoints.Value= fi([1,2,3,4], fixdt(1,8,1,0));To resolve the issue, separate the fi object?specification into the?Value?and?DataType?properties: >> lutObject.Breakpoints.Value= [1,2,3,4];?>> lutObject.Breakpoints.DataType= 'fixdt(1,8,1,0)';

Summary: MinGW compilation issue when building model reference simulation target with mixed C and C++ code
Description: When building a model reference simulation target that uses a mix of C and C++ code and the MinGW toolchain, the following compilation errors might occur: ?error: 'slmrAddResetEvents' was not declared in this scope ?error: 'slmrSetDataDictionarySet' was not declared in this scope? ?error: 'slmrSetHasSignalLogging' was not declared in this scope ?...

Summary: mldivide operator A\b gives inconsistent results between dense and sparse when matrix A is rectangular and contains non-finite values
Description: If a sparse rectangular matrix A  contains NaN  (Not-A-Number) values, then A\b  returns a vector that also contains NaN  values. This is the expected behavior of mldivide  regardless of whether A  is dense or sparse. However, if a dense rectangular matrix A  contains NaN  values, then A\b  returns a vector of all zeros. Additionally, a warning is displayed that says the matrix is rank deficient with rank = 0.

Summary: Export, evalin,?and save-to-source?operations in model workspace generate error?
Description: In R2017a, to identify a?variable or object?in a model workspace as a model argument, you use the Argument  check box in the Model Explorer.  After?you select or clear the check box for any variable in a model workspace, the model workspace generates an error when you?attempt any of these operations: Call?the evalin  method of a representative?Simulink.ModelWorkspace   object In the Model Explorer, export model workspace variables to a file, for example, by right-clicking the model workspace node and selecting Export  If you configure the model workspace to acquire data from an external MAT-file or script file, in the Model Explorer, save the contents of the workspace to the data source by clicking Save to Source
Workaround: Save and close the model.Reopen the model.Attempt the operation again. This time, the model workspace does not generate an error.

Summary: Configuration parameters defined in custom system target file might be lost after upgrade
Description: If you?save a configuration set with a custom system target file to a MAT file in?either R2015b or R2016a,?then load the file in either R2016b or R2017a,?access to the configuration set parameters defined in the custom system target file might result in?an error. For example, for a configuration set named MyProp , the error states: Property 'MyProp' does not exist   If you generate code for a model that uses such a configuration set, the parameters?are reset to the default values?as defined in the custom system target file, and the values stored in the original configuration set?are not used. If this happens, the Diagnostic Viewer displays this message: Target definition is different from that in the model. Updating target according to the new definition.  The following parameters were updated:?
Workaround: To get access to the configuration parameters defined in the custom system target file:In R2015b or R2016a, attach the configuration set to a model and save the model.In R2016b or R2017a, load the model and retrieve the configuration set.For example, if your configuration set config is saved in MAT file myconfig.mat, in R2015b or R2016a:>> load myconfig.mat>> new_system myconfig>> set(getActiveConfigSet('myconfig'),'Name','NotUsed')>> attachConfigSet('myconfig',config)>> setActiveConfigSet('myconfig',config.Name)>> save_system myconfig.slx?Then, in R2016b or R2017a:?>> load_system myconfig.slx>> config = getActiveConfigSet('myconfig')>> save myconfig.mat config

Summary: Charts created with yyaxis can display incorrectly when using a log scale along the x-axis
Description: When using the yyaxis  command, charts that use a log scale along the x-axis can display incorrectly. The issue can occur when the x-axis limits contain inf , -inf , or 0 . It can also occur when the x-axis limits contain both positive and negative values.? For example, this code plots two lines using a log scale along the x-axis. After you set the x-axis limits, the second line appears at an incorrect location along the x-axis. yyaxis left? semilogx([1 2],[1 2])? yyaxis right semilogx([3 4],[1 2]) xlim([0 4]);?  ?
Workaround: When using yyaxis, do not set the limits of a log scale chart to inf, -inf, or span 0. Let MATLAB choose the x-axis limits automatically or choose limits that are finite and do not span 0. For example:?xlim auto xlim([0.1 10])

Summary: Error when Merge block is driven by a variant subsystem in simplified initialization mode
Description: In Simplified Initialization mode, a Merge block can only be driven by inputs coming from conditionally executed subsystems. If all the variants of a variant subsystem are conditionally executed subsystems, they should be allowed to drive a Merge block. However, such variant subsystems (which contain only conditionally executed subsystems as the variant choices), are not allowed to drive Merge blocks and Simulink displays an error.
Workaround: Ensure that the conditionally executed subsystem layer is outside the variant subsystem, and that the inputs to the merge block are coming from such conditionally executed subsystems and not from the variant subsystem itself.?

Summary: Pattern for the Lognormal distribution produces code with a function name spelt incorrectly
Description: For SimEvents blocks with the option for writing custom code for Event Actions, there is a push-button in that tab that allows?you to choose from a set of random distributions. When you choose a distribution, the relevant MATLAB code for that distribution is auto-populated in the Event Action editor. For the Lognormal  distribution, the code that is produced has a typo for a function name. The function lognrnd  ?is mi s-spelled as logrnd  .

Summary: Errors or warnings about system time zone thrown on Linux
Description: If you run MATLAB on Linux and you create a datetime array, then it might throw errors or warnings that refer to the system time zone setting. These errors or warnings can occur even when the system time zone is set correctly. You might see an error message similar to: Error using datetime (line 503)ICU error: get canonical time zone ID, err='U_ILLEGAL_ARGUMENT_ERROR'.   Or a warning message similar to: Warning: The system time zone setting, 'US/Eastern', does not specify a single time zone unambiguously. It will be treated as 'America/New_York'. See the datetime.TimeZone property for details about specifying time zones.   when your system's time zone is not set to US/Eastern . Also, if you use the?parpool   command, it might throw an error or warning such as those shown above. The Parallel Computing Toolbox uses the datetime class during initialization. This problem occurs when both of the following conditions are met on a Linux system: The TZ  environment variable is not set. The symbolic link for /etc/localtime  uses a relative path.  If you use the Linux timedatectl  command to set the system clock, then it can cause the second condition to be met. timedatectl  creates a relative path to a directory containing time zone information and links it to?/etc/localtime . Note:  If the TZ  environment variable is set to a deprecated form such as US/Eastern  or EST , then you also might see the above warning.?This bug report does not apply to that case. The warning in that case occurs because specifying the time zone in such a form is ambiguous.?Set TZ  to a time zone of the form America/New_York  instead. To see a complete list of time zones, use the timezones  function.
Workaround: To work around the bug described in this report, use either of the solutions described below:1) Set the TZ environment variable to your desired time zone before starting MATLAB. For example (in csh):% setenv TZ America/Detroit; matlabOr:2) Create a symbolic link for /etc/localtime?using an absolute path rather than a relative one. For example:% ln -sf /usr/share/zoneinfo/America/Detroit /etc/localtimeChanging the symbolic link requires administrator privileges.To see a complete list of time zones accepted by the datetime class, use the timezones function in MATLAB.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when applying a custom code replacement library that uses createCRLEntry without specifying conceptual output argument name
Description: Code generator can generate incorrect code when all of the following conditions are true: Code generation from a model or MATLAB code is configured for the code generator to use a custom?code replacement?library. The custom code replacement library contains at least one entry created with the?createCRLEntry ?function and, in that usage, at least one conceptual output argument does not have a name.?
Workaround: Update the code replacement library such that every entry created by createCRLEntry?does one of the following:In the call to?createCRLEntry, make sure all?conceptual output arguments have their names explicitly set (for example, y1,y2).After calling createCRLEntry, explicitly set the?IOType?property for each implementation argument.

Summary: Root-level I/O?with reusable custom storage class specification and Inline Invariant Signals parameter selected may result in incorrect generated code
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The signals that connect to the root-level input and output?ports have the same reusable custom storage?specification. A Constant block connects to the root-level output port that has?the reusable custom storage class specification. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization>Signals and Parameters  pane, the Inline Invariant Signals  parameter is selected.?
Workaround: To avoid this bug,?try one of the following:?1. On the Optimization>Signals and Parameters pane,?deselect the Inline Invariant Signals check box.2. Remove the reusable custom storage class specification on the root-level input and output ports.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrectgenerated code is possible with subsystem enabled for Minimize algebraic loop occurrences and Signal store reuse but disabled for Enableblock outputs
Description: When a model has a subsystem with Minimize algebraic loop occurrences   set, the generated code can improperly share the outputs of the subsystem with other variables. Thisrequires the model to enable Signal storage reuse , but disable Enable localblock outputs . In the attached example, the generated code first writes Out2  from OtherSS , but then overwrites with the output of G2  during the update of the atomic subsystem Minimize . void g1538395_step(void)   { ...  ? ?/* Constant: '<Root>/Constant2' incorporates:?? ?* ?Outport: '<Root>/Out2'??? */?? ?g1538395_OtherSS(1.0, &g1538395_Y.Out2);  ? ?/* Update for Atomic SubSystem: '<Root>/Minimize' */?? /* Outputs for Atomic SubSystem: '<S1>/SubsystemInner' */?? /* Outport: '<Root>/Out2' incorporates:?? ?* ?Constant: '<Root>/Constant'?? ?* ?Gain: '<S3>/G2'?? ?*/??? g1538395_Y.Out2 = 1.6;  ...
Workaround: Recommended workaround:Enable the model parameter Enable local block outputs. This is highly recommended because it generates more efficient code.Alternative workaround:Disable Minimize algebraic loop occurrences on the subsystems.Disable Signal storage reuse. This will result in considerably less efficient code.

Summary: COM server throughput is no more than one COM request per second
Description: MATLAB running as a COM server can only service one COM request per second. If the client application makes COM requests faster than once per second, then the client application may appear unresponsive.

Summary: Test harness creation with a Test Sequence block source aborts when the main model uses a configuration reference
Description: When you try to create a test harness with a Test Sequence block source for a component or a model with bus output, and the main model uses a configuration reference, the test harness creation fails with this error message: A configuration set reference does not allow writing to parameters in the source configuration set.
Workaround: R2017a: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.R2016b: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1671801?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2016b.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for a subsystem containing a State Control block set to Synchronous
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model meeting these conditions: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Signal Storage Reuse  parameter is selected. The model contains a subsystem. The subsystem contains a State Control block in which the State control  parameter is set to Synchronous . The subsystem also contains a Resettable Delay block. In the Resettable Delay block parameters dialog box, the Delay length  parameter has a value of 1 . The subsystem does not contain an Enable Port block. The input to the Resettable Delay block is also the input to a Delay, Integrator, or other block that only samples its input during update. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pack Boolean data into bitfields  parameter is deselected.  When the above modelling conditions exist, the reset of the Resettable Delay block may change the input signal, so that the other block that this signal feeds into may use the reset value rather than the correct value.
Workaround: You can change any of the conditions?to avoid the bug. ?However, the least impactful solution is?to take one of the following actions:Select the Pack Boolean data?into bitfields parameter.Remove the State Control block from the model.Do not directly share the Resettable Delay block input with another block in the same subsystem. Instead,?insert a Signal Conversion block and deselect the Block reduction parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: State variable accessed in both atomic subcharts and graphical functions in the same parent chart might be incorrectly eliminated
Description: Under these conditions, a state variable might be incorrectly optimized away in a Stateflow chart: (1) An atomic subchart and a graphical function co-exist in the same parent chart.? (2) The atomic subchart calls the graphical function.? (3) The state variable is written in both the atomic subchart and the graphical function.? (4) There are uses of the state variable inside the atomic subchart whose definitions come from the graphical function.?

Summary: Displaying a timeseries object generates an error when Time?is not of type double
Description: When you?create a timeseries  object, you can set its?Time  property to a data type that is not double . Although this creates the resulting object as expected, when you try to display the timeseries,   it might error on the command line.
Workaround: Do not display the timeseries object, and suppress the output when assigning its property values.For example, add a semicolon to the following command:?  t = timeseries(ones(1,1000),uint16(1:1000));

Summary: Refresh Data Types menu item in Ports and Data Manager does not refresh the dialog
Description: When you add a custom datatype to the workspace, and then click on the Refresh Data Types  menu item in the Ports and Data Manager for a Stateflow Chart, MATLAB Function, Truth Table, or State Transition Table block, the dialog does not refresh and the new datatype does not appear.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: isfloat(numerictype('single')) and isfloat(numerictype('double'))? code generation or simulation can produce?silent wrong answer
Description: Code generation or simulation can produce a?silent wrong answer in calls to isfloat(numerictype('single')) and? isfloat(numerictype('double')) .? It returns FALSE  when it should return TRUE .?  In workflows using the Simulink platform, this problem occurs when the code is used as part of model such as inside a MATLAB Function Block. Simulation also produces the problem. However,?if the code is used for model initialization?and always uses interpreted mode, the correct answer is produced. In workflows using the MATLAB-only platform,?code generation and accelerated simulations produce the wrong answer. Interpreted simulations produce the correct answer.  ???
Workaround: Suppose you have defined a numerictype named MyNumericType?Instead of calling????? isfloat(MyNumericType)Use???? isfloat(fi([],MyNumericType))?

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier does not produce results if you click the 'Highlight' button during analysis of a partially compatible model
Description: ?In R2016b ,  Simulink Design Verifier does not produce results if you perform these steps during analysis of a partially compatible model: In the Design Verifier Pane, clear the Automatic stubbing of unsupported blocks and functions  option. Invoke analysis from Analysis  ˇú   Design Verifier  sub-menu or using sldvrun  API with?the? 'showUI'   option set to 'true' . After the?compatibility check finishes, click the Continue  button in Simulink Design Verifier Results Summary  window. Click the?Highlight  button while analysis is running.  ? In R2017a ,  Simulink Design Verifier does not produce results if you perform these steps during analysis of a partially compatible model: In the Design Verifier Pane, clear the Automatic stubbing of unsupported blocks and functions  option. Invoke analysis using sldvrun  API with the  'showUI'   option set to 'true' . Click the?Highlight  button while analysis is running.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for Rate Transition blocks which display?buf with Optimize global data access?set to?Use global to hold temporary results
Description: For a model containing a Rate Transition block, the critical section of the generated code that protects a global buffer may be unsafe under the following conditions:?? Code Generation flag condition:?  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Optimize global data access  parameter?is set to Use global to hold temporary results .?   ?Rate Transition block conditions:? The input sample time of the Rate Transition block is?faster than the output sample time. The output sample time?is a multiple of the input sample time. The output of the Rate Transition block must connect to an output port directly. In the Rate Transition block parameters dialog box, the?Ensure data integrity during data transfer? parameter is selected. In the Rate Transition block?parameters dialog box, the?Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)? parameter is not selected. The Rate Transition block displays?buf ?on the block icon because in?the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the?Solver ?pane, you select?Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer? and?Whenever Possible? or?Never (minimum delay) ?for?Deterministic data transfer .?buf ?signifies that a model contains such a type of Rate Transition block.  In the incorrect generated code, the global variable may be localized outside of the protection code.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, change the Optimize global data access parameter is to None or Minimize global data access.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when?using an unassigned property of an object that was the result of calling a function
Description: The generated code may read uninitialized memory when the following conditions hold: The object is the output of?a function that was called with an input object. The input object has an unassigned property. Inside the function,?the output object was assigned?from the input object.  The expected behavior is an error. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for noncontiguous reads and writes to the same matrix for which there is data dependency
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for multiple reads and writes to the same matrix that meets these conditions: The matrix reads and writes are noncontiguous.? There is data dependency between the matrix reads and writes.? For each dimension, all of the reads and writes access the same number of matrix elements. For each dimension, all of the reads and writes use the same stride between indexed elements.  In the incorrect generated code, a single, nested for  loop is generated?for the multiple reads and writes to the data. In the correct generated code, there should be separate, nested?for  loops to properly represent the data dependency. See the attached Simulink model ebr_example_1536107  for example modeling patterns that match the above conditions. ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, modify the model so that the code generator places the?dependent reads and writes in separate functions.?If the reads and writes are spread across multiple Simulink blocks, you can achieve this by using an atomic subsystem that has the Function packaging parameter set to Reusable function?or Nonreusable function. For a MATLAB Function Block or MATLAB System Block, you?can achieve this by using functions with the specification coder.inline('never'). See the attached model ebr_workaround_1536107 for examples of these workarounds.

Summary: Analysis fails with error 'side-by-side configuration is incorrect' on Microsoft Windows?
Description: On Microsoft Windows, a Polyspace analysis fails immediately with the following error message in the Run Log  pane:?  "The application has failed to start because its side-by-side configuration is incorrect. Please see the application event log or use the command-line sxstrace.exe tool for more detail. "  ?When the "Microsoft Visual C++ 2005 Service Pack 1 Redistributable Package MFC Security Update" is not installed.  ?
Workaround: Close the?Polyspace interface.Install the?missing package?from the Microsoft web site.Restart the Polyspace interface.

Summary: In the Test Manager, after calling?copySimulationSettings(testCase, 1, 2), the Simulation Outputs section for Simulation 2 shows "No outputs available"?
Description: In the Test Manager, if you have a test file with an equivalence test case, and you call the Test Manager API copySimulationSettings(testCase, 1, 2)  on the test case, then in Simulation 2, the Simulation Outputs section always shows "No outputs available", even if there are signals listed in the same section in Simulation 1.
Workaround: To view the copied Simulation Outputs settings for Simulation 2, save, close, and reopen the test file in the Test Manager.Alternatively, you can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.?

Summary: DOM API cannot open PDF or?unzipped HTML?document using relative path
Description: The DOM API cannot open a PDF or unzipped HTML?document that has a relative output path. A relative path is a path?that contains two periods to indicate?a relative parent folder, for example '../../foo/MyDocument' .  ?For example, this script fails import mlreportgen.dom.*mkdir('foo/foo');cd('foo/foo');rpt = Document('../mydoc','pdf');append(rpt,'Hello');close(rpt);cd('../..'); with the error message Error using mlreportgen.dom.Document/openCannot create document package: createFolders failed: fl:filesystem:InvalidArgument
Workaround: To enable using a relative path, you can use the java.io.File?class to resolve?the path. For example,?% Create?two inner foldersmkdir('foo/foo');cd('foo/foo');% Set the output path relative to?the two?upper foldersoutputPath = '../../MyDocument';% Workaround: use java.io.File to resolve the relative pathfile=java.io.File(fullfile(pwd,outputPath));outputPath = char(file.getCanonicalPath);% Open the document with the resolved pathrpt = mlreportgen.dom.Document(outputPath,'pdf');append(rpt,'Hello');close(rpt);cd('../..');?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code may occur when a reusable custom storage class is specified at the input port and another signal except for the output port of a Model Reference block
Description: Incorrect generated code may occur for a model that meets these conditions:  The model contains a referenced model. Inside the Model Reference block, a reusable custom storage class is specified at the input port and another signal except for the one entering the output port. The signal entering the Model Reference block branches to another signal line. A block on the branched signal line executes after the Model Reference block.  The attached example model shows one such case. In g1534640_top , the referenced model and the Gain block use the Bias block output. Inside g1534640_bot , all the signals except for the one entering the output port contain the Simulink.Signal  object reuse . When the model executes, the Gain block gets the wrong value because the referenced model executes prior to the Gain block and overwrites the value of the reuse signal. The incorrect generated code is as follows:  void g1534640_top_step(void){ˇˇ int32_T i;ˇˇ for (i = 0; i < 256; i++) {ˇˇ ˇˇ reuse[i] = rtU.SigIn[i] + 2.0;? ?}? ?g1534640_bot(&rtY.SigOut[0]);? ?for (i = 0; i < 256; i++) {ˇˇ ˇˇ rtY.SigOut1[i] = 2.1 * reuse[i];ˇˇ }} ....void g1534640_bot(real_T rty_SigOut[256]){?? int32_T i; ?? for (i = 0; i < 256; i++) {   ???? reuse[i] = 3.0 * reuse[i];   ???? reuse[i] = reuse[i] + 11.0;   ???? rty_SigOut[i] = -reuse[i]; ? }} ˇˇ The code for the reference model g1534640_bot  modifies the reuse variable prior to the Gain block in the top model?using it. As a result, the value of rtY.SigOut1[idx]  is 2.1 * (3 * (rtU.SigIn[idx] + 2) + 11) ?when it should be?2.1 * (rtU.SigIn[idx] - 2).
Workaround: You can implement either of these workarounds:Remove the reusable custom storage class specification from the model reference input or the other signals inside the Model Reference block.Specify the?reusable custom storage class?on one of the model reference output ports.

Summary: fmincon does not compute finite differences in parallel even when the?UseParallel option is true
Description: The?fmincon  'sqp'   algorithm   does not compute finite differences in parallel even when the UseParallel   option is set to true . The other fmincon  algorithms do not have this problem.
Workaround: To have fmincon compute finite differences in parallel, set the UseParallel option to true and set the Algorithm option to either 'sqp-legacy' or 'interior-point'. For example,options = optimoptions('fmincon','Algorithm','sqp-legacy','UseParallel',true);?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer forsimulation and code generation?for Simulink.LookupTable objectreferencing Simulink.Breakpoint objects
Description: Possible wrong answer for simulation and code generation for a model containing these conditions: Interpolation Using Prelookup block with these settings Number of dimensions  parameter set to 2  or larger Specification  parameter set to Lookup   table   object  Specified Simulink.LookupTable  object has Specification  parameter set to Reference   Prelookup blocks with these settings are sources to the Interpolation Using Prelookup block Each block has Specification  parameter set to Breakpoint   object  Each specified Simulink.Breakpoint   object has Support Tunable Size  check box cleared  The name of the second or subsequent Simulink.Breakpoint  objects specified in the Interpolation Using Prelookup block Simulink.LookupTable  object?differs from the name of the Simulink.Breakpoint  object in the corresponding Prelookup block
Workaround: The name of the each Simulink.Breakpoint object specified in the Simulink.LookupTable object must be the same as the Simulink.Breakpoint object in the corresponding Prelookup block.

Summary: Updating diagram can cause crash after using custom storage class Reusable
Description: ?Updating a block diagram can cause MATLAB to crash if these conditions are true in the model: A root-level Outport block uses a storage class other than Auto  (the default). To apply a storage class to an Outport block, you use the Model Data Editor. A?signal in the model uses the custom storage class Reusable .?To apply Reusable  to a signal, you resolve the signal to a Simulink.Signal  object and apply Reusable   to the object.  ?

Summary: mlreportgen.dom.ScaleToFit format expands smaller images in PDF output
Description: ScaleToFit  format should scale an image to fit the page or table entry only if the image dimensions are bigger than the available space for rendering. Currently, this format also expands the smaller images in the PDF output. For example, import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('MyReport', 'pdf');open(d);append(d, Paragraph('Below is a 1in wide image with ScaleToFit format to be false'));image1 = append(d, Image(which('ngc6543a.jpg')));image1.Style = [image1.Style {ScaleToFit(false)}];image1.Width = '1in';image1.Height = [];append(d, PageBreak);append(d, Paragraph('Below is a 1in wide image with ScaleToFit format to be true'));image2 = append(d, Image(which('ngc6543a.jpg')));image2.Style = [image2.Style {ScaleToFit(true)}];image2.Width = '1in';image2.Height = [];close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may be incorrect for a Constant block with a sample time other than inf that branches to multiple destinations
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a Constant block that meets these conditions: The Constant block has a sample time other than inf .? The signal line that leaves the Constant block branches?to multiple destinations.? The Constant block?or one of its destinations has an asynchronous rate, or the?Constant block has a discrete rate, and one of its destinations has a faster discrete rate.?
Workaround: You can choose?either of these?two workarounds:In the Constant block parameters dialog box, set the Sample time parameter to Inf.??Place a Signal Conversion block at the output of the Constant block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box,?select Exclude this block from Block reduction optimization'.

Summary: Connecting an Outport block directly to an Inport block through a Bus Selector block can crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB can crash when all of these conditions exist: 1. An Outport block within a conditional subsystem is connected to an Inport block through one or more Bus Selector blocks.? 2. The Inport block outputs a nonvirtual bus. 3. ?There are no other nonvirtual blocks between the Inport and Outport blocks.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block before the Outport block. In the Signal Conversion block,?set?the Output parameter to Signal copy. Inserting this block?can result in an additional buffer in the generated code.

Summary: ROS functionality in MATLAB and Simulink can be slow on Windows 10 machines
Description: ROS-related functionality in MATLAB and Simulink can be slow on some Windows 10 machines. Although everything still works, the slowdown can be on the order of 10 - 20 seconds (compared to 3 - 5 seconds under normal conditions). This affects the following functionality: ? MATLAB rosinit function robotics.ros.Core class robotics.ros.Node class  Simulink When running a model containing ROS blocks in simulation When generating code for a model containing ROS blocks   This issue only occurs on Windows 10 machines, but not all Windows 10 installations exhibit this issue. Depending on your network configuration, you might or might not encounter this problem. Other Windows variants are not affected. There are no visible warning or error messages, but the delay between command execution and command completion is very noticeable.?
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Summary: Conflicting bus object definitions in multi-level Simulink models that use Model blocks with data dictionaries can cause MATLAB crash
Description: ?A crash can occur when all  of these conditions occur: You create a bus object in the base workspace with the same name as a bus object contained in a data dictionary. The parent model is configured to reference the workspace version of the bus object, while the child model is configured to reference the data dictionary version. The bus is then connected to an input port on the child model.  If the bus is nonvirtual, the duplicate definition is detected during the pre-compilation phase and an error is generated. However, if the bus is virtual, the conflict is not detected, and if the buses have different numbers of elements, MATLAB crashes due to a null pointer dereferencing attempt.
Workaround: Because?the bug is due to an undetected error in the model configuration, the workaround is to eliminate the duplicate bus definition from the model hierarchy.

Summary: Code generation of cell array index?with too many dimensions causes MATLAB crash during constant folding
Description: During code generation, a MATLAB crash can occur when?all of the following conditions are true: The number of dimensions of a cell array index?is greater than the number of dimensions of the cell array. In the index, all dimensions higher than the maximum dimension of the cell array have value 1.  The function that contains the cell array index is constant-folded.  For example,?in function foo1, the index into?cell array x specifies four dimensions, but x has only two dimensions.? function result = foox = {1 2 3 4 5};result = coder.const(foo1(x));function result = foo1(x)result = x{1,1,1,1};
Workaround: When constant folding a function that indexes a cell array, do not index with more dimensions than the number of cell array dimensions. For the example in the Problem section, replace x{1,1,1,1} with x{1,1}.function result = foox = {1 2 3 4 5};result = coder.const(foo1(x));function result = foo1(x)result = x{1, 1};

Summary: Model Advisor MAAB check Check for mixing blocks and subsystem incorrectly flags Simulink function input and output ports
Description: The Model Advisor check??Check for mixing blocks and subsystem  (Check ID: mathworks.maab.db_0143) verifies the model or subsystem to be in accordance with the MAAB guideline db_0143. This guideline states that each level of a model must be designed with blocks of the same level (for example, only subsystems or only basic blocks). When a Simulink system contains a Simulink Function, the Model Advisor check??Check for mixing blocks and subsystem  incorrectly reports the input and output ports of the Simulink function as a violation of this guideline. The correct behavior for this check is to ignore input and output ports of all types at any level in the model hierarchy.
Workaround: Add Simulink Function Input and Output port to the Model Advisor configuration as follows -Open the Simulink Model.Open Model Advisor on?model level.Select the check?Check for mixing blocks and subsystem under By Product > Simulink Verification and Validation > Modeling Standards > MathWorks Automotice Advisory Board ChecksClick on Configure hidden parametersIn the Model Advisor Configuration Editor, navigate to Check for mixing blocks and subsystem under By Product > Simulink Verification and Validation > Modeling Standards > MathWorks Automotice Advisory Board ChecksUnder Input Parameters,Change Standard to CustomClick the Add Button under Block type listIn the new row added to the list, enter ArgIn under BlockTypeUse step 6.2 above and add another row.Enter ArgOut under BlockType in the new row.Use step 6.2 above and add another row.Enter?FunctionCaller under BlockType in the new row.Click ApplyClick on SaveClose the Configuration Editor.Start Model Advisor on the model again and run the check?Check for mixing blocks and subsystem under By Product > Simulink Verification and Validation > Modeling Standards > MathWorks Automotice Advisory Board Checks

Summary: Non-tunable block parameter that uses model argument did not result in an error

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation versus code generation mismatch is possible for a referenced model that has an input that connects only to blocks that do not use their input values to calculate their outputs values during the same time step
Description: A mismatch between simulation and code generation results is possible for a model that contains a referenced model that meets the following conditions: The referenced model has a single output/update function, uses function?prototype control, or generates C++ code. A input to the referenced model connects only to blocks that do not use their input values to calculate?their output values during the same time step, such as Delay blocks or Integrator blocks. This input port is not associated with a function call port in the referenced model. The referenced model uses a shared global resource such as a global data store.  The above modeling pattern may cause the sorted order in simulation to be different than the generated code. This difference may cause a mismatch between simulation and code generation?results.? The attached example model top_ mismatch_modref  illustrates the simulation versus code generation mismatch. The atomic subsystem SubsysWDSM  reads from the global?data store GDSM . The referenced model modref_dsm  writes to GDSM . The input port InNDF  connects to a Unit Delay block.? During simulation, modref_dsm  executes prior to SubsysWDSM , so Out2  has the latest value that modref_dsm  writes to GDSM . During?code generation, SubsysWDSM  executes prior to modref_dsm , so that InNDF  uses the GDSM  value?prior to modref_dsm  updating it. As a result, in the generated code, the Out2  value is?shifted by 1 simulation timestep relative to its value in simulation.?
Workaround: You can workaround this issue by implementing one of the following workarounds:Set?block priorities so that execution order is consistent between simulation and code generation. Note any warning messages about priority not being satisfied. If code generation?cannot honor an order,?you will get?a warning but simulation?will still follow the order giving the simulation and code generation mismatch.Deselect the Single output/update function parameter, do not specify Function Prototype Control,?or do not choose the?C++ Language.Connect?the referenced model input port to a?Signal Conversion block and select?the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter. Connect the output of the Signal Conversion block to the block that does not use its input value to calculate its output value during the same time step.

Summary: ordschur and ordqz give wrong result if complex conjugate pairs are split between clusters
Description: When a Schur or QZ decomposition of a real matrix has a conjugate pair of eigenvalues, the reordering in ordschur  and ordqz  can only move both eigenvalues into the same cluster of eigenvalues. If the input argument CLUSTERS assigns two conjugate eigenvalues to different clusters, then a wrong reordering is returned.
Workaround: Verify if clusters has the same value for every conjugate pair of eigenvalues. Here T is the second input to ordschur; for ordqz, use the first input AA instead.conjPair = find(diag(T, -1));validInput = all(clusters(conjPair) == clusters(conjPair+1));Construct a valid clusters vector from an invalid one by using the cluster of larger index for each conjugate pair. Here T is the second input to ordschur; for ordqz, use the first input AA instead.conjPair = find(diag(T, -1));clusters(conjPair) = max(clusters(conjPair), clusters(conjPair+1));clusters(conjPair+1) = clusters(conjPair);

Summary: Simulation of a model with multiple identical subsystem instances containing algebraic loops might result in wrong answers in accelerator mode
Description: Simulation of a Simulink model might result in wrong answers under the following conditions: The?model contains multiple identical instances of a subsystem  These subsystems contain an algebraic loop inside them The model?is simulated?in accelerator mode  The wrong answers originate from the subsystems containing algebraic loops, with all but one of them giving wrong answers.
Workaround: Any of the following will resolve the issue:?Model is changed such that only one instance of the subsystem containing the algebraic loop is present.Model is changed such that algebraic loops are outside of subsystems.Model is changed such that algebraic loops are removed altogether.Model is run in normal mode instead of accelerator mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect behavior of code generated from MATLAB fprintf?function in Simulink with portable word sizes enabled
Description: When generating code from the MATLAB fprintf  function in Simulink, incorrect behavior might occur if all of the following conditions are true:   The Enable portable word sizes  check box is selected. (This option is available for ERT targets in the Code Generation > Verification  category.)  An integral type is printed using an integer format specifier, such as %d, %i, %u, %o, %x, %X.  The generated code uses? different underlying C types to represent the argument to  fprintf  on the host machine and on the target machine. For example, an  int32  is passed to  fprintf  and the generated code represents 32-bit integers using the C type  long  in the production hardware and represents 32-bit integers using  int  in the host hardware.  The generated code is compiled and executed on the host computer. This issue does not occur when the generated code is executed in the target execution environment.   The following example illustrates this issue. Suppose that:  A?MATLAB Function block contains the code:   f = fopen(fname,'a+'); y = fprintf(f,'%d\n',x); fclose(f);   The Enable portable word sizes  check box is selected. The?production hardware has these integer sizes (in bits):   char: 16short: 16int: 16long: 32  The host machine has these integer sizes (in bits):    char: 8short: 16int: 32long: 64  The code generated for the preceding call to fprintf  will be:  fprintf(filestar, "%ld\n", x);   where x  has type  int32_T . When compiled for the host hardware,  int32_T  is mapped to the C type  int. W hen compiled for the target hardware, it is mapped to the C type  long .  The presence of the 'l'  prefix in the format specifier causes the input to fprintf  to be interpreted as a 64-bit integer on the host machine rather than a 32-bit integer. This discrepancy?can cause incorrect behavior.  Given this situation, you might see printed values such as:  simData = '-100           2147483647          'rtwData = '4294967196           2147483647        ? '   simData  is the correct output and  rtwData  is the incorrect output during host execution.
Workaround: Take any of these steps:Convert the input to fprintf?to a MATLAB integral type which has the same C type representation on both the host and target hardware.Convert the input to?fprintf?to a floating-point type such as double or?single,?and use a floating-point format specifier like '%g'?or?'%f'.Clear the Enable portable word sizes check box and verify the generated code in the target execution environment.
