Summary: Opening the Symbols?window for a chart containing multiple exported functions can crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB crashes when you open the Symbols window for a Stateflow chart containing more than one exported function. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [1] SLFunctionGraphCatalogMI::findVisibleFunctions (in \matlab\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll)?[2] SLFunctionGraphCatalogMI::invoke (in \matlab\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll)
Workaround: Use the Model Explorer to manage Stateflow objects in charts containing multiple exported functions.

Summary: Precision mismatch between simulation and code generation results in the Lookup Table Dynamic, Lookup Table (obsolete), and Lookup Table (2-D) (obsolete) blocks
Description: Simulation and code generation results from the Lookup Table Dynamic,?Lookup Table (obsolete), and Lookup Table (2-D) (obsolete) blocks? may have a precision mismatch for?some input values when: The?Lookup Method ?parameter is set to either Interpolation-Extrapolation  or Interpolation-Use End Values . The datatype of any of input, breakpoints, table, or output is double.  The precision difference is because?simulation and code generation use different formulas to calculate the interpolation output. Formula for simulation:? y = (1-f)*T1 + f*T2   Formula for code generation:  y =?T1 + f*(T2-T1)?   Where:  y  is the output f ? is the?fraction T  is?the?table value selected by the input and the breakpoints   Simulink prefers the code generation formula for blocks with these settings.

Summary: Undefined function errors when restoring a model definition view
Description: When you restore?the model definition view, undefined functions can cause MBC Model Fitting  app errors. ?
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results in Accelerator and SIL modes when Model block references a JMAAB-B model
Description: Model simulation may produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Model block? The referenced model contains a root Inport block with parameter?Output function call ?selected? The root Inport block is connected to an Asynchronous Task Specification block. The combination of these two blocks constitutes the modeling style known as JMAAB-B. In the top model, the function-call input port for the Model block corresponding to the above root Inport block in the referenced model is driven by a function-call initiator block, such as a Function-Call Generator block, that is placed inside an Enabled Subsystem. The simulation mode of either the top model or the Model block is a mode other than Normal mode.  If you try to simulate a model as described above, but in Normal mode, Simulink throws an error. In other modes, no error is thrown, and simulation results may be incorrect.

Summary: Generation of HDL Coder code?generation report might fail for long folder names
Description: If the code generation folder name is too long, generation of the HDL Coder code generation report might fail with the following error:? "Warning: Cannot create webview report, because the system cannot find the path specified. "

Summary: HDL code generation takes time for large lookup tables
Description: If certain optimizations are enabled on a Simulink model or subsystems that contain Lookup table blocks with large 'Table Data', HDL code generation can take time during model generation.
Workaround: Disable all optimizations on the Simulink model or subsystems that contain large Lookup table blocks,? Examples of optimizations that you can disable include clock-rate pipelining and hierarchy flattening.

Summary: ImageDatastore can not read past corrupt images on Windows? computers
Description: On a Windows? computer, when ImageDatastore  encounters a corrupt image, it is unable to proceed further with the read command, and does not allow removal of the corrupt file.  For instance, create an ImageDatastore  with a single image: copyfile(fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox','matlab','imagesci','peppers.png'),'peppers.png');  Create an empty image in this location from the Command Window (or terminal):? touch foo.jpg  Create an ImageDatastore  with these 2 files:? imds = imageDatastore('.');  Since "foo.jpg " is a corrupt file, reading from the datastore will throw an error: read(imds)  Also, attempting to delete the file will display the following warning:? "Warning: File not found or permission denied"  The corrupt file can neither be deleted from MATLAB, nor from the Command Window. The datastore leaves the file handle open when it attempts to read the file. The only solution is to exit MATLAB, at which point the file handle is released, and then the file can be deleted. ?
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1532495 to download a patch that addresses this and other bugs that exist in R2016b.

Summary: Error when converting from atomic subchart to normal subchart
Description: When converting an atomic subchart back to a normal state and a parameter in the atomic subchart is mapped to a name that matches with an object in the main chart, the following error is thrown to the MATLAB Command Window.  Error using containers.Map/subsref The specified key is not present in this container.?  For example, if the main chart has an output data named y , and the parameter in the atomic subchart is mapped to a workspace variable that is also named  y , the error will occur.

Summary: Code generation?with preprocessor conditions may?fail for a model that?has a?Variant Subsystem with only one variant choice
Description: The build procedure may abort due to a syntax error for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Variant Subsystem that has only one variant choice. The model?contains at least one Variant block with the Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter selected.

Summary: Line drawing fails for lines that have more than 25,000 data points
Description: Line drawing might fail?for lines that have?more than 25,000 data points?when the Projection  property of the Axes  is set to 'perspective' . The following warning message displays: Warning: Error updating Line. Attempt to project a Point4d with W==0
Workaround: Do either of the following:Specify less than 25,000 data points.Remove all data points that have the same coordinates as the CameraPosition property of the Axes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The Pulse Generator block could output incorrect pulses for simulation and generated code
Description: The Pulse Generator block could output incorrect pulses for simulation and generated code when the following conditions are met, 1. ?The Pulse Generator block has 'Pulse type' set to be 'Time based'. 2. The value of the parameter 'Period' is not exact multiples of the solver's step. For example, it is set to be '5e-5' while the solver step is '1e-6', or the calculated 'Pulse Width' is not multiples of the solver step. Given the above, the output pulse could have wrong period or pulse width.

Summary: Incorrect uppercase <PREFIX> for generated AUTOSAR compiler abstraction macro
Description: If you select the model configuration option Use AUTOSAR compiler abstraction macros  for a model, the <PREFIX> in the generated C macros incorrectly uses uppercase rather than mixed case.? For example, if the model contains an AUTOSAR software component named simpleSwc , the code generator generates a function prototype similar to: FUNC(void, SIMPLESWC_CODE) Runnable_Step_Func(void) The generated function prototype should be: FUNC(void, simpleSwc_CODE) Runnable_Step_Func(void)

Summary: Signal Conversion blocks placed on a?rate transition boundary?can crash Simulink during update diagram
Description: This situation happens when the downstream of?a Signal Conversion block has a different rate, and? the configuration parameter for Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is enabled .
Workaround: Disable the Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer configuration parameter in the Additional options section of the Solver pane.??

Summary: Incorrect generated compiler abstraction macro for AUTOSAR model with constant or ground blocks
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR model might generate incorrect compiler abstraction macros if both of these conditions exist: The model contains a constant or ground block. Model configuration option Use AUTOSAR compiler abstraction macros  is selected.  Under these conditions, the generated C code uses a VAR macro rather than a CONST macro.? For example, the build generates: VAR(gndName, swc_VAR) mdlName_rtZgndName =   { ?? 0.0F,?????????????????????????????? /* Element1 */ ?? 0.0F??????????????????????????????? /* Element2 */   } ;??????????????????????????????????? /* gndName ground */   rather than: CONST(gndName, swc_VAR) mdlName_rtZgndName =   { ?? 0.0F,?????????????????????????????? /* Element1 */ ?? 0.0F??????????????????????????????? /* Element2 */   } ;??????????????????????????????????? /* gndName ground */
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Sine HDL Optimized block outputs incorrect waveform
Description: The Sine HDL Optimized block will not output the expected waveform when the block dialog setting Simulate RAM Delay  is enabled. When this block dialog setting is disabled (which is the default behavior), the block simulates correctly.

Summary: Very slow to close model comparison report
Description: On Windows, closing a model comparison report can take several minutes. MATLAB continues to respond, and the report will close eventually.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: The max and min functions with 'includenan' or 'omitnan' option and empty second argument return arrays of the wrong size
Description: When using the nanflag  option with the max  function, where nanflag  is either 'includenan'  or 'omitnan' , the syntaxes ? max(a,b)  and max(a,b,nanflag)   can result in different outputs when b  is empty, even if no NaN  values are present. ?This occurs because max ?ignores b  and returns the largest element of a ,? instead of comparing them element-wise.  The same issue applies to the min  function.? For example, the following two commands yield different results: >> max(3,zeros(2,0))  ans =? 2ˇÁ0 empty double matrix   >> max(3,zeros(2,0),'includenan')ans =???? 3
Workaround: The following function returns the element-wise comparison between inputs a and b, and does not ignore NaN values in a or b. ?Replace max with min to apply the workaround to the min function.?function c = elementwiseMaxMissing(a,b,nanflag) if isempty(b)? ? if isempty(a)??????? % nanflag irrelevant if both inputs are empty??????? c = max(a,b);??? else??????? % avoid empty second input by switching input order??????? c = max(b,a,nanflag); ??? endelse??? c = max(a,b,nanflag);end

Summary: Compilation error when using #include <type_traits> and around __is_assignable intrinsic
Description: When you use polyspace-configure to create a Polyspace Bug Finder project from a Visual Studio 2015 solution and then run analysis, the compilation phase fails?with the following error message: C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio 14.0\VC\include\type_traits, line 524: error: identifier "__is_assignable" is undefined|????????????????? : integral_constant<bool, __is_assignable(_To, _From)>|??????????????????????????????????????????? ^C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio 14.0\VC\include\type_traits, line 524: error: type name is not allowed|????????????????? : integral_constant<bool, __is_assignable(_To, _From)>|??????????????????????????????????????????????????????????? ^

Summary: Model build may fail for Variant Subsystem containing blocks with Simulink.Parameters or mpt.Parameters with ImportedDefine storage class
Description: For a model?with a Variant Subsystem containing blocks that have Simulink.Parameters or mpt.Parameters  with an ImportedDefine  storage class,?the model build may fail because of an error pointing to undefined variables in the model_types.h  file. This error may occur because the Variant Control parameter definitions are in a separate header file or have different storage classes than the Simulink.Parameters or mpt.Parameters? specified for the block parameters.
Workaround: You can workaround this issue setting the storage class of variant control variables as ImportedDefine and placing all ImportedDefine variable definitions in the same header file.

Summary: Parsing an XCP A2L file might crash MATLAB
Description: Some XCP A2L files, depending on their content and improper encoding per the ASAM XCP A2L standard, might crash MATLAB when attempting to parse them. This can happen when using the xcpA2L  function in MATLAB or the XCP blocks in a Simulink model.

Summary: MATLAB throws an error when trying to generate code for a model with reusable subsystem connected to a block using TLC
Description: MATLAB throws an error when you try to generate code for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains at least one reusable subsystem The reusable subsystem has one of its outputs branched and connected to a block that uses TLC for code generation The block has reference to its input signal in its TLC implementation

Summary: Additional latency incurred when HDL Coder is unable to absorb the delays at the output of those blocks whose HDL implementation requires delays
Description: For any blocks that require additional latency in the HDL implementation, such as Filters and Trigonometric blocks, HDL Coder is unable to absorb the delays at the output of these blocks. This results in an additional latency being incurred in the design.
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow the instructions below to install the attached file depending on the release version. The attachment also addresses bugs from previously published bug reports:?Bug Report ID 1521143 Bug Report ID?1480618Bug Report ID?1488778Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Occasional crash on Linux involving freetype
Description: On Linux, MATLAB might occasionally crash with a stack trace that contains the following lines:  [  0] 0x00007fc885ba77ec [unknown function] at /usr/lib/jvm/default/lib/amd64/server/libjvm.so+6379495 (file not found) [  1] 0x00007fc807db3a91 [unknown function] at /usr/lib/jvm/java-7-openjdk/jre/lib/amd64/libfontmanager.so+35468 (file not found) [  2] 0x00007fc83efdb8d6 FT_Stream_Free at <matlabroot>/bin/glnxa64/libfreetype.so.6+88273 [  3] 0x00007fc83efddc33 destroy_face at ftbase.c:0 (in <matlabroot>/bin/glnxa64/libfreetype.so.6) [  4] 0x00007fc83efddd41 FT_Done_Face at <matlabroot>/bin/glnxa64/libfreetype.so.6+97596 [  5] 0x00007fc807db576a Java_sun_font_FreetypeFontScaler_disposeNativeScaler+00000042 at /usr/lib/jvm/java-7-openjdk/jre/lib/amd64/libfontmanager.so+42853 (file not found)  ?
Workaround: This is a rare occurrence involving an issue with Java. We believe you can work around this issue by upgrading to Java 1.7.0 update 80 (7u80), which you can download from Oracle.? While MathWorks has not qualified MATLAB to work with this update, we?do not expect issues. For more information about using a version of Java other than the one that ships with MATLAB, see this MATLAB Answer.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For code generation, assigning the result of coder.nullcopy to an object property may produce incorrect code
Description: If?you generate code from ?MATLAB code that contains the pattern o.p = coder.nullcopy(...) , then the generated code?may produce?a?wrong answer. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When replacement function has more than one caller, code replacement?data alignment may not align?data at?requested boundary
Description: The code generator might not align data at the requested boundary for code replacement data alignment when all of these conditions are met: The generated code uses the implementation function of a code replacement library entry with one or more arguments that require data alignment at specific memory boundaries.? The implementation function of the code replacement library entry is called from multiple places in the generated code.??  If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric results or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly,?the generated code can produce numeric results that differ from simulation results or result in crashes when all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: Traceability matrix XLS file does not include links from custom library blocks to requirement documents???
Description: If you generate an XLS  file that contains a traceability matrix, the file does not?include links from custom library blocks to requirements documents.??
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: nargin and nargout return 0 when applied to functions that are not supported for code generation
Description: In the generated code, nargin  and nargout  return 0 when applied to unsupported functions. For example, consider this function: function [x, y] = foox = nargin(@plot);y = nargout(@plot); Because plot  is not supported for code generation, x  and y  are 0.
Workaround: Declare nargin and nargout as extrinsic when using them with?unsupported functions. Call these extrinsic versions with the name of the function, rather than the function handle (function handles are not supported as arguments to extrinsic functions). For example, rewrite:x = nargin(@plot)ascoder.extrinsic('nargin');x = nargin('plot');Note that declaring these functions as extrinsic affects other uses of these functions in the same enclosing function.?

Summary: Text disappears from 3-D view after changing position property and rotating the view
Description: Text might disappear from 3-D views when the view is rotated after the Position  property of a Text  object is changed in either of the following ways:  You use plotedit , propedit , or an item from the figure's Edit  menu to change the?Text  object's position while the Units  property is set to 'data' . You edit the Position  property propgrammatically with Units  set to 'pixels' , and then change the Units  property to 'data' .

Summary: ?Simulating accelerated model reference?models with MATLAB structure parameters and unoriented Simulink Bus vector elements can fail
Description: ?Accelerated model reference fails to simulate when all  of these conditions exist: The reference model uses a MATLAB?structure that contains vector elements (for example, the dimension is [1 3] or [3 1]).? The corresponding Simulink Bus object matches the structure parameter, except that vector elements are specified unoriented (for example, "3ˇ°).?? The bus goes through a Unit Delay block, with MATLAB structure specified as the initialization value, or the MATLAB structure is used in a constant block with the Simulink Bus specified as its output type.  However, these conditions do not cause a failure for normal mode simulation.?
Workaround: Make sure that vector Simulink Bus elements are not specified unoriented and always match the orientation of its associated MATLAB structure value.

Summary: Maximum identifier length?is not honored for a linked Box
Description: When you generate code for a linked Stateflow Box ?referenced to a library block , the value entered in Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Symbols > Maximum identifier length ?isignored and a value of 256 is used.

Summary: Running the Upgrade Advisor API with OneLevelOnly option runs non-compile checks only
Description: If you use the upgradeadvisor  function to create an upgrader  object, and set the the OneLevelOnly  property to true, then only the non-compile checks run when you execute analyze(upgrader)  or upgrade(upgrader) . No checks run that require compiled information.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. Summary: Generated code might be?uncompilable for referenced models with MATLAB structure parameters
Description: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code for referenced models that meet the following conditions:  Child and parent models use the same parameter that is a MATLAB structure. In the child model, the structure elements are assigned to a Simulink.Bus  object with matching structure elements. This is the only reference to the Simulink.Bus  object in the model hierarchy.
Workaround: For the model parameter, use a Simulink.Parameter instead of a MATLAB structure . The Simulink.Parameter should have the MATLAB structure as its value and the Simulink.Bus object as its type.

Summary: Code generation may fail for a MATLAB Function block assignment to a bus signal containing dynamically-sized matrices
Description: Code generation may fail for a model that meets all of these conditions: A bus signal enters a MATLAB Function block. The bus signal contains dynamically-sized matrices meaning that variables define the upper bounds of the matrices. The?MATLAB Function block uses an assignment statement to assigns values to the bus signal elements.  Code generation fails with an error message similar?to the following:?   failed assertion inb:\?matlab\?src\?cgir_xform\?dom_core\?lowering\?dynamicmatrixtypeloweringimpl.cppat line 897: !aRequirements.requireElemsSize()
Workaround: Use a for loop instead of an assignment?statement to assign values to the elements of dynamically-sized matrices in a bus signal.For example, history_of_alarms_out is a dynamically sized matrix of size number_of_alarms in an array of buses. Instead of using?the following?assignment statement to assign values to history_of_alarms_out: history_of_alarms_out(1:number_of_alarms,burst_idx) = alarm_list( 1:number_of_alarms);Use?the following?for loop:for i = 1:number_of_alarms ? ?history_of_alarms_out(i,burst_idx) = alarm_list(i);end?

Summary: Datetime constructed with 'now' incorrectly sets time during the last hour of daylight saving time
Description: If you create a datetime  using 'now'  to specify the current date and time, and the current time is within the last hour of daylight saving time in the specified time zone, then the datetime  function sets the time as standard time, not daylight saving time. For example, create a datetime  specifying 'now'  and the local time zone on a system whose date and time?is 2016-11-06 1:00 AM EDT. The time is the last hour before the end of daylight saving time in this time zone. d = datetime('now','TimeZone','local','Format','uuuu/MM/dd HH:mm:ss zzz')   This code returns the datetime  value: d =? datetime?06-Nov-2016 01:00:00 EST ?datetime  creates the time in EST instead of EDT, skipping the last hour of daylight saving time. This happens only when you specify 'now'  and specify the TimeZone  property as a time zone that observes daylight saving time.

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler (MJS) can become unresponsive
Description: After extended operation, MATLAB Job Scheduler (MJS) can become unresponsive. Operations such as cluster discovery, parcluster, and nodestatus hang indefinitely. This also causes the MATLAB client to block, requiring it to be restarted.
Workaround: To recover from this problem, restart MJS. You must do this from the command line as Admin Center is also affected by this problem. On the head node run the following commands.Linux/Mac OS:?cd <matlabroot>/toolbox/distcomp/binsudo ./mdce stopsudo ./mdce start?Windows: ?cd <matlabroot>/toolbox/distcomp/binmdce stopmdce start??Here <matlabroot> is the root of your MATLAB Distributed Computing Server installation. Note that it will take some time for the job manager and workers to restart.?

Summary: DOM API?sets empty table?row height to zero in?PDF
Description: The DOM API renders an empty?table?row?differently in?PDF than it does in Word and HTML.?In PDF, the API sets the height of the empty row to zero. In HTML?and DOCX, the API sets the row?height to the table font size. For example, the following script generates a table that appears to have only two rows because two of its four rows are empty and therefore have zero height. import mlreportgen.dom.*  data = { ...  ? ? '1', '1', '1'; ...  ? ? [], [], []; ...  ? ? [], [], []; ...  ? ? '4', '4', '4'};    rpt = Document('output','pdf');  tbl = append(rpt,data);  tbl.RowSep = 'solid';  tbl.ColSep = 'solid';  close(rpt);  rptview(rpt.OutputPath);
Workaround: Add a nonbreaking space character (shown as <nbsp> in the example) to an entry in the empty table row. For example:?import mlreportgen.dom.*data = { ...? ? '1', '1', '1'; ...? ? <nbsp>, [], []; ...? ? [], [],?<nbsp>; ...? ? '4', '4', '4'};rpt = Document('output','pdf');tbl = append(rpt,data);tbl.RowSep = 'solid';tbl.ColSep = 'solid';% Workaround: Append a non breaking space to the empty table entriesnbsp = char(160);?for row = tbl.Children? ? for entry = row.Children? ? ? ? if isempty(entry.Children)? ? ? ? ? ? append(entry,nbsp);? ? ? ? end? ? endendclose(rpt);rptview(rpt.OutputPath);??

Summary: Unused variable compiler warning is possible for a model containing Variant Source or Variant Sink blocks
Description: The generated code may contain unguarded local variable definitions for a model containing Variant Source or Variant Sink blocks. These unguarded local variable definitions are possible for the following blocks: Model blocks S-functions Stateflow Charts or MATLAB Function blocks that?have Function Packaging  of Reusable function  Blocks for?viewing simulation results such as?Scope?and Display blocks  The warning?may state that a variable is unused or that a variable is set?but unused. The exact wording of the warning depends on the C compiler.
Fixed In: R2016a Update 7, R2016b Update 6, R2017a, R2017b

Summary: Uncompilable code for a Stateflow chart?inside?of a?Variant subsystem
Description: The code generator may generate uncompilable code for a model that meets the following conditions:? The model contains a Stateflow?chart. In the Block Parameters dialog box, on the Code?Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Reusable function .? The Stateflow chart is inside of?a Variant subsystem.  In the uncompilable code, the chart's initialization function contains initialization statements for variables that are local to the output function and not the initialization function. The compiler error occurs because the initialization function does not contain the declarations for these variables.? ?
Workaround: For the Stateflow chart, change the function packaging parameter?to Nonreusable function or Inlined.?

Summary: Deleted jobs continue to take up disk space in the job manager database for MJS clusters
Description: Even after you delete jobs, the job manager database for MJS clusters might not get smaller, especially if a job stores a large amount of data in its input or output arguments. Over time, the job manager database can grow to a very large size even though it contains little or no data. You can see this problem if, after deleting all jobs on your MJS cluster and stopping your job manager, a large number of .temp.lob.db files remain in the folder LOGBASE/YOURJOBMANAGERNAME_jobmanager_storage/database.lobs.db. LOGBASE and YOURJOBMANAGERNAME are defined in your mdce_def file.
Workaround: There are two workarounds.Periodically clean the job manager databaseIf you notice your job manager database is growing too large and are OK with stopping the job manager and deleting all of its data, to correct the problem:1) Destroy the job manager by selecting "MJS > Destroy..." in AdminCenter or calling the "stopjobmanager" script with the "-clean" flag. ?WARNING: This irrecoverably deletes all jobs in the database.2) Start a new job manager with AdminCenter or using the "startjobmanager" script.You might have to repeat these steps periodically or when your new job manager database grows too large.Upgrade the job manager database to the latest stable versionIf your job manager database is growing too quickly or too often for the workaround above to be practical, try upgrading the job manager database to the latest stable version. ?The latest version of the database automatically frees the disk space used by deleted jobs when the job manager is stopped.1) Download the zip file http://www.h2database.com/h2-2014-04-05.zip, which contains the latest H2 database.2) Open the .zip file and extract the file h2/bin/h2-1.3.176.jar to a folder of your choice.3) Rename the extracted h2-1.3.176.jar file to h2.jar.4) Stop your job manager and delete all of its data by selecting "MJS > Destroy" in AdminCenter of calling the "stopjobmanager" script with the "-clean" flag. ?WARNING: This irrecoverably deletes all jobs in the database.5) On the headnode that contains the job manager, replace the h2.jar file in matlab/java/jarext/distcomp/ with the one from step 3).6) Start a new job manager with AdminCenter or using the "startjobmanager" script.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Global storage class with alias on reusable subsystem output signal may?cause incorrect code generation
Description: For a model that meets the following conditions, the code generator might generate incorrect code for the reusable subsystem output function: The model contains at least two instances of the same reusable subsystem. Both reusable subsystem instances has an output signal annotated with the same global storage class The global storage class has a different alias for each reusable subsystem instance.

Summary: Closing a Simulink test harness can?crash MATLAB
Description: Closing a test harness crashes MATLAB in thisscenario: A subsystem that has a harness contains a Stateflow chart.  The chart is open in its own Simulink Editor tab.  You open the subsystem harness.   When you return to the model (close theharness), the crash occurs.
Workaround: If a chart is open in its own tab, close the tab before you open?the harness on the parent subsystem.

Summary: In MATLAB Function Block, using concatenation to initialize a dynamic matrix generates incorrect answers
Description: In MATLAB Function Block or MATLAB script, when you initialize a dynamic matrix, such as: y1 = [y1; [1 2 3 4] ];  the code generator generates incorrect dimensions that result in wrong answers. The concatenation used for initializing values into y1? generates incorrect dimension information. It also initializes y1  with incorrect values.  This bug?might occur when you write a horizontal concatenation expression that causes read-write issue of initializing memory for a dynamically allocated matrix.
Workaround: Quick workaround is to change the order of variables in the concatenation expression, such as:y1 = [ [1,2,3,4]; y1]

Summary: Acquisition might error when using macvideo?adaptor with imaging cameras on a Mac
Description: If you use the macvideo  adaptor on a Mac machine, you might encounter an error with the following message after acquisition is started:Unable to allocate memory for an incoming image frame due to insufficient free physical memory.
Workaround: To work around this problem, do not limit the physical memory on MAC machines. Execute the following commands before using Image Acquisition Toolbox functions in a specific MATLAB session:imaqresetimaqmex('feature','-limitPhysicalMemoryUsage',false);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible when you use the same reusable custom storage class?specification on the input and output signals of a Unit Delay block
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model in which you use the same reusable custom storage class specification?on the input and output signals of a Unit Delay block (or a Delay block with a Length ?of 1 ) and on?a third?signal. For a model in which the bug occurs, the code generator cannot reuse the same variable name due to conflicting uses. The code generator renames and declares one of the signals but does not use the new name later on in the generated code. The incorrect generated code still uses the user-specified name for all three signals. For example, the attached model has a reusable custom storage class named reuse. For this model, th  e correct generated code?is as follows:? ? real_T reuse_0;  real_T reuse;  ...  void bug1416636_step(void)  {  ? bug1416636_Y.Out1 = reuse_0 + 3.0;  ? reuse = 2.0 * reuse;  ? reuse_0 = bug1416636_U.In1 + reuse;  }? ? The variable reuse_0  is the renamed signal. Note that this signal is declared and used correctly in the generated code In the incorrect generated code, reuse_0  is declared but not correctly used as follows: ? real_T reuse_0;  real_T reuse;  ...  void bug1416636_step(void)  {  ? bug1416636_Y.Out1 = reuse  + 3.0;  ? reuse = 2.0 * reuse;  ? reuse  = bug1416636_U.In1 + reuse;  }? ? Note that this bug does not occur on?a consistent basis. For a model?that has?the bug, you can generate correct code?multiple times in a row and then generate incorrect code. ?
Workaround: Do not use the same reusable custom storage class?specification?on both the input and output signals of a Unit Delay block (or a Delay Block with a Length equal to 1). If you want?to?specify?buffer reuse?for the input and output signals of a Unit Delay block, put the reusable custom storage specification on either the input or output signal, but not on both signals. The code generator still attempts to use the same buffer for the Unit Delay input and output even without specification.

Summary: Performance regression when virtual buses cross model reference boundaries
Description: When generating a model reference SIM Target or RTW Target for a model that has virtual buses at the root inports or outports, performance can regress, compared to releases before R2016a. The performance regression can occur when virtual buses at root inports have many elements that are used inside the referenced model or when virtual buses at root outports have many elements.
Workaround: Unzip and use the attached script on the top model in your hierarchy to convert all referenced model virtual bus inports and outports to accept nonvirtual buses.The script adds Signal Conversion blocks, as necessary, to retain backwards compatibility with previous releases. Call the script as follows:virtualBusUpgrade(topmodel) Make sure your models and libraries are writable before calling the script because it saves the models and libraries that it edits.

Summary: Graphics displayed in upper left of figure on Mac after changing display configuration
Description: On Mac systems with Retinadisplay, if you change the display configuration while MATLAB is running, thenthe graphics in a figure might shrink and appear only in the upper left corner ofthe figure. The display configuration changes when you connect or disconnect anexternal monitor. It also changes if you are working with multiple monitors andchange the setting to mirror the displays.?  ?
Workaround: Restart MATLAB after making any changes to the display configuration.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: If a top model and referenced model have different fixed-step sizes, duplicate values might be logged for signals in the referenced model
Description: A signal logged in the referenced model will have duplicate values for all time steps if a model hierarchy meets the following requirements: The referenced model uses a fixed-step solver. The referenced model only contains blocks with a continuous sample time. The fixed-step size of the referenced model does not match the fixed-step size of the top model. The Model block for the referenced model specifies simulation in accelerator mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Vision HDL blocks that contain a line buffer do not work when valid is toggled within a line
Description: If the input valid signal (within the pixelcontrol bus) is not held high for the duration of a line, Vision HDL blocks that contain a line buffer generate incorrect results. This problem occurs in both simulation and generated HDL code. The simulation and generated code also do not react the same way to this type of input, so there is also a mismatch in verification of the generated code. The blocks that do not function correctly are:  Analysis& Enhancement: EdgeDetector, MedianFilter Conversions: Demosaic Interpolator Geometric Transforms: Birds-Eye View? Filtering: ImageFilter, Median Filter, Bilateral Filter Morphological Operations: Closing, Dilation, Erosion, GrayscaleClosing, Grayscale Dilation, GrayscaleErosion, Grayscale Opening, Opening

Summary: Unexpected fine lines across graphics in PDF, PS, EPS and SVG files
Description: If you save a figure with MATLAB graphics to a PDF, PS, EPS, or SVG file and then open the saved file in a viewer application (such as Adobe Reader, GSView, or a web browser), unexpected fine lines can appear across filled areas. Zooming into the graphics does not eliminate the lines. These lines are a side effect of anti-aliasing algorithms used by some viewer applications when displaying the shapes that MATLAB graphics creates in the files. The lines do not appear if you print the file to a paper copy because printers do not use anti-aliasing.
Workaround: You can remove the white lines in some viewing applications by disabling anti-aliasing for graphics objects. You can typically find the setting in a display-related options menu. Instructions for common applications are given below: GSViewSelect Media > Display SettingsChange the Graphics Alpha value to 1 bitAdobe Reader XISelect Edit > PreferencesSelect the "Page Display" category in the list on the leftUncheck the "Smooth line art" option in the Rendering options panel on the right

Summary: Text strings can have gaps when printing to a vector file such as PDF or PS
Description: Printing a figure to a vector file such as PDF or PS using the Painters renderer can cause gaps to appear within text strings in the figure.  The length of the gap is proportional to the length of the string.  For example, this code creates a figure with text and saves it to a PDF file: t = text(.1, .5, 'A long string with a math symbol \pi');print -dpdf mathGap.pdf -r0  The attached comparison file, exampleGapInStrings.zip, shows both the actual figure and the saved figure. The figure in the PDF file contains a gap in the string that does not appear in the actual figure.
Workaround: Gaps in text strings occur only with vector formats when using the Painters renderer. Thus, you can work around this issue by either using the OpenGL renderer or generating non-vector formats instead.To use the OpenGL renderer, set the figure's Renderer property. For example, create a figure with two strings. Then, save the figure to a PDF file.figure('Renderer','opengl');t = text(.1, .5, 'A long string with a math symbol \pi');print -dpdf mathGap.pdf -r0To generate non-vector formats, print or export the figure using a bitmap image format such as PNG, JPEG, or BMP.print -dpng noGap.png -r0?

Summary: The polybool function may error or return an incorrect result when input polygons have overlapping edges
Description: This tends to arise when the boundary of one polygon is coincident with the boundary of the other, but some overlapping edges do not share the same end points. The problem is more acute if the overlapping edges are not exactly coincident; this condition is difficult to determine by visual inspection.
Workaround: There is a straightforward pattern for replacing calls to polybool with calls to polyshape and its Boolean operations. In the case of polygon union, for example, you can change:[x,y] = polybool('union',x1,y1,x2,y2)?to:p1 = polyshape(x1,y1,'Simplify',false);p2 = polyshape(x2,y2,'Simplify',false);?p = union(p1,p2);[x,y] = boundary(p);?Note that the polygon vertex order is likely to differ between the output from polybool and the output from the call to boundary, because there is no single right answer. (Even in a simple one-region polygon, the vertices can be permuted cyclically without affecting the underlying geometry.) In addition, if the geometries of the inputs are not perfectly clean (free from self-intersections, etc.), then the polyshape union operation may make small changes that are not necessarily performed in polybool.?

Summary: packNGo fails for a referenced model hierarchy that allows only one instance per top model
Description: Using the packNGo  function fails to package model code in a zip file for a model reference hierarchy when both these conditions apply: The Configuration Parameters > Model Referencing > Total number of instances allowed per top model  parameter for at least one referenced model is set to One . That referenced model contains a child model.  This situation produces an error message such as this: Warning: The following error occurred while attempting to run the preprocessor to find the minimum needed set of include files: While parsing the source file '/work/slprj/ert/mid/mid.c' the following error occurred /work/slprj/ert/mid/mid.h:31: cannot open source file "sub.h" | #include "sub.h" |                 ^ Reverting to full header search. > In DAStudio.warning (line 28)   In RTW.BuildInfo/findIncludeFiles>RTW.BuildInfo.findIncludeFiles (line 71)   In RTW.BuildInfo/packNGo>RTW.BuildInfo.packNGo (line 59)   In BuildTopModel (line 11)
Workaround: For versions R2014b or later, use the attached script specialPackNGo instead of packNGo. See the bugreport/help.txt file for usage information.Alternatively, use packNGo(buildInfo, {'minimalHeaders' false}). This includes the header files found on the include path in the zip file instead of including only the minimal header files required to build the code in the zip file.

Summary: pack clears variables greater than 2 GB
Description: The pack  function is unable to save variables that are bigger than 2 GB in size. As a result, these variables are cleared from the workspace without first being saved. A warning message is displayed every time a variable that is greater than 2^31 is encountered: >>? Warning: Variable 'x' cannot be saved to a MAT-file because its storage requirements exceed 2^31 bytes. This limitation will be addressed in a future release.Consider storing this variable in HDF5 file format (see HDF5WRITE). Skipping...?
Workaround: There are no workarounds for this bug.
